lines.t: Marie-Louise!  What do you think you're doing?

No!  You're needed here!

{{GM}}There is a hungry glint in Jessica’s eyes.{{/GM}}

{{GM}}She composes herself.{{/GM}}

So it would seem.

{{GM}}She composes herself.{{/GM}}

Sound advice.

{{GM}}She composes herself.{{/GM}}

So you say.
But I'm glad you're here. Truly, I am. You've helped me more than you can know. Further opened my already wide eyes.
I thought I understood my feelings toward Sam. I thought I knew why I needed to put him and my old life behind me. But Sam represented so much more.
This world is sick, you see. Humanity is sick. And perhaps better than anyone, Sam embodied that sickness. He cared nothing for others. He barely cared for himself.

Self-destruction. It was Sam’s path, and it is humanity’s path, if we don’t make a change, if we don’t embrace a new way of thinking. We must put each other first. And this is the basis of my new family.
I’m not talking about the Brotherhood. They’re sheep - servants and sustenance. Or, if they’re very lucky, hosts for my new family members. My true family.

There is a new world coming, $(l.name)!  And it’s beautiful! 

Ordered. Safe. Unambiguous. A world where everyone knows their place.

Imagine it!  A world without chaos. Without selfishness, or any other human failings. No one to betray you or hurt you or--
Soon, I won’t be. I will transcend, becoming part of something bigger than myself.

{{GM}}She smiles a wicked smile.{{/GM}}

But first, I think it's time you met my new family. They're eager to make your acquaintance.
Visionaries are seldom understood in their own time. But someday you will all join me in transcendence.
Save your help for the weak. I am beyond such frailties. Soon, I will transcend.
The last selfish act I committed was to bury my mother, and even that was done in service of cleansing myself so that I can serve my new family without distraction.
Sam and my mother were anchors, dragging me down with them into the gutter of human existence. But that part of my life ended at the burial. I am free now. Free to be uplifted by my new family.
Don’t be afraid, darling girl. No one will hurt you. You are our Queen!

How entertaining!

I’m glad you weren’t killed at the cemetery, $(l.name). It will be much more fun to watch you die.
There! Now you see them in their truest form. Now you see that they cannot die.
This is the future of Man. This is how our species will achieve everlasting life.
Open your mind, $(l.name). They are so much more than you think.

My mind was open to her when she called from beyond the veil. And she has rewarded my service and my faith with great power!

This could have been yours as well, Marie-Louise. You were to be host for our Queen. But no matter. There is another...
It's because you plod through life with your eyes sealed shut and your mind closed.
Why would I stop? I see things so clearly now.
They are the immortal soul, made flesh. They are our destiny.
I have given flesh to the immortal soul. I have unlocked Mankind's destiny.

{{GM}}Jessica appears to be in the throes of a violent trance or mild seizure. A manic state brought on by the terrible energies she is attempting to conduct.{{/GM}}

$(l.name), stop!  The Queen... approaches!  The vessel... The summoning... We... cannot... be... disturbed!

{{GM}}When Jessica speaks next, her eyes have a disturbing sharpness. Her words are echoed by Lynne in a manner that suggests a connection beyond their shared zealotry.{{/GM}}

The Hive has no thought beyond hunger, no goal beyond reproduction. We are one.

{{GM}}Jessica's body then suddenly spasms, arching her back and nearly lifting her off her feet. She lets loose a scream falling somewhere between elation and agony.{{/GM}}

The Queen... IS NEAR!

{{GM}}This seems to throw her off.{{/GM}}

What?
Vile appetites!
Because *I* willed it.
{{GM}}The hum of magical energy permeating the room seems to quiet. She's listening.{{/GM}}
The Brotherhood... They... they were a family to me.

{{GM}}She falters.{{/GM}}

I... I had to. For what he did... what he had become.
You're... trying to... confuse me.
No, I... He...

{{GM}}Jessica falls quiet. She bows her head.{{/GM}}

I... I'm sorry.

But there's no turning back. I cannot undo what's been done.

And I can't stop what's to come.

The once-beautiful Jessica Watts is a hollow shell of her former self. Her skin is pale, her lips are ashen, and her eyes are sunken in her skull. It is more than her body’s reaction to the physical damage she’s sustained or the strain of channeling magical power from another plane of existence. There is something missing from her now.

It’s gone... My link to the... the Queen. She has... abandoned me... expelled me.

Unworthy... Unworthy.

{{GM}}She shakes her head weakly, her body slumped as if it might fold itself into the ground.{{/GM}}

You... you don't understand... There are nests everywhere... in every major city. It is only a matter of time...

{{GM}}Tears run down her face.{{/GM}}

Oh God. What have I done?

And Sam...

I'm so sorry, Sam...

{{GM}}She hugs herself and begins shaking uncontrollably, little cries of anguish and despair escaping her chest.{{/GM}}

I know... I... I will to go to Hell for this... if I still have a soul at all. I betrayed humanity...

{{GM}}She bows her head and closes her eyes, forcing a torrent of tears to cascade down her cheeks.{{/GM}}

I wish that were true... but there’s no hope for me now. There’s no hope for anyone.

{{GM}}She opens her eyes and there is nothing but hopelessness within.{{/GM}}

There is a nest like this in every major city. There's no stopping them. Oh God...

{{GM}}Jessica looks at you, pleading.{{/GM}}

Please, $(l.name). Don’t let them... don’t let them eat me!
Wait!  Please!  No!
What?!  No, please!  Don't!

{{GM}}She lowers her head in shame.{{/GM}}

I understand.
At a glance, you can tell that Sam’s sister Jessica is from a different world - the opposite of Sam in almost every way. Her suit is tailored, her eyes are sharp, and her style exudes authority.

{{GM}}She eyes you up and down warily. She does a good job of hiding it, but it is clear that she is well outside of her comfort zone.{{/GM}}

Yes?  And you are?
I was told as much on the phone.
A woman who called herself “Coyote” contacted me this morning to inform me that my brother was dead, and that I should come to this place and speak to someone about an "investigation".

{{GM}}She briefly casts her eyes skyward, then gives a small shake of her head.{{/GM}}

I wish I could say it's a shock. But given the circles he ran in...
If you say. But even so, look at this place. These people. And you...
I don't know what use I'll be.
All right, but...
Thank you.
I must admit, it isn’t. None of this is.

{{GM}}She then furrows her brow, regarding you more critically.{{/GM}}

You're not with the police, are you?

{{GM}}Her frown grows more pronounced.{{/GM}}

No. It's just that... I don't want anyone else getting hurt on account of my brother.
Surely, there is an official police investigation going on. Why not let the professionals handle this?
But you must have better things to do than waste your time searching for whoever killed my lowlife brother.

{{GM}}She seems genuinely surprised.{{/GM}}

Someone's paying you?  I find it hard to believe that anyone who really knew Sam would put up the money. Who is it?
I don't understand.

{{GM}}Jessica composes herself, and in a breath, the mask is back in place.{{/GM}}

Listen to me, $(l.name). You seem like a decent person. But I’m trying to move on with my life. Our mother killed herself last year and Sam... Well, Sam was Sam. You know? I’ve worked so hard to put my family issues behind me. I don't want to see this drag on.
I just don't know that I can take it. The waiting. The worrying. Please reconsider.
Honor? How about honoring the wishes of the living instead of the dead?
So just like everything else with Sam, he finds a way to use people, whether he's alive or dead. No, I don’t have that kind of money.
Are we talking about the same man? My brother was worthless, $(l.name). Worthless. He used people. He...

{{GM}}She sighs. She’s done.{{/GM}}

Nevermind. I can see that you are not going to let this go and I respect that you are honoring my brother’s memory - in your own way. But I hope you can understand how emotional this is for me.
It's been ages. I can't even remember.
I don't know what to say. Maybe he dwelled on the past when he was in his cups. We certainly argued enough when we were younger.
I wrote many such notes, in the beginning. But I haven’t made such an effort in a long time now. I don’t know why he would have kept it.
No. To be honest, I seldom thought of him before I got the call this morning.
I will overlook the implied accusation and tell you that I was at a fundraiser all evening. A very crowded fundraiser.
Sam’s biggest enemy was Sam. I don’t know of any others. You would likely know better than me these days.
I think I’ve made my feelings about Sam pretty clear, don’t you?
Of course I want justice. I just can't let him drag me down again.

{{GM}}Jessica takes the photo from you guardedly, as if it might sting her. But that guard drops the moment she looks at it.{{/GM}}

Where... where did you find this?
I can't believe he kept this...
{{GM}}She studies the old photo for several silent moments.{{/GM}}
Things were different back then. We were different. We were a family.

{{GM}}She holds the photo forward, one part of her ready to relinquish it along with the past and another looking to hold on to both.{{/GM}}

May I keep this?
Thank you.

But I... All right.

{{GM}}She hands it back.{{/GM}}
Thank you.
I... I don't know anymore.

{{GM}}She smiles through a brief reverie.{{/GM}}

We were twins. Partners in crime. Best friends.
I suppose I came to hate him, yes. It's largely why I left.
I mourned losing Sam long before he died.
She was a devout Catholic. I don’t think it's an exaggeration to say that she sacrificed her life for Sam and me.
But she turned a blind eye to what Sam was becoming, refused to acknowledge his downward slide. I couldn’t watch her do it anymore and so I moved out.
After I left, we drifted farther and farther apart. I wasn’t here when she died. In fact, I didn’t even know about it until I returned to Seattle five months ago.
CalFree.
It was less of what brought me there than what made me leave here. I felt lost in Seattle, so I moved to California to see if I could find myself.
Actually, yes. While I was there, I developed a whole new outlook on life. A vision for what the world could become, and I came back here to help make it happen.
Just as well. I need to get back to the office.

But before I go, you should know that I'm reinterring my mother's body tomorrow, and I'm arranging to have Sam buried with her. The funeral is tomorrow at Our Lady of the Blessed Sacrament. Seven o’clock. You're more than welcome to attend.
Goodbye, $(l.name). I hope you find what you're looking for. Stay safe.
No. I don't know. Maybe.
I appreciate that, but... But I think I'd prefer to just let it be. No one else needs to get hurt.
This is ridiculous. He was my brother.
Fine. I’ll pay you a hundred just to tell me.

{{GM}}She scowls. An ugly expression on an otherwise pretty face.{{/GM}}

This is just what I'm worried about. Such an attitude could jeopardize any real shot of solving this case.

{{GM}}By the look on her face, she is clearly struggling with this.{{/GM}}

I'll be honest. I would just as soon put this all behind me, and the fastest way to do that is through proper channels.
I should have guessed.
You look like the type. Seedy.

{{GM}}She seems to instantly regret saying it.{{/GM}}

I'm sorry. Look, I just want to understand what's going on here.
That's funny. I didn't think Sam had any friends.
I suppose it would have to be.
So you're the one I was told about on the phone?
I see. And now that business extends to investigating my brother's death?
I'm sorry. It's just that... it doesn't match with the picture of Sam I've had in my head all these years.

{{GM}}She shakes free the thought and attempts a lukewarm smile.{{/GM}}

So you're the one then?  The one I was called about.
Thank you, Father. I know that my mother rests easier now that she’s finally in the parish cemetery.

{{GM}}She opens her hands to the elf standing across from her.{{/GM}}

Thank you for coming, Lynne. Your support has meant the world to me. Now, I can live again - with my new family.

{{GM}}Jessica speaks to the rest of you.{{/GM}}

Thank you all for coming. I didn’t realize Sam had so many friends. I appreciate your support and your friendship with Sam.
Thank you, Father. We'll just be a moment.

{{GM}}She eyes you suspiciously, and glances over at her elven companion.{{/GM}}

I suppose. But let's not keep Father O'Malley.

{{GM}}She’s guarded but playing polite.{{/GM}}

Very well. But let's not keep Father O'Malley.
All right, what would you like to talk about, $(l.name)?

{{GM}}Jessica's demeanor turns oddly cool and stony in response to such a simple question.{{/GM}}

I'm afraid you're mistaken.
You don’t know what you’re talking about.

{{GM}}Her jaw clenches.{{/GM}}

She wasn't a donor. She was a victim. A victim of my brother's greed. He took from her, desecrating her body, all to benefit himself. She never would have agreed to such a thing.

{{GM}}Cracks are starting to appear on Jessica's carefully crafted mask.{{/GM}}

Sam destroyed his own liver pursuing oblivion. That was his choice.
It was not a gift!  It was stolen. And I wasn't about to let him destroy what little remained of her with his vulgar appetites!

{{GM}}Jessica's eyes burn with righteous anger. Gone are any attempts to hide her guilt.{{/GM}}

Dr. Holmes made my mother whole again!  He made it possible for me to place her to rest and say goodbye.

{{GM}}She snaps.{{/GM}}

Those people were scavengers! Ghouls! They deserved to die for desecrating my mother’s body.
And poor Sam?  It was his drinking and debauchery that drove my mother to the grave, as sure as if he had murdered her himself!

My mother deserved honor and dignity. I gave her that today. She’s finally whole again with a proper Catholic burial. Those ghouls who stole her organs, including Sam, have paid the price for their sins!
My old family is dead and buried where they should be. They can’t taint my NEW family now. They can’t corrupt my children. I won’t go through that again!

{{GM}}She seethes at you.{{/GM}}

What happens now is on your head. If you had just walked away when I told you to. McKlusky would have eventually found Holmes, collected his lieutenant’s badge, and closed the case. But you had to keep DIGGING.
Now, you and your friends have to die. Let their blood be on your hands.

{{GM}}You watch her eyes harden at the mention of Dr. Holmes.{{/GM}}

I have no idea what you mean.

Never heard of him.

{{GM}}Despite her words, her eyes say something altogether different.{{/GM}}
It doesn't surprise me that Sam would have taken something for himself.

{{GM}}Emotions race across the woman's face until it settles on a snarl of hatred.{{/GM}}

No, you've got it all turned around. He USED her!  He sold her for parts!
Yes!  They all did!

{{GM}}Jessica seems to choose her words carefully, due to the circumstances or the company.{{/GM}}

No, but it doesn’t surprise me that he needed a new liver, considering his... lifestyle.

{{GM}}She flushes bright, marring her perfectly made up face.{{/GM}}

No. How could I?
And why would I care about such things?

{{GM}}She eyes you suspiciously.{{/GM}}

I can't say that I have.

{{GM}}Jessica practically growls her words at you.{{/GM}}

Certainly not my mother.
My mother was not a donor!  She was as much a victim as anyone!

{{GM}}Jessica is taken aback, or she's poising herself to bolt.{{/GM}}

She... she was nothing of the sort.

One can't be a donor when they have no say in the matter. Those butchers robbed her of her body and her dignity!

{{GM}}Her fight or flight response is now edging toward fight.{{/GM}}

{{GM}}Any pretense of innocence has now been cast aside. She snarls.{{/GM}}

This was a reckoning. Holmes delivered justice!

{{GM}}Jessica appears puzzled, and just a little bit curious.{{/GM}}

I can't say that I do.
No, it... it was handled through the city.

{{GM}}Her posture changes ever so slightly, marked by the tensing of her muscles and a tightness around her eyes.{{/GM}}

I could only hazard to guess.
That Sam had a transplant?  Or that it was the reason for his death?

{{GM}}Jessica's lip begins to curl and her eyes narrow.{{/GM}}

Given Sam's appetites, it doesn't surprise me that he needed a new liver. And given the type of people he called friend, it doesn't surprise me that he would eventually face the consequences of those appetites, one way or another.

{{GM}}The last remnants of Jessica's mask fall to pieces and she glares venomously.{{/GM}}

I am here to lay my mother to rest!
Don't speak of her in that way, like a thing.

{{GM}}Jessica's face turns scarlet.{{/GM}}

They had no right!

{{GM}}The last vestiges of Jessica's cool facade fall away and she practically spits her next words at you.{{/GM}}

He used her!
Hello, this is Jessica.
Who is it?
I don’t, but I need to know who it is before if you want to know my feelings on the matter.

I don’t know why a name meant anything to me, but it does, it makes it real, it makes him real. I’m not your client, and I can’t tell you want to do, but in contrasting your results to Lone Stars I scares me to think of him continuing to be alive and free. In other words I am saying that I understand now and trust your world’s street justice more than my world’s supposed professional justice. Best of luck, and thank you.
Yes it matters! You asked for my opinion and I need to know before I can respond to your question.
Damn it! This is critical to me, just tell me who it is so I can answer question.

characters.name: Coyote
characters.bio: Part time runner, full time badass. Product of the streets who mixes a mean cocktail.

lines.t:
{{GM}}She’s amped. Ready.{{/GM}}

Five or six? That’s not so bad. We have surprise on our side.

{{GM}}Coyote’s face is pale and her voice is low, shaky.{{/GM}}

What the hell was that back there, $(l.man)? Just what the frag was that?
And Sam’s sister is their leader. If I didn’t see it, there is no way I’d believe it. Giant unkillable bugs.
Okay. Later.
You do that.
Yeah, we will.
Thanks for helping me out back there.

Ouch! Bad joke right now.

{{GM}}She looks down at her mangled arm.{{/GM}}
Sam’s dead?
How did he die?
We’ll see. It’s gonna take years to work off this debt.
I want something badass. Got one with a laser inside?
Leave it.
I earned this face. By being stupid. I’m gonna keep it. End story.
Who said anything about me going to sleep? Just give me something to bite on...

{{GM}}Coyote looks both better and worse than you last saw her. All the gaping holes are plugged and she's sporting a shiny new cyber-arm, but now that the adrenaline has worn off, it's clear she needs some rest.{{/GM}}

Good... morning. Thanks to the miracle of modern science, combined with Doc Castle’s magical healing powers I’m almost good as new. Better, really.

{{GM}}She flashes you a puzzled look.{{/GM}}

What kind of questions?
Sam Watts? What about him?

Holy drek. Sam.

I can’t say I’m surprised. He was on a downward spiral for a long time.

What can I tell you?

{{GM}}Coyote looks genuinely sad.{{/GM}}

Look about Sam...

I can’t say I’m surprised he’s dead. Sam was on a downward spiral for a long time.

What can I tell you?
I did. He made me laugh. No one else seemed to like Sam’s jokes, but I did.
Not when he was sober. He was chill. And funny. I guess I knew him the best of everyone here. Sorry he’s gone.
You were friends?

Got it. Guess it was the same way for me, in a way. We drank together. Sam just had a way of charming you.

I guess I knew him the best of everyone here. Sorry he’s gone.

Got it. For us, it was more about me saving *his* ass once or twice. But Sam just had a way of charming you, y’know.

I guess I knew him the best of everyone here. Sorry he’s gone.

Got it. Then you know. He was a charmer, wasn’t he? 

I guess I knew him the best of everyone here. Sorry he’s gone.
It was a pretty average night. Regular crowd, as I remember. Sam was drinking with a guy named Armitage.
Yeah, you know him?

Mr. Kluwe wasn’t around - can’t remember why - so she asked Jake to do the honors. 

Jake dragged him out the back, into the alley and that’s the last time I saw Sam.

{{GM}}She thinks.{{/GM}}

Standard Sam drek. How he grew up rich and didn’t deserve this. How he hated his mother. How he loved his mother. It was pretty pathetic stuff.

On the streets, mostly. He’d occasionally convince someone to let him flop on their couch but he’d always overstay his welcome and get kicked out after a few days.

Sometimes, I’d sneak him down here so he could crash in one of the bunks. He used one the night before I saw him last.
If it was just the drinking, it would have been bad. But Sam wasn’t the monogamous type. He dabbled in everything. Booze... chips... drugs - he loved the Nitro - whatever he could get his hands on.
It wasn’t always like that but once he got sick, he started using more and more stuff to try and forget about it.

Dying. Didn’t you know? Yeah, everyone could tell. You could just look at him and see he was a walking corpse. It had to be the drinking. 

Then he disappeared for awhile and one day he came back all better. Looked good, even.
He said his mom helped him out. Never said how, though.
Now I need you two to do something for me.
I need you to talk to Mr. Delilah for me about the Royale run. He is usually upstairs.
Tell him I didn’t get the gems. Maybe I can take another run at it when I recover.
Tell him I didn’t get the gems. Maybe you can make some sort of deal.
You’re back. I think the highlights of the Wolves game will be on soon, wanna take a load off and watch with me?
Yeah? Great! What was it?
Another Ripper murder? Where?

Okay, listen. I want to help. You dragged me out of the Royale before... something bad happened. Worse than getting my arm torn off. And Sam was my friend.

You head to the docks and I’ll see if I can track down Sam’s sister, Jessica. She might be able to help us.

{{GM}}Her brow furrows.{{/GM}}

Yeah, I think he did! He mentioned her once. It didn't sound like they got along that well.

{{GM}}Her brow furrows.{{/GM}}

No. Why?
Jessica Watts... yeah! He mentioned her once. It didn't sound like they got along that well.

{{GM}}When Coyote sees you, her eyes glisten with gratitude.{{/GM}}

There $(l.he) is - the $(l.man) of the hour.
The world looks pretty different to me, now that I’ve got Gino back.
Now, I’m gonna finish my shift and get some sleep. I think I’m due for some. After that? Probably buy some ammo. I’m running low.
See you around. And don’t forget about me if you need some backup of your own.

{{GM}}She looks at you, confused.{{/GM}}

Hell yeah, I’m gonna be a runner. I *am* a runner, omae. I just play bartender to make ends meet.

{{GM}}She laughs.{{/GM}}

Don’t get ahead of yourself, omae. Paco’s pretty and he’s been there for me when I needed him - but I don’t think I’m the marrying kind.

{{GM}}She considers.{{/GM}}

I’m good. I think I’m good. I’m done with BTL squats. Done with the crusade.

{{GM}}She smiles.{{/GM}}

He’s taking care of Gino. He’s got friends who know about that kind of thing. He’s a good man, that Paco.

{{GM}}When Coyote sees you, her eyes glisten with gratitude.{{/GM}}

There $(l.he) is - the $(l.race) of the hour.

{{GM}}Coyote is wiping down the back bar. She doesn’t stop when you approach, keeps her eyes on her work.{{/GM}}

Hey.
Yep. Fine.

Listen, $(l.name). I don’t want to talk about what happened with Gino, okay? What’s done is done. Gino’s dead, I’m alive, and that’s all there is to it. 

It’s the Barrens, right? Nothing more to do or say.
I’m gonna be looking for... work. Your kind of work. So, if something comes up, keep me in mind.
Okay. I gotta do this now. The afternoon checklist isn’t gonna finish itself.
I’m fine. And a job will help me get my mind off... things. Anyway, just keep me in mind. I gotta do this now. The afternoon checklist isn’t gonna finish itself.
Yep. I know.
Afternoon checklist. Got to get the back bar ready for tonight.

{{GM}}Coyote is shaking. She looks horrified.{{/GM}}

Gino, it’s me. It’s Carla.
Gino, no.
We’re trying to help you.
We’re done here. I’m done. I don’t need to step foot in one of these places ever again.
Coyote looks to you, tears in her eyes.
Every. Mother-fragging. One.
Gino, no!

{{GM}}Coyote looks tense.{{/GM}}

Hoi, $(l.name). You in the middle of something?
I hear you.

{{GM}}Her jaw sets.{{/GM}}

I don’t know if I ever told you this, but I was born in the Royale. BTL pushers like Stevie J ran the squats. Ran my world when I was growing up.

{{GM}}She looks relieved.{{/GM}}

Wiz.

I have a cousin - Gino - who’s been missing for months. He hit the Sprawl ‘bout a year ago and immediately fell in with some tweakers. Bad guys. They hooked him on high-amp dreamchips and started using him for all sorts of drek. I tried to help him but he pushed me away. Then he disappeared. I’ve been shooting up BTL labs ever since trying to find him.
Maybe. That’s what Mrs. Kubota calls it.

{{GM}}Her face lights up. She’s running hot. Has a serious edge.{{/GM}}

I know exactly where he is this time. Got an old friend who eyeballed him herself. I’m on my way. Paco’s with me but we could use another hand. You in? I need you right now.
I knew you’d come. I’ll send you the address. See you soon.

{{GM}}Her face falls.{{/GM}}

I got nothing, $(l.man). Call it a favor?
Thanks. I’ll send you the address. See you soon.
I understand. Paco and I can handle it. I’ve done this before. Later.
I get it. You gotta focus. Paco and I will handle it. I’ve done this before. Later.
It’s not a lead!
So, here’s the thing.

{{GM}}Her brow furrows.{{/GM}}

It was... for all kinds of reasons.

{{GM}}Her eyes close.{{/GM}}

Right. Sorry.
Sorry. I wouldn’t bother you if it wasn’t important.
Backup.

{{GM}}Mrs. Kubota’s lips are tight and there appears to be a weight on her shoulders you haven’t seen before.{{/GM}}

Have you seen Coyote, omae? She is late for another shift.

{{GM}}She eyes you closely.{{/GM}}

Very well. I will cover the bar myself.
{{GM}}Mrs. Kubota declines her head. Stares at the floor. Breathes.{{/GM}}

My girl... is not coming back.

My girl.

{{GM}}She looks up. Steely.{{/GM}}

It was her crusade, wasn’t it? Her private war on the BTL trade. Do not tell me it was a senseless shadowrun for some corporation who saw her as nothing more than a disposable asset.

Wait. Do not tell me at all. It will change nothing. 

Coyote will still be dead.
{{GM}}Coyote looks to you, tears in her eyes.{{/GM}}
Every. Mother-fragging. One.
Guess that’s it, huh? Sam’s killer is bug food. He finally got his justice.
Tough week, huh?
I bet.
Yep. Sure as I’m standing here. I am going to take a flamethrower to that whole slagging business. One squat at a time, if I have to.
Nah. Like I said, that’s over. I’m gonna take care of Gino - get him back to health. But no more storming BTL labs.

{{GM}}She gets a twinkle in her eye.{{/GM}}

Someday, maybe. When the rest of my body is made of metal too. But not today.
Today, I run the shadows. Get paid. Get dirty.
See you around, $(l.name). You’re quite a $(l.guy). I can honestly say, I’ve never met anyone quite like you.
See you around, $(l.name). You’re quite a $(l.guy). I can honestly say, I’ve never met an $(l.race) quite like you.
See you around, $(l.name). You’re quite a $(l.guy). I can honestly say, I’ve never met a $(l.race) quite like you.
What will you do with the money?
You don’t seem like the type.

{{GM}}She looks back at the Universal Brotherhood.{{/GM}}

Just a little.
Guess that’s it, huh? You geeked Sam’s killer. He got his justice from beyond the grave.
Guess that’s it, huh? You captured Sam’s killer and now he’ll get his justice.

{{GM}}Your commlink chirps and Coyote appears on the screen.{{/GM}} 

How's it going?
Yep. I went down an alley and found a side door near the back. I'll send the coordinates so you can find it.
Negative. It only opens from the inside.
Null sweat. Just open the door when you're ready.
Take your time to look around and open the door when you're ready. We're chill here.
Definitely. Take your time to reconnoiter. We're chill here.

{{GM}}Coyote whispers with urgency.{{/GM}}

No matter what, I don’t want any collateral damage in there. Shoot all the BTL pushers you want but no chip-heads die, if we can help it. Got it?
$(l.name), don't attack any of the chip-heads! They don't know what they're doing, and you could hurt Gino.
My run, my rules.
I’m sorry, Mrs. Kubota. I had a run that went... bad.
Hai, Mrs. Kubota. My apologies again.
Mrs. Kubota, I can’t afford a cyberarm...
Yes, ma'am.

{{GM}}Jumping at the opportunity, Coyote follows your lead, pinning her captor's arms.
You finish what you started and soon the ganger is convulsing in an interior reality of pain and terror.{{/GM}}
Sam may have had his problems, but he was our friend. Part of the Union family.
We need to find out where Sam's sister might've gone. Maybe one of these guys can tell us... with a little encouragement.
You didn’t recognize who the elf was?
She’s Lynne Telestrian, super rich and super into the Universal Brotherhood. She's a major spokesperson for them in Seattle.

If you’re going to hit the Universal Brotherhood, I'm coming too. That psycho just admitted she had my friend Sam and a lot of innocent people killed so she could put her dead mother back together. 

That is totally slagged-up. Plus, she and her elf-buddy Lynne just tried to geek me.

They're gonna hurt for that.
Yep, she’s a major spokesperson for the Universal Brotherhood. Her name is Lynne Telestrian. She’s super rich.

{{GM}}She leans in, whispers in your ear.{{/GM}}

This guy’s a clocker - trades work time for chips. Part salesman, part lookout. Probably has a signal device for the guys inside.

{{GM}}Coyote’s eyes are hard, her pupils tight little pinpricks of hate.{{/GM}}

You do snuff too, don’t you?

{{GM}}Coyote stops you for a moment.{{/GM}}

Listen people, this is important.
No matter what, I don’t want any collateral damage in there. Shoot all the BTL pushers you want but no chip-heads die, if we can help it. Got it?
My run, my rules.
Good. We kicked the hornet's nest back there, so be ready.
Good. Let’s go and play it cool.
Thanks for meeting us, $(l.name). I owe you one.
Yeah, you never know what sort of firepower to expect in one of these BTL squats.
You never know.
You got it.

{{GM}}She brightens.{{/GM}}

Oh, hey, listen. I got good news for you - y’know Sam’s sister, Jessica? One of my contacts found her for me while you were on your way here. I called her and asked her to meet you at the Union later today. Hope that helps.

{{GM}}She looks at you, her scarred face soft.{{/GM}}

Least I can do.

{{GM}}She looks at you, her scarred face hard.{{/GM}}

Yeah, I remember. You’re welcome.

Alright, let’s get this done. My cousin, Gino, should be in one of the squats past that door up ahead. I hear there’s a whole lab setup in there. From my experience, there’s usually a lookout watching for cops outside and a guard at the door inside. Like I said, don’t know how much firepower to expect but these guys are nasty, so stay on guard. I just want to get in, grab Gino, and get out.
My run, my rules.
Good. Let's go burn down their playhouse.

{{GM}}Your commlink chirps and the face of Baron Samedi fills the screen.{{/GM}}

Together again, eh, mon ami? You and Samedi shall do great things this night! Great things!
Sarcasm should have no place between partners, eh? Let us be professional!

Point taken.

S'il vous plaît, but we must be friends! We are allied, you and Samedi!
Samedi told you! He is more than a simple decker. He is a technomancer!
It is true! It shall be proven it to you.
Indeed, mon ami! There will, of course, be complications to overcome.

These things are easily overcome, be assured. 

As you were told, there are some tasks that only you can do from inside the building just as there are some that Samedi must do from his remote location. We must coordinate our actions, that is all.

Boldness and a clear head will win the day!
Do not be so negative! Samedi has rerouted the guards in this section of the complex. The coast should be clear for you to navigate to the Accounting offices. Bonne chance!

{{GM}}Your commlink chrips and as expected, it's Baron Samedi.{{/GM}}

From what I can see there is still is more information you need, mon ami! Best check James Telestrian's system while you still have the chance!

{{GM}}Your commlink screen flickers and the Baron appears.{{/GM}}

They are moving fast. The Telestrian deckers have already regained control of the elevators to this level and several security teams have been dispatched. I suggest we find what we are looking for sooner rather than later. You must regain control over the elevators or else they will keep coming, to say nothing of getting out. Hurry!
Marie-Louise! I found you!

Call me Samedi, please! We’re in an unsecured node. 

Hang on - I'm running a decoy operation with a mirrors utility to mask you so the rest of the Brotherhood's grid doesn’t swarm you with ice.
You leave a very distinctive trail, ML. You need to work on that. Now what the hell is going on here?
I had heard some rumors on Shadowland but this is worse than I thought. I warned you about these people. You’ve got to get out of there!
Tell the leader of the runner team with you to contact Baron Samedi in the Matrix as soon as they can gain access. Tell them I will be looking for them.

{{GM}}His natural tone switches immediately to his Baron Samedi persona.{{/GM}}

Ah, the lurker in the Matrix speaks! But you would do better to ask who SHE is. Alas, there is no time! This place is dangerous - you and Marie-Louise must get safely away. Then we will talk.

{{GM}}The Baron appears on your commlink.{{/GM}}

So! It appears that James has been making moves to secure his information. Not the actions of man who has nothing to hide, eh? We need to get upstairs to office and deck into his personal system. From here it looks like the elevators are on lock down. You will need to find a access point to deck in so we can continue.
Hmmmm. Looks like some data on some new biotech genome research. This is worth some nuyen in the right hands, I'll send you the funds.
Well done mon ami! As it happens, Erik Silverstar's office is on this very floor! About time a little good fortune came our way, eh?
As you say. Now, to stop those elevators... I am sure there is an accessible node in this system we can access. Find it, before you are overrun with guards.

{{GM}}Your commlink chirps again and the Baron's face appears on screen. His voice is far less confident than before.{{/GM}}

Ah... eh... this is new.

Apparently, Mr. Silverstar's reputation for paranoia is well-founded. This panel is a hard-coded door control that cannot be accessed from the Matrix. It was not here yesterday. 

How did he have it installed so fast?
Samedi will think of something!
Very well - this will work but the plan requires you, and ONLY YOU to stay the night.

You will find the elevator, once again under our control, goes to an empty parking garage and safety. Your hired help will be able to clear the building with ease. 

You, however, will be Telestrian's newest employee come sunrise!

Now, allow me to tell you where to go...
Ah! What is this? This panel is odd, I need some time to investigate.
There must be a way to get in there! We must get that sample, but for now I suggest you eliminate the remaining guards. Unless you think stealing high security corp assets benefits from witnesses.
You must deal with those guards, I will deal with this panel!
That should be all of them, I don't see any other movement around the elevators. We should be clear.
Bonsoir, mon ami! I am pleased that we can have a semi-private conversation. You must be careful with tour guides like your friend Johnny, though. You cannot trust everyone you meet in the Matrix.
Samedi wishes to thank you for saving Marie-Louise from the Universal Brotherhood. The girl is quite... precious. If harm were to come to her, he does not know what he would do.

He also offers you his help. The insects you encountered, they are something, no? Something dark. To ignore them would be folly. There is much at stake.

Samedi knows you are after this woman, Jessica Watts, for your own reasons. He can help you get to this woman, *and* defeat her bugs.
Because our goals are aligned.
Now, now. Listen. Hear Samedi.

The job will not be simple, nor will it be straightforward, but Samedi can guarantee it will be dangerous! 

Telestrian Industries has been working for two years on a formula - codenamed AEGIS. They are working with another corporation - a foreign corporation - to refine it. This is their third attempt to get it right and they believe they have. Manufacturing began several months ago.

Unfortunately, their manufacturing plant in the Columbia Valley was recently... blown up. It could have been corporate espionage or it could have been something else. This is unknown. What is known is that a large sample of the formula survived.
We want Aegis because it kills bugs. Big ones. Like the ones you met at the Universal Brotherhood. If you want to go back there and bring the Watts woman to justice, you’ll need Aegis.

And, as it happens... Samedi knows where the last sample of Aegis is being kept. 

Hence, the job at hand! We are going to break into the Telestrian Industries primary operations office complex here in Seattle. Aegis will be somewhere within that building. We will find the Aegis sample, and liberate it for ourselves.

Would that I were but it is the only way. It is the only place to get what we need.

But take heart! You will have Samedi with you! And he is clever in the ways of the Matrix. We cannot lose.
So. Will you do this thing? Will you ally with Baron Samedi?

Telestrian! Do not talk to me of Telestrian. He is an executive. A businessman. 

Do you know what his reaction was when he learned of the insect threat? He called a meeting! He and his associates will be debating action forever.
So. Will you do this thing? Will you ally with Baron Samedi?

The Universal Brotherhood has connections - high level connections in the government and in Lone Star. And their message resonates with those who need something to believe in. 

We have no proof and we have no time. We must act.
So. Will you do this thing? Will you ally with Baron Samedi?
Because Samedi has personal connections into James Telestrian’s affairs. You see? He knows!
So. Will you do this thing? Will you ally with Baron Samedi?

What is there to know? Baron Samedi is gifted. You see? He was born to walk... he was born to travel the Matrix, bending it to his will. 

Who is Baron Samedi "really"? What is the difference? When a clown removes his paint, he is still a clown but in disguise, no? 

It is only when he is painted that people applaud.

Oui, that is true. And it is for her to say, not Samedi. He has nothing to lose, while she...

You must ask her.
And you are convinced?

Oh it is worth a great deal, mon ami. A great deal. 

You see... it kills bugs. Big ones. Like the ones you met at the Universal Brotherhood. If you want to go back there and bring the Watts woman to justice, you’ll need Aegis.
Bonsoir, mon ami! Samedi is pleased that we can have a private conversation.
So. Will you do this thing? Will you ally with Baron Samedi?
The Baron understands the need for information all too well. Ask what you will.
Ah yes. Revenge is a very motivating thing. This is something Samedi knows about. Yes, he does.

Excellent! Funds are transferring to your account now to help you assemble your team. Telestrian keeps Project Aegis information on a private local network - it cannot be accessed from the outside through the Matrix. You must jack into it from within the office complex itself.
Therefore, you must bring a decker with you. This is vital.
Samedi must suggest you bring a more experienced decker with you - in case your skills are not up to the challenge. Forgive the Baron. He means no disrespect. But this mission - it must succeed.

Samedi will be part of this evening’s events, but must remain remote. He will keep all of Seattle law enforcement and off-duty Telestrian Security away, while you locate the information we need from inside.

Ah! The Baron will handle that as well. Telestrian guards will be rerouted throughout the building. With the right timing, no one will even know you’re there.

But of course, even Baron Samedi cannot anticipate all possibilities. So tonight, you must use your contacts to hire a team, and travel to the Telestrian office complex downtown. 

You will be contacted again when you have gained entrance.
Ah yes, you have a code you follow. It is "all about the job", eh? There is nobility in this.
Very existential. Yes, nothing matters, why not die in hail of bullets, eh? You will be a fun partner tonight, mon ami!
Ah! Bon! Baron Samedi applauds you. We do this job for the *right* reason, eh?

There are giant insects nesting beneath Seattle. They cannot be killed. Do you know what insects do? Better than anything? They multiply. 

And they consume.

But... if that does not sway you, then perhaps you must be spoken to in a different language. Samedi is a technomancer - he turns thought into reality. Complete this operation and he will transfer fifty thousand nuyen into the account of your choosing. 

You are a mercenary and mercenaries get paid. Yes? Now will you do it?
You have the heart of a true mercenary. There is a purity in that, I suppose.

Excellent! So glad you have seen reason. The Baron is transferring a portion of your fee to your account now to help you assemble your team. The rest will be payable upon the successful retrieval of the sample. Telestrian keeps Project Aegis information on a private local network - it cannot be accessed from the outside through the Matrix. You must jack into it from within the office complex.
Therefore, you must bring a decker with you. This is vital.
Samedi must suggest you bring a more experienced decker with you - in case your skills are not up to the challenge. Forgive the Baron. He means no disrespect. But this mission - it must succeed.

Samedi will be part of this evening’s events, but must remain remote. He will keep all of Seattle law enforcement and off-duty Telestrian Security away, while you locate the information we need from inside.

Ah! The Baron will handle that as well. Telestrian guards will be rerouted throughout the building. With the right timing, no one will even know you’re there.

But of course, even Baron Samedi cannot anticipate all possibilities. So tonight, you must use your contacts to hire a team, and travel to the Telestrian office complex downtown. 

You will be contacted again when you have gained entrance.
Sacre bleu! Fine. Seventy-five. Done.
Very existential. Yes, nothing matters, why not die in hail of bullets, eh? You will be a fun partner tonight, mon ami!
Ah! Bon! Baron Samedi applauds you. We do this job for the *right* reason, eh?

Pardon? You are not “sold”? We speak of giant bugs, we speak of excessive payment, and still you are not SOLD? 

Very well. Consider this. You have hacked the Lone Star police database. You have left a trail of bodies throughout this city. Do not ask how Samedi knows. He knows. He is a technomancer.
You may have been justified. You may have been provoked. It does not matter. What does matter is that you are hot right now. And you can get hotter. Samedi can turn up the heat as high as it will go.

You will do this thing. You will do the *right* thing. You may do it because it’s right. You may do it because it pays. You may do it to save your worthless ass. 

But you will do it.
Apparently the coast was not-so clear.

Do not doubt Samedi. The Aegis sample will be ours before the night is through. 

There was reorganization in Telestrian recently. They have a new Vice President of Security and he is making changes to their standard procedures. It is nothing Samedi cannot handle. Now on to the Accounting offices!

{{GM}}The image that suddenly lights up your commlink is clearly a non-standard transmission. Whoever it is, they’re using the Matrix to tight-beam their avatar’s face instead of their own. You can tell that the voice has been modulated to disguise it.{{/GM}}

Bonsoir, mon ami! You must be careful with these devices - they can be hacked if you are not careful.
Forgive me... I am the Baron Samedi. Perhaps you have heard of Samedi? He is a decker of unique gifts. Some have gone so far as to call him a technomancer.
We two, we have a common knowledge now, and we share a common purpose.

The girl - she is important to Samedi. He is grateful for your timely assistance to her and he finds himself in a position to return the favor. Samedi knows the one you are after - he knows! And he can help you get to her!

But this commlink... it is not a secure connection is it? No! We need to meet face-to-face, so to speak. In cyberspace.
The one you are after poses a grave threat to many. But that is for later. We must hurry now!

You wound me! 

But let us speak more freely in the Matrix, and *then* you may decide whether to trust Samedi.
Ah... you see, the Baron - he is... unable to do such a thing in his current... condition. But enough about me.
Ah, yes. You would like to get away from this place, of course! Rest assured, what you saw is not yet ready to take to the surface. But it will be. Soon.

We two, we have a common knowledge now, and we share a common purpose. Samedi knows the one you are after - he knows! And he can help you get to her!

But this commlink... it is not a secure connection is it? No! We need to meet face-to-face, so to speak. In cyberspace.
Good morning, mon ami! You look well!

Given Mr. Silverstar's paranoia about security, we could not get into his office in a straightforward manner last night. However, thanks to the mayhem created by you and your shadowrunners, a situation ripe for exploitation now exists.

Oui! While you waited here, I continued to probe, looking into some of the less secure, more mundane Telestrian office files. Shipping records indicate that Mr. Silverstar recently took delivery of several paintings he commissioned after being promoted to VP of Telestrian Security. The shipment was called, "Our Values".

On the same day, Building Maintenance reported installing the artwork in his office at the same time as a piece of equipment purchased from Fuchi Corp. Although they attempted to obfuscate its use, I believe it is the safe.

You are very bright! Mr. Silverstar is personally conducting the investigation into last night's break-in. He is interviewing personnel who may have pertinent information. You need to be one of the personnel he interviews - in his office! Once you are inside, you will need him to leave so that you can find a way to get the sample.

Close! You will plant evidence of your own creation. There are three matrix emergency power junctions on this level. If we add some commlink chips to them, it will make last night's run appear to be preparation for a larger run. It will bring the attention of Telestrian security.

There is a janitor's uniform, ID badge and the commlink chips in the locker. They will get you general access and allow you to pass a basic inspection. The rest is up to you.

One more thing - Mr. Silverstar is well known for his creative security measures. You would do well to learn as much as you can about this man before meeting him. Now, quickly change and I will unlock the door.

{{GM}}Your commlink beings to ring, you pick up.{{/GM}}

So these paintings must be what all those expensive deliveries were. We don't have much time. I am tracking Silverstar, and he is already stepping off the elevator. I don't think your disguise will hold up much longer. I'll update you on his movements... hurry, mon ami.
He has a few guards, a tech worker and very nervous wage slave gathered in the board room and is beginning to question them.
He has finished interviewing a Matrix Tech and is now talking sternly to a pair of guards.
Now He is talking to the wage slave...This is not going well. They are raising their voices.
Silverstar and two guards are headed for the elevator you are almost out of time!

Great work mon ami! The time for stealth has passed, it has set off every alarm in the system but I have unlocked the executive elevator and your path is clear. Head to the elevator and make your escape!

Great work mon ami! The time for stealth has passed, it has set off every alarm in the system but I have unlocked the executive elevator and your path is clear. Head to the elevator and make your escape!

{{GM}}Your commlink chirps again and the Baron's face appears on-screen. His voice is far less confident than before.{{/GM}}

Ah... eh... this is new.

Apparently, Mr. Silverstar's reputation for paranoia is well-founded. This panel is a hard-coded door control that cannot be accessed from the Matrix. It was not here yesterday. 

How did he have it installed so fast?
I will think of something!

The CEO's office is the key to your goal. Go there now. 

I will consider what to do about this little setback.
We still must see if we can get into Erik Silverstar's office, the Aegis sample is there.

{{GM}}Your commlink chirps and as expected, it's Baron Samedi.{{/GM}}

You must still get into Erik Silverstar's office, the Aegis sample is there.

Félicitations! You have stolen the sample! Excellent work. 

You see? Samedi told you it could be done!

Of course. Samedi is happy to complete the wire transfer now. You are flush with nuyen, mon ami!

And now, Samedi shall - DREK!

{{GM}} Your commlink chirps once again.{{/GM}}

Well done, mon ami! The elevator is on its way. Now comes the truly entertaining part!

We shall achieve our goals, Samedi assures you. And perhaps some day, we will meet in person and drink a toast to our mutually beneficial partnership - and to the world, which we have saved together!

But first, the plan requires you, and ONLY YOU to stay the night.

You will find this elevator goes to an empty parking garage and safety. Your hired help will be able to clear the building with ease and Samedi will send Telestrian response teams in circles looking for them.

You, however, will be Telestrian's newest employee come sunrise!

Exactly that! You are clever! 

Now, here is what you must do...

{{GM}}Samedi drops the French accent - and the voice modulation. He sounds young. 18 maybe.{{/GM}}

I need you to listen to me right now. Telestrian Security is fully mobilized. Three helicopters containing elite armed response teams took off from Tir Tairngire 10 minutes ago. Time is running out. We need to make it appear like this operation is over - that you  got what you were looking for and escaped. That will cause them to stand down their forces and initiate an inquest.

No! We can't! Haven't you been listening? The Universal Brotherhood is... the bugs... 

DAMN IT!

We're never going to get a shot like this again. Their security is going to increase. Improve! They have resources and lots of them.
How else may I be of service?
Yes, Mr. Delilah in the back bar. That's usually where he does business.

Coyote grew up in the Royale but managed to escape that life. However, her cousin was apparently not so lucky.

He came to town about a year ago and fell in with the wrong circle. He was introduced to simchips and became addicted to BTLs. Coyote has been tearing her way through chiphouses for months now, searching for him and acting as a one-woman cleanup crew.
I am a former runner. Now, I provide a safe haven and a marketplace for runners who need a trustworthy place to congregate and do business.
In addition to Mr. Delilah, you may wish to speak to Van Graas. He is often stage-side. Van Graas is most often a “receiver of found articles” but he occasionally has work.

{{GM}}The former runner stands near the door, appraising her bar. She appears worried in a way you haven’t seen before.{{/GM}}

Konbanwa, $(l.name).

{{GM}}She nods slowly. Sadly.{{/GM}}

Hai. There is something very wrong, yes.
No. I will grieve for my girl later. Alone. This is bigger than the life of one shadowrunner.
I wish you could be of service.

I have learned through my contacts that the city is planning to quietly shut down the power grid in Touristville. They espouse many reasons for this “minor rerouting of resources” but I know the truth. They do not want to devote the police manpower to protect their precious citizens who come here to get a taste of the darker side of the Sprawl.

{{GM}}She purses her lips, clearly frustrated.{{/GM}}

Thank you but I believe this is beyond the power of a shadowrunner to solve. It is about the power in bureaucracy. The small people live for the convenience of the rich.

{{GM}}She purses her lips, clearly frustrated.{{/GM}}

You are correct. It is about the power in bureaucracy. The small people live for the convenience of the rich.

{{GM}}She purses her lips, clearly frustrated.{{/GM}}

I believe this is beyond the power of mere money to solve. It is about the power in bureaucracy. The small people live for the convenience of the rich. One bribe cannot change a natural law.
It is trouble, yes. For many.

{{GM}}She smiles at your use of Japanese.{{/GM}}

You are a good $(l.man), omae, and a friend of the Seamstresses Union. It is unfortunate that we do not have more friends like you.
I wish you could be of service, my friend.
Hai. There is something very wrong, yes.
Are you headed out for the night, omae?

You say, “when” but I hear “if” in your voice. I know that tone - I have used it myself on occasion. 

Very well, omae. Let us say “So long for now.” But before you go, I remind you to take advantage of all the Union has to offer.

{{GM}}She looks you in the eye. Sighs.{{/GM}}

I know, omae, I know. From what I have seen, working for your friend, Sam, has proven to be a bigger task than perhaps you thought.

Perhaps too big. It is not for me to say. 

I am not here to comment on the way you live your life. I will simply remind you to take all that you need to guarantee success. The people... downstairs are there to serve you.

Of course. When I was in your line of work, I often visited sick friends in the middle of the night. Do you have everything you need - to visit your friend? Sick friends may require extended care. Be sure you leave here... fully packed.
Johnny Clean told us where you were going, omae. We have been waiting for you to return.

We each have our reasons for wanting the killer found. Sam was a regular here and his loss has been felt, regardless of his... shortcomings. 

The whole Sprawl has been shaken by these killings as well - the randomness of them. No one knows if they will be next or what the killer might take from them.

I admit that the killings have hampered business as well. I am sorry but it is true. It does not help that Sam’s body was found down the street from here. Even my regular customers have been loathe to venture out with a killer on the loose.
Now tell us, omae. Did you find the person responsible for the Ripper murders?
This sounds more complicated than I suspected.
I sense a cause and effect in this. Coyote and Jake Armitage just left here to attend Sam’s funeral. I am told that there will be a reinterment ceremony for his mother as well.
It is clear that you must go to the funeral and talk with Jessica Watts, $(l.name).
I am sure you will.

{{GM}}Mrs. Kubota raises her hand and the conversation stops.{{/GM}}

Wait. Before you go, there is one thing you did not tell us, $(l.name). Where is the Emerald City Ripper now?

{{GM}}She nods in satisfaction.{{/GM}}

Hai. That is good.
Of course you were. Thank you for allowing us to catch up.
Of course.
Was there something else, omae?

Oh, my... this is... unspeakable. Murder was not enough for this person? Selling patients as bunraku slaves! 

Thank you for bringing this to me. I know you are still hot on the trail of your friend Sam's killer. I will contact some runners to liberate these poor souls from the buyers in this list.
You have done much good here, today. The hand of the Ripper was more of a monster than anyone could have known.
To you as well.
No.
I will. Goodbye, for now.

{{GM}}$(s.name) watches you cross the room, sizing you up as you approach. As you get closer, you can see that she’s of mixed race - African and Japanese. Her demeanor says, “This is my house, mess with it at your peril.” but her eyes twinkle with a playful light when she speaks.{{/GM}}

Konbanwa! Good evening!
I suspected as much when you walked in, omae! What business do you have with me?

{{GM}}She eyes you suspiciously.{{/GM}} 

Of course you are. Knowledge is power, neh?
So ka. And why should I help you?

{{GM}}Her face brightens - amused.{{/GM}} 

Ah! So, *you* are the "little insurance policy" he would go on about when he was drunk. His avenging angel who would strike back for him from beyond the grave.
I am glad to hear that Jake Armitage is alive... again. He lives an interesting life. Very well. If Jake sent you to me, I will help you.

{{GM}}Her face clouds when you mention the Ripper.{{/GM}} 

Keep your voice down when you mention the Ripper, omae. He isn't good for business. Besides, I'm worried about my girl, Coyote. She's missing and I'm worried she may end up as his next victim.
What do you want to know?

I knew him - we all did. Sam was a regular here, whenever he could beg or borrow enough nuyen to become altered in some way. Drugs, chips, alcohol - it didn’t matter to Sam. As long as he was bent. He was always looking for his next fix.
He clung to this place like it was his lifeline and we treated him as part of the family, even if none of us truly liked him - except Coyote.

He was here, quite inebriated, as he often was. Coyote was working bar that night and she informed me that Sam was getting rowdy and belligerent with other customers. When I requested he leave, he refused. 

My bouncer, Mr. Kluwe, was off dealing with another issue so I requested that Jake escort Sam out the back door to the alley. That was the last I saw of either of them.

During the gold rush years, there was a census, and the politicians wanted as high a number as possible to gain power and revenue. To bolster their numbers, they decided to include all the “working girls” - of which there were many - to the rolls. However, given the times, they could not list the girl’s true occupation so they entered them all as “seamstresses”.

When a girl accumulated enough money to open her own place of business, she named it the Seamstresses Union so potential workers would know that they would be treated fairly there. And thus, a rich tradition was born.
No. Perhaps when we know each other more, I will reveal more about myself. For now, enjoy the Union.

{{GM}}Her face darkens.{{/GM}}

Would that I could. I have not seen her in two days.
She is a smart woman, and quite dangerous, but I fear for her.
Please. If you are what I think you are, you know. There is always someone more dangerous.

Her room is upstairs. If you are looking for her, I invite you to examine it. You may be able to uncover her whereabouts.

I would not normally betray her privacy in this way, but... she's missed two shifts now, and cannot be reached on her comm. It is unlike her. If something has happened, I will not have inaction on my conscience.

Here is the key.
Because she is in a dangerous line of work and there is always someone smarter. More prepared.
Perhaps. All the same, I wish I could see her. She is like a daughter to me.
How can I help you?
He's a ganger. A member of the Cutters. He is a good kid in a nasty line of work. I warned Coyote against getting too attached to that type - they don’t live long.
{{GM}}Her face twists in disgust.{{/GM}} No. I am a vegetarian.
I'd be more surprised if she hadn't. Bouncers can deal with most of the troublemakers, but around here you need a gun just to take trash out to the dumpster.
I never ask about the past. None of us do. Coyote's real name is Gina. That is the most personal information I have about her.
Indeed I do. She plays her Starfire records so loud you can hear them down here. I've told Mr. Kluwe to have her turn that drek down more than once.
Not to sound like I don't care about the girl, but I never thought to ask. Just assumed she was from around here.
If you would go down there, it might bring me peace of mind. I'll call a cab for you - it should be able to get you there in time. Gambatte Kudasai. Good luck.

{{GM}}She looks you over, noting the signs of your nocturnal activities. She nods.{{/GM}}

Any run you can walk away from is a good run.
You have been told right. You must believe it.

{{GM}}She smiles a haunted smile.{{/GM}}

That has been my experience as well. I am glad to have left the game.
It is good to have you back.

I can tell you have been busy and I can see by the look on your face that there is something you need. 

Is this about the Ripper?

That is excellent! I will unlock the piano so you may go downstairs. 

I am certain someone in the safe house will be able to help you, if they are awake. 

It is likely that you will visit the Matrix before the day is done.
Gambatte Kudasai. Good luck.
And you.
Take the whole plate.

I believe so, yes. I will unlock the piano so you may go downstairs. 

I hope you get the answers you are looking for.

It is likely that you will visit the Matrix before the day is done.

I understand. I will unlock the piano so you may go downstairs. 

Someone down there should be able to help you, if they are awake. 

It is likely that you will visit the Matrix before the day is done.
Woman, how dare you miss two shifts and then come back and bleed on my floor?
So ka! I can see that. Your arm is a mess.

Was this your crusade again? Do not answer. It will only upset me further. 

You caused me to worry about you, Coyote, and that distracted me from my business.
Cherry, take her downstairs to Dr. Castle.
And tell Castle to put something new and shiny where that arm used to be.

I am aware of your financial situation. When you are healed, we will discuss the concept of “giri” - the debt of honor. 

Now go. Bleed elsewhere.
Her anger at Coyote's rashness slowly washes from her eyes and is replaced by tears. She sniffs, wipes them away, and inclines her head to you.

Domo arigato, $(l.Name). That girl is precious to me. 

It is not often that we see acts like these in the Barrens. You have performed a great service for my little family and I welcome you into my home. Consider it yours while your work keeps you here. 

But we both know that words are mere air. Beyond my thanks, I offer you this renumeration. Please take it as a show of respect.
It is. You will find that there is more to the Union then meets the eye.
You will find more than a bunk, $(l.name). There is more to the Union then meets the eye.
You are most welcome. And I offer more than simple lodging. You will find that there is more to the Union then meets the eye.

Below us is a small facility available exclusively for discriminating independent operatives like yourself. 

In it, you will find vendors selling the best gear the black market has to offer, a fully-equipped cyberdoc, and a secure place to rest when “the drek hits the fan” - as they say.

Indeed. Normally, I require a percentage of the runner’s income for the use of this facility but as I said, you are family now. Consider it on the house. 

To gain entrance, play G-A-F-F-C on the piano.

Hovering over the recently-departed is a small dwarf, whistling a tune. His broad grin says "I love my job" a little more than you'd want or expect from someone in the chop shop trade. As you approach, he looks up with a lopsided grin. There’s something kindly in his eyes... though it might just be a stray reflection of chrome and surgical tools.
Sorry, didn't expect any visitors at this hour. And some asshole at corporate took my receptionist. What can I do for you, $(l.Sir)?

I’m John Dresden, the Organ Grinders Branch Manager here. So yeah, that makes me this franchise area’s coroner, too. 

And you are?
{{GM}}His grin fades.{{/GM}} And what makes you think I know anything about that?

{{GM}}He frowns.{{/GM}} Interesting. A dead man makes for a strange client. 

But what makes you think he's here?

{{GM}}He fixes you with a hard gaze.{{/GM}} Careful, friend. I can have Lone Star here in 2 minutes. 

Now, what makes you think I'd know anything about Sam Watts?
I see. Well, you're right, he's here. Not too many people know about the murder yet, though... the press haven't caught wind of it yet, what with it being all the way out here in the Barrens.
So who told you he was dead?

{{GM}}The dwarf raises his eyebrows, a smile wiping the suspicion from his face.{{/GM}} A dead man's switch, eh? Fascinating!

I was working on him earlier. He’s over here.
He’s my second Emerald City Ripper victim. The third one was downtown.

{{GM}}He sighs.{{/GM}} Not my title. That’s what the Seattle press insists on calling the killer. All I know is that like the original Jack, our Ripper knows how to handle a scalpel. But this one’s even more twisted... he or she always removes an internal organ from the victim.
Watts’ liver was cleanly cut out.
The first victim’s heart was missing, and the third had the spleen removed.
{{GM}}Dresden looks amused.{{/GM}} Do you always make friends that easily?
{{GM}}He cocks his head to one side.{{/GM}} Be straight with me. You really gonna work for the dead man?
What can I do to help you?

I don’t know, but someone with some major pull has been looking out for McKlusky’s career. 

And wallet.
The official report is that she committed suicide about a year ago.

My name’s on the report, but my actual findings left some... doubts. I can’t say that it wasn’t suicide, but there were unusual bruises on her upper arms, and she didn’t use her dominant hand to pull the trigger.

I was told to drop it, so I dropped it.

A whole healthy body can be worth a bunch of nuyen, but individual organs? Not worth as much anymore, what with all the synth and cyber stuff on the market these days.

Organ Grinders only deals in the recently deceased. There's plenty of other chop shops that aren’t as picky, though - and they don't care where the bodies come from, either.

Doctors still learn how to use them in their first year of medical school - as do coroners - but neither profession uses them much any more. It's possible some of the slimier chop shops still use scalpels, I suppose, but I wouldn't know where to look.

It’s a nearby club in the Barrens that attracts lowlifes. You'd probably like it. 

Me, I'm not really the going-out type. Always too much to be done around here. Plus, the dead are just easier to get along with.

{{GM}}Dresden looks up at you intently for a moment before speaking.{{/GM}} You know, I might be able to do you one better. 

Why don’t you poke around those body lockers in the back and see if you find anything... useful.
Dresden says nothing. A sly smile plays across his face as he turns back to his work, whistling a tune.
{{GM}}The coroner frowns.{{/GM}} So you insisted at the time.

I’ve already posted everything I know. 

The killer stuns the target with a combination of drugs and magic, then removes a single internal organ while they're still alive.

The perpetrator is most likely right-handed, with a slim hand that knows its way around a scalpel. Has a decent understanding of human and metahuman anatomy, too.

Not necessarily. 

I don’t know any surgeons who still use scalpels, anymore. These days it's all done with computer-controlled lasers. Could be anyone from a military field surgeon to an antique medicine aficionado.
Your commlink chips and the screen shows John Dresden, the coroner from Organ Grinders. He doesn’t look happy.

Never mind that, I’m fine. Listen, I’m at the NTSB investigation facility down on the docks with the corpse of what looks like another Ripper victim. If you want to check this out, I would get here fast - before McKlusky does.
There’s been another Ripper murder, this one at the NTSB investigation facility down on the docks.
Hey, $(l.name). I gotta talk fast. Looks like the Ripper got another one. The victim worked at the NTSB investigation facility down on the docks.
Yes, but you’d better get here quick - before McKlusky arrives.
Yes, but you’d better get here quick - before McKlusky arrives.

{{GM}}The little coroner is surprised to see you but he can’t hide his delight.{{/GM}}

Hey, $(l.name). I was wondering if I was going to see you again! I guess I shouldn’t be surprised to find you here.
I guess so!

{{GM}}He pops his eyebrows, eager.{{/GM}}

You know it’s all over the trivid, right? They found the Emerald City Ripper dead at some asylum out in Snohomish, of all places! Turns out he’d been masquerading as the Head Administrator or something. They found him dead from some sort of vigilante attack.

{{GM}}He leans in, whispers conspiratorially.{{/GM}}

You wouldn’t know anything about that, would you?
I know! It’s fascinating, isn’t it?
So! The big question! What about the dead man’s switch? Now that the ripper’s dead, are you going to collect?
Excellent! I’m sure you deserve it. How do you collect?

{{GM}}He brightens.{{/GM}}

Lone Star’s Emergency Response Team set up a secure phone line right here to coordinate their efforts. I can authorize you to use it!
Don’t mention it! Take care, $(l.name)!
Great. I’m sure there are other people you’ll want to talk to first. Take care, $(l.name)!
I guess so! Take care, $(l.name)!
Forgot? Come on! How do you collect?
Then do it for me! I want to see how this comes out. How do you collect?
I’m sure! Yes, indeed - hard to say!

{{GM}}Dresden winks.{{/GM}}

That’s what I thought.
Thanks, but I prefer natural causes.
Busy!
So? What did he say?

$(l.name)! Still working for the dead man? {{GM}}He chuckles.{{/GM}}

As you can see... we've got another Ripper victim on our hands.

Ha! Yes... yes, indeed.

The victim's male, mixed Native American descent, age 38. Based on the condition of the body, it looks like he was killed around two in the morning.

Cause of death... unknown. Several internal organs were removed from his body after death, quite gruesomely. I'm not sure if the killer even knew what they wanted before they started cutting.

Not much else to tell yet, really. He does have lot of pre-existing scar tissue, so he probably had a major surgery at some point in recent history. Unfortunately, with the organs gone, I can't determine the nature of the surgery.
Well, the killer definitely took the lungs. The more I look at it though, the other missing organs may just be eviscerated within the chest cavity.

McKlusky couldn't find his ass with both hands.

Don't tell him I said that, though.

Aguirre would love to solve it before McKlusky and get his gold detective's shield. Plus, he'd love to see McKlusky suck it in front of his superiors. He's already tried to bribe me to keep information from McKlusky and slip it to him.

I'm inspired by their tireless pursuit of justice.

{{GM}}He grins from ear to ear.{{/GM}}

Do you have any idea how much this job pays these days, $(l.sir)? I don't think Aguirre's officer's salary is going to do much to seduce me into helping him.

Sorry, I don't do theories. I'm just a scientist who happens to prefer dead people. However, while there was massive damage in the chest cavity, I can see that the victims lungs were transplants. Which is interesting, since another victim's missing heart was also a transplant.
Granted, modern medicine has made organ transplants relatively easy but it is an odd coincidence. But, as a scientist, it's just that. A coincidence.
This is only the third body I've personally examined. One of our other branch managers handled the other two. If I find that more of the victims had organ transplants, I'll let you know.
No clue. She just showed up. Next of kin, I think.
Good luck out there.
Good luck out there.
Well, the killer definitely took the lungs. The more I look at it though... the other missing organs may just be eviscerated within the chest cavity.

Well, it seems that the victim was quite dead before the organs were removed. So at least he had that going for him, don't you think?

The killer definitely took the lungs. The more I look at it though, the other missing organs may just be eviscerated within the chest cavity.
The victim's male, mixed Native American descent, age 38. Based on the condition of the body, it looks like he was killed around two in the morning.

The victim's male, mixed Native American descent, age 38. Based on the condition of the body, it looks like he was killed around two in the morning.

And yes, I'm a little tired. Bodies have been piling up like cordwood, it seems.

$+(l.name)! Surprised to see you here. Let me guess... Officer Aguirre tipped you off? Suppose you must'a made an impression on him at the Market yesterday. Or your credstick did, at any rate. {{GM}}He chuckles.{{/GM}}

Anyway, surprise... we've got another Ripper victim on our hands.
Yes?
It's alright, officer. $+(l.he)'s with me. {{GM}}Dresden steps up to the barrier with a warm grin.{{/GM}}
So, what brings you out here? Hot on the trail of the dead man's killer?
Yeah, that's what it looks like. As you can see, the Ripper went for the eyes this time. Pretty clean work. I gotta hand it to him, our Ripper knows what he's doing. Or she, I suppose.

Well, not much. {{GM}}Dresden scratches his head absentmindedly, probably breaking some sort of sanitation protocol.{{/GM}}

She's been dead for about three hours. Her name was Lucy Warden, worked at the Stuffer Shack just around the corner. Looks like she was just leaving work when it happened.

That's the strange thing. There don't seem to be any signs of a struggle. Not a single bruise on her body. Yet, she was clearly alive when the eyes were taken... died of blood-loss shortly thereafter. As to what knocked her out, I won’t know until I can run some tests back at the lab.

Yeah, well, I don't really mind the change of scenery. {{GM}}Dresden laughs.{{/GM}}

The coroner for the downtown branch is out on maternity, so I told management that I'd cover for her on this one. Plus, I want this sicko caught.
Ah! Completely unrelated. It appears some wild dogs dragged the body out here from the alley, some time after her death.
Now there's an interesting thought. No, nothing obvious though. I'm sure when McKlusky shows up he'll call in a full magical forensics team though, just to be sure.
Trophies of some sort, I suppose? Probably of some symbolic significance to the killer. Beyond that, I couldn't speculate.
Huh? Where? {{GM}}Dresden scans the spectators surrounding the crime scene.{{/GM}}

Interesting. Well... there's those who might be interested in purchasing some of her parts, sure, but that's pretty poor form to inquire at the site of a murder. An ugly elf, eh? I'll keep an eye out. Shouldn't be hard to spot if he comes back around.
Of course. What would you like to know?
{{GM}}He chuckles.{{/GM}} Yeah, that sounds about right. Any opportunity to get in McKlusky's way, he'll take it. A bit sleazy, sure, but I'd take Aguirre over McKlusky any day of the week.

Hey, I figure if I help you out, there's a better chance to get this scumbag off the streets a little sooner. McKlusky wants the Ripper in a cell, sure, but he couldn't care less if it takes another dozen murders. Good luck out there eh? {{GM}}Dresden starts to turn back to the body, then stops.{{/GM}}

Speaking of McKlusky... you should probably get going soon, before he shows up.
Anytime.
{{GM}}Dresden looks up, and a grin spreads across his face.{{/GM}} Need something else? Any fascinating new leads?
Yeah, $(l.he)’s on the level. Working for Sam, believe it or not... some sort of dead man’s switch. I thought you could help $(l.him) out. *Maybe* even stop moping around the shop all day...

Get your own damn cup! My hands are dirty anyway. 

Now what’s wrong with this intestine.... {{GM}}You hear a loud *squelching* sound as Dresden continues his work.{{/GM}}
Yeah, Jake here’s a regular. Should’ve started a rewards card, Jake - a few more visits and you’d get a free night’s stay.
{{GM}}He yells over his shoulder.{{/GM}} Very funny Jake. You can sleep in the dumpster tomorrow.

characters.name: Johnny Clean
characters.bio: Whether working as a janitor or a decker, he gets out clean.

lines.t: Thanks to the semiconductor-based gene sequencing system that David employed, we should have the information we need to track the Ripper in the Matrix.

I'm really impressed with his results, considering it was his first attempt, he was utilizing an unproven technique he'd read about in a science journal, and he was working from memory on minimal sleep.

We hit the Lone Star DNA database first to see if our donor has a prior criminal record. They have extensive DNA archives. Then we go hunting based on what we find.

Let's hit the decks and jack into the Matrix. I can get into their private grid easily, but I've got to warn you - once we're in, it could get a little rough. How much decking experience have you had?
Don't be like that. It's arctic-cool 2054 tech!

Yes! Maybe we can sit down with David and he can share his research. 

But let's stay focused.

Here's what I'm thinking. We hit the Lone Star DNA database first to see if our donor has a prior criminal record. They have extensive DNA archives. Then we go hunting based on what we find.

Let's hit the decks and jack into the Matrix. I can get into their private grid easily, but I've got to warn you - once we're in, it could get a little rough. How much decking experience have you had?
I have a trode net here. It’s a headband you wear that lets you piggyback me in the Matrix. You’ll see and hear everything I do.
Alright, follow my lead then.
Great. I'll come with, just in case any IC decides to crash our party.
So here's what we know. Our DNA evidence belongs to a dead man, whose death was never explained. He worked with chop shops... which fits with the LIVING guy that you've met.
The NewsNet says he left his estate to his psychiatrist. Maybe finding out who his psychiatrist was will give us our next clue.
My gut says, we can deck in to the medical board's records and reverse trace to find this doctor. You in?

{{GM}}Johnny is hunched over his desk, idly fiddling with a cyberdeck. He seems preoccupied. He looks up when you approach, eyes narrowed.{{/GM}}

Been making new friends out there?

{{GM}}He frowns.{{/GM}}

Apparently so. You seem to have made a new friend in the Matrix, at least. Calls himself Baron Samedi. He’s been asking about you on Shadowlands. He has a pretty distinctive icon - skull face with a top hat.
I can’t tell what he wants... but the guy seems pretty serious. I wouldn’t ignore this, if I were you. Deckers have a way of making people’s lives miserable if they’re so inclined.

{{GM}}Johnny’s janitor persona is gone, replaced by something colder, more dangerous.{{/GM}}

I did some checking but didn’t get much because he’s new on the scene. He’s no Wilson, though. Got a rep as a drek-hot wire-boy from what I hear. Never rides along with physical-entry teams though. Purely remote work.
