Baron Samedi is the name of a Loa, or Haitian voodoo spirit. He sits at the crossroads of the living and the dead.

I’ve been around a long time and I’ve seen a lot of avatars running around the ‘trix. A lot of ‘em. 

You ask me, it’s just some guy trying to look scary to make a name for himself. Think of it as “personal branding”.
Yeah, that’s the one. He sits at the crossroads of the living and the dead.
He’s been spamming Shadowlands for the last hour, I’d say. He’s new on the scene but everyone’s talking about him - even the hottest deckers like Dodger are saying he’s a force to be reckoned with.
Something I should know?

That’s where the shadow community goes to talk about anything... shadowy - who’s planning what projects, who’s hiring, which corp is screwing which - anything that affects shadowrunners and how they make money. 

It’s where I’d go to see if I could pick up any dirt about you.
Is that a joke?
Good. You had me worried there for a minute!
What do you mean, “indestructible”?
Whatever it is you’ve gotten into, $(l.name)... you’re in it deep. From what I hear, the Brotherhood has members in high places - government, corps, law enforcement... you be careful, chummer.
Okay, you can use my secure jackpoint. I’ve already set up a portal to Shadowlands. Feel free to jack in whenever you’re ready.
Okay, you let me know when you’re ready and I’ll let you hitch a ride into the Matrix again. I’ve already set up a portal to Shadowlands.
We call it trode net. As in electrode. It’s a headband you wear that lets you piggyback me in the Matrix. You’ll see and hear everything I do.

{{GM}}He frowns.{{/GM}} 
Look... I don’t trust this guy either, but something tells me you’d better see what he wants. If he’s as novahot as everyone’s saying, he’ll find you one way or another. I’m afraid you may not have much of a choice in the matter.
Anyway, if you decide to talk to him, you can use my secure jackpoint. I’ve already set up a portal to Shadowlands. Feel free to jack in whenever you’re ready.
Anyway, let me know if you want to talk to him, and you can hitch a ride into the Matrix with me again. I’ve already set up a portal to Shadowlands.
You're back.

{{GM}}Johnny whispers to you.{{/GM}}

He’s not bluffing. This guy has the right connections.

{{GM}}Johnny whistles a long, slow whistle.{{/GM}}

Ho-ly crap. Telestrian Industries. You’ve hit the big time, chum. You’re going to need a serious decker for this one. Fortunately for you, I know one of the best.

{{GM}}He sighs.{{/GM}}

No... I’m not an inside-man any more. My running days are over.
I’m retired, not dead. Of course I know where you can find one.
There’s an elf who owes me a favor. He goes by “Dodger” - you may have heard of him. He’s one of the best deckers in Seattle - and this is *me* saying it.
I’ll get you his contact information. You’ll know him by the way he talks. Like he’s in a damn Charles Dickens novel.

{{GM}}Johnny whistles a long, slow whistle.{{/GM}}

Ho-ly crap. Telestrian Industries. You’ve hit the big time, chum. If you need a second decker, you might as well take one of the best.

{{GM}}Johnny Clean is talking with Cherry Bomb and Mrs. Kubota when you walk up.{{/GM}}

We were just talking about you, $(l.name).

{{GM}}Johnny frowns and nods.{{/GM}}

You’re right. I should have kept my mouth shut. I should know better. It’s just that we have a personal stake in the Ripper murders.
I don’t understand. Are you saying the killer wasn’t responsible for his own actions?
Think his sister Jessica had something to do with it?
I agree. The ceremony feels like the culmination of Sam’s mother getting all her body parts back. Think his sister Jessica had something to do with it?
Someone’s pulling the strings of a serial killer?

{{GM}}The man is dressed like a janitor but is wearing unusually clean overalls. He's tall, rail-thin and has a cunning look in his eye that says he's more than just a maintenance man.{{/GM}}

Howdy. Name's Johnny Clean. You new?
Well enjoy yourself. This looks like your type of place. I'm just sweeping up a bit.
Null sweat, chummer. You have a good day now. {{GM}}He mumbles something under his breath.{{/GM}}

True. Quite true. And I keep my mouth shut about it, too. That's the secret to keeping a job here... and staying alive in general. 

Gotta work. See ya 'round.
You look like you've seen some action, too.
Fry can help you out.
Really? Fry can help you out then.

But that's where I come in. 

David, if you can get me that code sequence I’m pretty sure we can track it’s owner down via a Matrix run.
$(l.name), when David gives me the sequence, I'll jack in and help you trace the blood sample back to its source.
I just got a second wind! Meet you at my rig, $(l.name).
We found an exact match for the DNA, but it was linked to a dead chop shop assistant by the name of Silas Forsberg. Now, I have my theories, but what do you think is going on here, $(l.name)?
A bit ... out there, as far as theories go. I guess we can't rule it out just yet. Did you see anything in the autopsy report that could fill in some more detail?
That's a distinct possibility. What was it that we saw in the autopsy report?

Good catch. That matches up with something I've seen runners try... It's easy enough to find a body shop that'll make a replica set of teeth for you. Find some schlub off the street no one will miss, swap the teeth and throw the heat off your trail for a while. Lucky I've never been that desperate.

Could be. But we're not going to find proof of that laying around. It is a little strange that he would do this to slip some pursuer, but left his belongings to his psychiatrist. Wouldn't he want to keep them? Was the psychiatrist in on it?
Why would his DNA wind up at the crime scene, though? Was he posing as a doctor at a previous murder site?
Silas became Dr. Holmes? Huh. If he replaced the real Holmes, that's probably whose body they found in Silas' apartment in the first place.

We have no evidence of that. Personally, I think it sounds like he wanted to become a surgeon. But he got caught in the wrong line of work - during his tenure at the chop shop, he was brought in for unlicensed plastic surgery. Won't get a medical license after that one.
You have a different idea?
That's a new one by me. Unless you were kidding.
You have a different idea?
We didn't see any evidence of that. And he left his estate to his psychiatrist, right?
True. Wouldn't look too good on someone's record if they wanted to become a doctor. I think this is beginning to come together... The late Silas left all his belongings to a doctor.

Dr. Holmes. Employment records have him as the administrator at Mercy Mental, and the picture on his file matches the person you saw downtown. The same person this DNA belongs to. All the evidence points to him being your killer, whoever he really is.

This guy clearly did not want to be found. If I was you, I'd hire a team before heading over there. 

I sideloaded a ton of valuable data from those Lonestar matrix nodes. Here's your cut.
I think my part here is done. You might want to go sticking your hand into a hornet's nest, but I'm a little more wary of the jobs I take these days.

Please pay him my respects, I always find that high caliber rounds get the message across. You should clearly hire some friends and go loaded for bear.

I side loaded a ton of valuable data from those Lonestar matrix nodes. Here's your cut.
Coroner really phoned it in on that one. Happens way too often when there's no next of kin willing to foot the bills for a thorough autopsy.
I definitely don't think that report told the whole story.

They matched the identity by the dental records, right? Not the most reliable. It matches up with something I've seen runners try... It's easy enough to find a body shop that'll make a replica set of teeth for you. Find some schlub off the street no one will miss, swap the teeth and throw the heat off your trail for a while. Lucky I've never been that desperate.
The coroner seemed to think so. But their report wasn't very thorough. They usually aren't when a family member isn't around to pay for the work.

I guess that's still a possibility. You seemed pretty sure when we got here that it did. 

What about Silas' autopsy report, though? Anything stand out?
{{GM}}Johnny leans on his seemingly brand new mop and surveying the crowd at the Union.{{/GM}}
Don’t know what you’re talking about.
The piano is a little out of tune, check it out.

I fail to see the connection between a murder and a raid on one of my office buildings. There is no connection between the research project and a murder that I am aware of.
Yes, I am quite the dullard and cannot understand challenging concepts such as why a criminal would break into a business office.

{{GM}}Telestrian cuts in quickly.{{/GM}}

Thank you, Marie-Louise. You have confirmed the identity of your rescuer and given me reason to forgive $(l.him) for $(l.hisher) trespasses against me.
You can relax, Marie-Louise, you are safe. It is over.
I don't know if that is sarcasm or not. Just... behave.

{{GM}}His jaw sets.{{/GM}}

No one is suggesting you join them - nor that are you worthy of doing so. You are attending a briefing. Now come with me and watch your tongue.
Not if you wish to draw another breath, no.

Lady and gentlemen this is... $(l.name). $+(l.he) is the $(l.race) who saved my daughter and the only one who has faced our common enemy in combat. 

Herr Brackhaus, what does the representative of the Great Dragon, Lofwyr, have to tell us about the magical insect this... shadowrunner uncovered?

The best candidates are the disaffected and the disenfranchised. In short, the weak-willed. Their minds are the most susceptible to suggestion, which is helpful in making the transformation. As you may imagine, these are the sort of people easily attracted to a cult - such as the Universal Brotherhood.

This is not an infestation, $(l.name). 

It is an invasion.
My Director of R&D, Diane Ravenwood, will explain how Project Aegis will be used in the field. Dr. Ravenwood?

Crudely put but accurate.

We must stop the Universal Brotherhood from summoning a Queen and we must stop them immediately. You are the the only one who has been inside their facility and the only one who has personally fought these creatures before. That, along with your highly-effective assault upon my property indicates that you are the ideal person to lead the attack.
Enough. Are you willing to undergo this mission, $(l.name)?
Excellent.
Of course. Here is my proposal. If you prevent the Queen from being summoned and destroy the insects in the next, one million nuyen will be divided by the surviving team members.
Excellent.

Do not overplay your hand, $(l.sir). You could still be arrested for the attack on my office and, if my sources are correct, the justifiable but illegal death of Dr. Holmes. Alternately, I could simply eliminate you and find one of the mercenaries you hired to play your part.
Excellent.
Done.
I believe our quests are aligned. I need proof that Aegis will kill the monsters and you need to kill the monsters to get to your target and complete your objective.
Excellent.

{{GM}}He sighs.{{/GM}}

Yes, speak with Harlequin when you are ready to depart. If you wish to acquire additional supplies for your mission, find my assistant, Quoth. He is highly... resourceful.
Is there something I can clarify for you?

Telestrian Industries Corporation has been working on Project Aegis for two years without fully understanding its use. Lowfyr did not trust me with the information. My engineers finally met The Dragon’s specifications 3 months ago and had just begun the production process when my cousin Lynne hired shadowrunners to destroy the lab and the factory - leaving us only the sample you stole.

{{GM}}He shakes his head.{{/GM}} 

I’m not sure. The host for the Queen is chosen very carefully as the interactions between the Queen and lead shaman are critical - a family connection between the two roles is ideal. Obviously, there is a blood connection between my cousin Lynne and my daughter but I do not believe Lynne is the shaman. That would be Jessica Watts.

My people have weaponized the Project Aegis formula by creating shells which, when fired, propel a high-velocity cloud of the material which should be effective at killing exposed insect spirits. 

There are more effective ways to deliver Aegis - obviously - but time was of the essence and I needed to improvise.
Indeed.
And?

{{GM}}James assesses you with a newfound respect.{{/GM}}

Very clean. I believe our test of Project Aegis was a success, $(l.name). Herr Brackhaus is having it weaponized now for distribution to Knight Errant forces. The authorities are going to have a great many questions about this incident and I will need to provide details. The Universal Brotherhood has a great many chapter-houses and they must be dealt with immediately.
That is for others to determine. People other than you. Still, you have turned in a yeoman’s effort, and - regardless of your obvious flaws - you are deserving of a reward.
Yes, you were of service. There can be no debate on that topic. A yeoman’s effort, to be sure, and - regardless of your obvious flaws - you are deserving of a reward.
Yes, we should discuss you. Regardless of your obvious flaws, you have a done a great service to the world and are deserving of a reward.
Ah yes. Do not be concerned. I will keep my end of the bargain.
Mr. Quoth!
See that our hero gets $(l.his) reward. After deductions, of course.
Yes, of course. You still owe me for your raid on my offices.

Very good. That transaction is complete. 

Now then, you have turned in a yeoman’s effort, and - regardless of your obvious flaws - you are deserving of a reward.
Ah yes. Do not be concerned. I will keep my end of the bargain.
Mr. Quoth!
See that our hero gets $(l.his) reward. After deductions, of course.
Yes, of course. You still owe me for your raid on my offices.
What would you ask of me?

{{GM}}Telestrian’s ice breaks - for a moment.{{/GM}}

I do indeed - and I believe you have already undergone the required interview. Very well, $(l.name), report to work as Vice President of Telestrian Industries Corporation Security on Monday.
Just wear something more appropriate. Good day.

{{GM}}Telestrian regards you a moment - he seems to be choosing his words.{{/GM}}

Unfortunately, a $(l.race) is not an appropriate fit for a Telestrian Industries Corporation executive. I’m sure you understand. However, I believe whomever fills the position would benefit from an Executive Consultant of your caliber. The position will pay quite handsomely, I assure you. Report to work on Monday.
Just wear something more appropriate. Good day.

{{GM}}Telestrian’s ice breaks - for a moment.{{/GM}}

You short-circuit an invasion of extra-dimensional insects and for your reward, you wish to have a insignificant police detective dismissed from his position?

{{GM}}He smiles an icy smile.{{/GM}}

Nor do I. It repulses me as does the rest of him.

Very well, Detective McKlusky is now Citizen McKlusky - the man grates on my nerves anyway. And since this action requires little more than a nod to the proper peon, you may ask *another* boon of me. 

I am feeling generous today.

{{GM}}He smiles an icy smile.{{/GM}}

I cannot disagree. He displays a rare combination of brute and sycophant that I cannot abide.

{{GM}}He smiles an icy smile.{{/GM}}

Indeed. I won’t waste brain cells even contemplating your reason for requesting it.

{{GM}}He smiles an icy smile.{{/GM}}

I would expect no less from you. Mr. Quoth, please place a suitable bonus on our friend’s credstick. A bonus of such size that additional negotiations are unnecessary. I have no interest in furthering our relationship.
I am sure you appreciate a good slum as much as the next shadowrunner. However, I fail to see the significance of the Redmond Barrens or its energy resources in the context of this conversation.

{{GM}}Posted at the doorway to the VIP section is a tower of troll muscle wrapped in an impossibly tailored suit. Whether the product of good genes or expensive after-market cosmetic work, the troll’s gleaming horns perfectly frame his face, and his polished tusks and goatee accentuate the set of a lantern jaw.{{/GM}}

Welcome. Please behave yourself.
Nothing a stern look can’t usually solve.
You have business here?
Heard about that. Real shame what happened to him.
There is a sharpness in Kluwe’s eyes, the look of a man who has seen much and earned wisdom at a young age.
Encouraged to call it a night, I’d say.
Sam was a drunk, but he usually wasn’t a violent one.

He was a bit agitated. Didn’t catch the specifics. Might have been over a woman. 

Thought I was going to have to show him out, but I had to deal with a couple of rival go-gangers posturing for one of the working girls upstairs. Jake helped Sam out instead.
No problem.
Always do.
Happy to help.
Not so much toss as nudge on his way.
Yes, albeit gently.
Any business conducted on these premises is my business.
Figured as much.

You’re not a regular, and you don’t have the look of some wage slave thrill-seeker braving Touristville. You’re new in town, and showing up on the heels of Sam’s death. Stands to reason you’re here about him.
Don’t think that’ll be necessary.
Sure, everyone here knew Sam. Shame to lose a part of the family.
Must I?  Only if you insist.
Load bearing, too.

{{GM}}The hulking troll bouncer in the immaculate suit stands as impassively as ever. The absence of dust on his broad shoulders is the only real indication that the man ever moves. He nods to you when you approach.{{/GM}}

Evening. I see Coyote is back, looking only a little worse for wear. We have you to thank for that?
You have the gratitude of everyone here, especially mine.
Couldn’t stomach losing anyone else so soon after what happened to Sam.
Immensely. Toward anyone who walks through that door, be they employee or patron. Unless they mean ill will, of course.
Haven’t minded so far.
People are saying it was the Ripper. But people say a lot of things about what they don’t know, or what they don’t understand.
I think Van Graas is at the bar near the stage. Dwarf with a cyber-eye. Can’t miss him.

I crawled in here after I gobli... after I Awoke.

Mrs. Kubota took me in and gave me a job. I've been here ever since.
It’s not the size they charge more for. It's the blood under the fingernails.
Catch you round.
More than just my job. It’s my role in the Union family. It’s how I belong.
Not the comparison made of most trolls, but I’m happy to defy expectations.
If I had any reason to suspect that, you’d be on the floor. But seeing as how you walked in together, I’m going to assume you had something to do with her being found. You have my thanks.
Always wondered what she saw in him, but I guess he ain’t all bad. I’m just glad she’s back.
Evening, $(l.sir).
There’s been more talk of the Ripper killings. Some people are saying they’re hate crimes on account of the victims all being human.
My thoughts exactly.

People are just too quick to label something a hate crime. Check the census. The city is still 66% humans. Close your eyes and throw a rock, you’re more than likely to hit a human. Doesn’t make it a hate crime.

Still a crime though, and that should be enough.

{{GM}}This is the first real rise you’ve seen out of Mr. Kluwe. But he reins himself in with a roll of his thick shoulders and a smoothing of his jacket.{{/GM}}

Don’t worry. Not gonna start throwing rocks. Wouldn’t want to reinforce the stereotype.
I'll leave you to your business, $(l.sir).
Well, I wouldn’t go that far.
Still doesn’t mean the killings are racially motivated.
Glad to see you well, $(l.name).
As always. Though people are more on edge than usual.
Word got around about that latest Ripper killing, and people aren’t feeling safe. And when people feel unsafe, they either go to ground or they huddle up.

You noticed how empty the streets are these days?  That’s cuz everyone’s either locked in their squats, holed up in places like this, or seeking refuge in the nearest Universal Brotherhood chapter house.

Safety in numbers, chummer.
Not that we need numbers here. I got it covered.
Later.
Combat ready. Perhaps more so than usual. Tensions are up.
There’s a good bit of nervous energy in the place, for sure.
Welcome back, $(l.name).
I feel like I should be asking you.
Word around here was that you were closing in on the Ripper when you last left. Dare I ask how that went?
I hope that means what I think it means.
And yet your shoulders are no more relaxed, and you still survey the room like a $(l.man) who has yet to return from war. This isn’t over, is it?

{{GM}}The big guy sighs.{{/GM}}

I was hoping this would soon be all behind us. The Barrens has a short memory, but for wounds such as these, it makes an exception. To see this prolonged, I fear for how it may forever change the landscape.

Gangs have already started to take advantage of the chaos left in the Ripper’s wake. And that only serves to destabilize what semblance of order there was, paving the way for the megacorps to make land grabs and push poor SINless further to the fringes, where the dangers are greatest.

Sooner or later, even the Union could be threatened.
Let’s hope.
Take care, $(l.name).
Maybe a couple.
Good thing we’ve got each other.
Where he belongs. Good.
Sam would be grateful, I’m sure.
I get the feeling you’re the one carrying the intel these days.
Most the rumors are about you and what you’ve been doing.
You all right, $(l.name)?
I heard you made trouble with the Universal Brotherhood.

People are starting to say there is some dark drek going down in those chapter houses. Like they’re taking advantage of the poor folk who go there looking for help. I gotta say, that makes my blood boil.
It’s bad enough that the government labels half the population as probationary citizens. We don’t need more people preying on the most defenseless members of society.
And I hate to bring race into it, but it’s a fact that a good number of people who go to the Brotherhood for help are metas like me. But what options do we have?
Do you realize that it would take an act of Congress to grant me full citizenship?  And they have yet to do it once. Wars are started more easily than a meta gets a SIN in this country.
So it’s no wonder we turn to people like the Brotherhood, who claim to listen to our woes, and speak on our behalf.

{{GM}}He catches himself, takes a breath, and visibly calms.{{/GM}}

Sorry. I just know some folks who went to the Brotherhood for these very reasons. I might have ended up there myself if Mrs. Kubota hadn’t taken me in.
Whatever’s going on in there, I trust you’ll put an end to it, one way or another.
Go get ‘em, $(l.sir).
Dresden! Get out here!

Towering over the diminutive coroner is a homicide detective right out of Central Casting -  if you ignore the tusks, pointed ears, and neanderthal brow. You can smell his cheap aftershave from a mile away.
So this new Ripper vic, Watts... name's familiar. Didn’t his mother kill herself a while back?
{{GM}}He chortles.{{/GM}} C'mon, she offed herself... I had it on very good authority.
Now let's go, Dresden - give me something to work with here. This Ripper case is my ticket to a Lieutenant’s badge.
So... I’m looking for a whacked-out surgeon?
You’re no damn help, dwarf.
The Lone Star detective finally notices you. You note his superhuman powers of observation.
Who the hell 're you?
That’s right. And if you ever do hear something, I'll knock it right out of your head.

If you're new in town, then let me be the first to tell you that I run the show.

If you get in my way, you’ll end up back here on a slab.
{{GM}}His face contorts in a hideous snarl.{{/GM}} You're funny, $(l.race). Maybe, we’ll find another time to do a little dance later, you and me.

The drek you were! 

You get anywhere near my investigation and it will be YOU on the slab, $(l.race).

{{GM}}He looks back at the dwarf.{{/GM}}

Dresden, get me more. I am putting someone in a cell or a box THIS week and claiming my promotion!

{{GM}}McKlusky smirks.{{/GM}}

Want some advice, moron? Of course you don't. You're a shadowrunner and you live by your own rules, don'tcha?

I suggest you keep your smart-ass remarks to yourself this time, $(l.race). 

Mr. Telestrian isn’t some streetmeat you can impress or intimidate. He’s the brains behind the throne of Tir Tairngire and one of the richest men in Seattle.

I suggest you keep your smart-ass remarks to yourself this time.

Mr. Telestrian isn’t some streetmeat you can impress or intimidate. He’s the brains behind the throne of Tir Tairngire and one of the richest men in Seattle.
You’re dumber than I thought. Enjoy your chat. I’ll dispose of your body later.
You’re dumber than I thought. Enjoy your chat. I’ll dispose of your body later.
You’re gonna die, asshole.
You’ll still die screaming.
Mmm hmm.
Yeah, but you’re gonna die screaming.

{{GM}}Detective McKlusky stands smirking, surrounded by armed and twitchy Lone Star officers.{{/GM}}

Good morning, moron!

{{GM}}The smirk broadens.{{/GM}}

That's right, dummy. Keep flapping your jaw. That sort of thing will be perfect for where you're going.

{{GM}}The smirking stops.{{/GM}}

What I want is to take you into an alley and come out alone. But there's someone who wants to meet you, so we're going for a little ride.

{{GM}}He chortles.{{/GM}}

You really that dumb, dummy? Do you know who I work for? The same guy whose building you just broke into.

Mr. Telestrian wants to meet you in person. He wants to chat about last night's fun an' games.

You can come along quietly or you can meet him in a body bag.

{{GM}}He smiles wolfishly.{{/GM}}

Come on, drek-for-brains. Make the wrong choice.
Damn. I was hoping to be able to bring you in feet-first.
I'm counting on it, ass-hat.

{{GM}}McKlusky’s alternating between trying to look important and trying to avoid actual work.{{/GM}}

What are you looking at, choob?

{{GM}}McKlusky beams, then his brow furrows.{{/GM}}

I did? I didn’t hear about it.

{{GM}}He smirks.{{/GM}}
Heh. I’ll surprise *them* then.
Izzat right?
Won what?
What the? Mr. Telestrian! Hey, Mr. Telestrian!
What're you talking about?
What the hell are you doing here, Aguirre? I told you to stay downtown and work the streets. You horning in on my investigation, you little puke?

You wanna help? Do what you're fragging told. That's how you can help.

Who's the Native broad? This is a crime scene, Officer Aguirre, not some Salish drum circle.
Well, get her out of here. I don't need her polluting my evidence.

Then why am I wasting my time here? I've got a promotion riding on that Emerald City asshat.

Get the body loaded and get this place squared away.

You're not a cop.

{{GM}}She looks up, watching you warily.{{/GM}}

{{GM}}The suspicion in her voice and eyes departs almost instantly when the subject turns to the deceased.{{/GM}}

He's my brother. I... I've been trying to speak with him.
But I'm afraid his spirit is too disoriented by his recent journey to answer me.
Yes, for all the good it's doing me. If I could just make contact, I might be able to deliver justice and allow my brother's spirit to rest.

Not so far as I can tell. Not among the living anyway.

{{GM}}Realization then dawns, bringing the light back into her eyes.{{/GM}}

But my brother's spirit may not be alone here this night.

{{GM}}She closes her eyes and concentrates, droning a low chant you can barely hear.{{/GM}}

Yes... There are others... Other spirits who may be of help.

{{GM}}Her features harden in frustration, as if she's searching for something that remains elusive. Sighing, she opens her eyes and fixes you with a stare.{{/GM}}
But I can't do it alone.

This warehouse contains the spirits of plane crash victims trapped between worlds. 

They wish to help. They wish to be heard. And I think they have something to say about my brother's murder.
Lone Star won't let me in there, but you seem to have the run of the place.

If you can find personal items that belonged to the victims, I believe that I can summon a spirit to speak with us. 

I'll need at least two such objects. Can you find them for me?

A spirit is but an echo, and my brother's is in a very fragile state.

{{GM}}Her brow then furrows with a sudden thought.{{/GM}}

{{GM}}She looks at you, the flattery largely failing to affect her, given the circumstances. But then inspiration dispels the dark cloud surrounding her.{{/GM}}

Perhaps. My brother's spirit is too weak. That's a given.

{{GM}}She glares at you for a moment and you can see pain in her eyes, along with something else. Power.{{/GM}}

He's my brother, and I'm trying to learn from him the identity of his killer.

Yes. He's my older brother. I've asked Bear to help me speak to him...

{{GM}}She shakes her head, looking like one who has found herself lost for the first time in her life.{{/GM}}
We'll need at least two objects of significance from inside the warehouse. I'd go myself, but these pigs have barred me from entering.
It feels as though you have found enough objects.

{{GM}}You hand her the objects you've found. She closes her eyes and chants over them for a time. When her eyes open again, they're filled with tears.{{/GM}}

Thank you. These items are all I will need. It shouldn't take me long to--
What the hell is all this?
This is a crime scene, officer. Not some Salish drum circle.
Ma'am, consider yourself contacted.
Now get the hell out.
No, that's a corpse, which makes it my business.
Watch your ass, else you're going to find my boot lodged up in it.

{{GM}}He smirks.{{/GM}} Hey, I'm already at "shoot on sight" status with these guys. Killing John Paul means one less 'Weener to shoot me in the back while I'm buying smokes. Hell - they'll be doing the same to you as soon as there's a profit in it.
How naive. Ah, well... maybe it'll be worth it to see him running off with his tail between his legs.
Hold on, $(l.name). Think about this.

The Brotherhood probably retains mercs like the ones that just attacked us for their security detail. You're gonna need a full team if you're heading to their chapter house. 

I can arrange for some of Delilah's runners to meet you there, if you want to go now.
You change your mind?

{{GM}}Jake shakes his head smirking as you approach. When you finally get close, he breaks out laughing.{{/GM}}

I heard the Universal Brotherhood was getting shot up on the police scanner - knew it had to be you. Did you get her?
Not for a minute.
I heard Mitsuhama’s looking for a team and they specifically want a woman. No idea what that’s about.

And I just got word that something’s going on at Crater Lake. The Tir’s got it on total lockdown. No-fly zone, the whole thing. There could be something in it for us, if we poke around.

And there’s always the Ork Underground - haven’t seen that yet, have you? I got plenty of friends there who are good for a job.
Something’s bound to come up. Whaddaya say?
You do that.

If you want to find me, I’ll be at McKraken’s later - got a taste for some seafood.

Take it easy, chummer.
Good! We can hash it out over dinner.
I get it. Me too. And those morgue drawers are kinda tight to begin with. Hard to fit someone else.
I get it. This town isn’t for everybody. Well, there’s work all over.
I heard Mitsuhama’s looking for a team and they specifically want $(l.race_plural). No idea what that’s about.
I heard Mitsuhama’s looking for a team. No idea what that’s about but it could be lucrative.
Figured you would.
Guess she had it coming.

{{GM}}He slides his sunglasses down his nose. Cyber-eyes.{{/GM}}

Sounds like there’s a story there.
Ah, justice is served.
Typical. Well... odds are, those are the guys we're after.

Yep. That's him.

{{GM}}Jake checks his pistol.{{/GM}}
This is one of the two that got away. If I hadn't missed my shot, these idiots would have given up and moved on by now.
Whoa! Easy there.
I told John to wake me up at six in the morning. Is it six yet? It doesn’t feel like six yet.
Yeah, Dresden thinks he's pretty funny. You're not the first person he's pulled this one on.

{{GM}}He chuckles.{{/GM}}

Well, so much for a good night’s sleep. On the plus side, I noticed you haven’t killed me yet, so... that’s good. If you aren’t after me, then what’s your story?
Sam, eh? Glad somebody cares. We used to drink together every now and then, over at the Union. Decent enough guy. Always in trouble over something or other though...
{{GM}}Jake yells towards the other side of the room.{{/GM}} John! Is this $(l.guy) cool?

Thanks for volunteering me. {{GM}}He pauses. Might be sizing you up, but it’s hard to tell behind those shades.{{/GM}}

Alright then. The name’s Jake. And you are...?

Nice to meet you. Well, it sounds like you’re taking a dive into the deep end here. John’s right, I might be able to help you out.

I was with Sam the other night, the night of the murder... poor guy. He was hangin’ at the Seamstresses Union that night, tripped out and rowdy. I’d been laying low there for a few days after a bad run. Mrs. Kubota asked me to throw Sam out, so I did. But out in the alley some gangers got the jump on me...

{{GM}}He winces.{{/GM}}

Damn. Maybe I need some soykaf after all. John, could you grab me a cup?

Thanks John, you’re a real pal. Anyway, there’s a big fat corp bounty on my head. Like I said, my last job didn’t exactly go according to plan. Out in the alley, a few Halloweeners got the jump on us. Damn gangers thought they could turn a quick profit off of my head. 

{{GM}}Jake smiles, and you get the impression that didn’t work out so well for the gangers.{{/GM}}
Sam stumbled off during the fight, though, and that’s the last I saw of him. Until he turned up here, dead on arrival. Reminds me of my last stay in this place.
It’s a long story. I end up in the morgue more often than the average guy.
{{GM}}Jake laughs.{{/GM}} I’ll hold you to that, John. Anyway, they found Sam’s body less than a block away from the Union, missing liver and all.

Tell you what. You look like you can handle yourself in a fight. I could use some backup to settle the score with those Halloweeners out there. Their leader's got the whole gang searching the Barrens for me. I need to get rid of that asshole.

In return, I’ll take you to the place Sam was murdered. It’s not safe to hit these streets alone at night, trust me.

{{GM}}Jake eyes you up and down.{{/GM}} ...And maybe I’ll throw in some decent supplies while we’re at it.
What d’ya say?
I get it. Gotta be careful in this trade. I’m not one to go back on a deal though, John can vouch for that. Plus, you know where I’m hiding out, right? Doesn’t leave me a lot of room to sell you out.
Guess I’d say the same, in your shoes. Fine. There’s a credstick with 1,000 nuyen with your name on it. Help me dust those gangers and it’s yours.
Hard bargainer. Alright, ¥1,500 it is, but you’d better be worth it...

Great. I’ve been hiding out here ever since that run-in with those Halloweeners. Whiny bunch of gangers, but this stretch of the Barrens is their turf. Hell, I’m surprised you even made it to this morgue in one piece without packing some heat.
So, you need a weapon?
My weapon of choice as well. Here, take this one.
So, ready for an evening out on the town?

Well, they're one of the nastier gangs in town. Their symbol is a flaming Jack O'Lantern, but you wouldn't like their version of Trick-or-Treating very much. Around here they're led by a troll named John Paul. He's got all the Halloweeners in the Barrens looking for me. We take him out, maybe I can breathe a bit easier.
I'll follow your lead. The Halloweeners aren't looking for you - yet.
Hey, I've already provided the party favors!
Like the subtle approach, eh? Alright, try this cannon out.
Spray and pray? Fair enough. Take this. It’s a little beat up, but it works.
You don’t like library windows do you? Never mind. Here’s a rifle you can borrow.
All I've got here is a baseball bat - but you look like you can handle yourself.

I know they found his body a block away from the Union, just lying there in broad daylight. That’s the Barrens for you. {{GM}}Jake looks down, his expression masked by chrome and crimson glass.{{/GM}} 

Shame though. Wish I’d been there. If those slaggin’ gangers hadn’t come along...

Fair enough. Well, it sounds like you’re taking a dive into the deep end here. John’s right, I might be able to help you out. 

I was with Sam the other night, the night of the murder... poor guy. He was hangin’ at the Seamstresses Union that night, tripped out and rowdy. I’d been laying low there for a few days after a bad run. Mrs. Kubota asked me to throw Sam out, so I did. But out in the alley some gangers got the jump on me...

And well mannered, too! Such a rare thing in this city. 

Well, it sounds like you’re taking a dive into the deep end here. John’s right, I might be able to help you out. I was with Sam the other night, the night of the murder... poor guy. He was hangin’ at the Seamstresses Union that night, tripped out and rowdy. I’d been laying low there for a few days after a bad run. Mrs. Kubota asked me to throw Sam out, so I did. But out in the alley some gangers got the jump on me...
Well, playing dead, mostly. A bunch of gangers out there want my head, and I’d rather keep it. Figured I’d lay low for a while, give them some time to cool down.
Give me a break, chummer. Haven't had my soykaf yet.
Don't tell me you haven't considered it. Cheaper than a coffin hotel, and the service is just as good.
I saw him out the night he died. Only fitting I see him out today.
We don’t know each other too well, but it seems to me that you need to find a better group of people to associate with.
Yeah, well it’s part of living in the shadows. Do the research, chummer.

{{GM}}You watch as Jake pulls out his modified PDA and slots the chip.{{/GM}}

Mister... Wylie here was with Eagle Security. They work for the UB. That must've been Lynne Telestrian’s security detail we just chewed through. If she’s protecting Jessica, they’ll be inside the Universal Brotherhood.

Suit yourself, lady. I was only stopping by to pay my respects to Sam. Merc hit squads, the Universal Brotherhood... not my scene, at the moment. 

I can call up some of Delilah's runners if you want to go there now. Let me know what you decide.

Sorry friend... I think this is where our paths diverge. 

Thanks again for the help with those Halloweeners. Here's your payment - they don't take nuyen where I'm going, and you look like you could use the funds.

Yeah, you too. 

{{GM}}He starts to leave, then tilts his head back.{{/GM}}

Hah.

{{GM}}He starts to leave, then tilts his head back.{{/GM}}

Yeah, sure. 

{{GM}}He starts to leave, then tilts his head back.{{/GM}}

Hey, one more thing. When you're done checking out your pal's crime scene, pop in to the Seamstresses Union. It's just down the street. You need gear, information, or just a damned stiff drink, that's the place to be. Best dive this side of Chicago. I used that place as a base of operations for years, back in the day. 

Make the right friends there and I'm sure you'll get to the bottom of this Ripper business.

Well - nice knowin' ya.

{{GM}}Jake turns, and disappears into shadowy depths of the Redmond Barrens.{{/GM}}

Sorry chummer, not my style. Besides - you seem like you can take care of yourself.

{{GM}}He takes a step, then tilts his head back.{{/GM}}
So, welcome to the Barrens. Guess I'll need to find a new spot to hide my gear.
{{GM}}He chuckles.{{/GM}} Well, at least they were too dumb to break my maglock.
Now that you've got some gear... let's go deal with those Halloweeners.
True. I've been around, but Seattle's still a runner's paradise as far as I'm concerned.
Only by the hour. Hey, you've seen where I stay the night.
Make it quick, I need to operate.
This is going to have to wait. This woman needs surgery. Now!
Okay folks, I’m going to have to ask you go sit in the waiting area. Watch some trivid or something. This young lady and I have work to do.
You’ll take what I give you, lady. Now, let’s have a look at your face...
Excuse me?

Do you see that arm, chummer?  I have to operate. Now. 

Okay Coyote, let’s take a look at your face...

You’re tough, kid, but you’re not that tough. 

Okay Coyote, let’s take a look at your face...

{{GM}}Doctor Castle glances up only briefly from her charts. Her impish friend, on the other hand, tracks your every move like some sort of humanoid raptor with a mouse under its shadow.{{/GM}}

Lived to see another day, have you?
So long as you end up here in the end and not on some Organ Grinders table, I'm satisfied.
Granted, a repeat customer means a person courting death more often than any good doctor should condone. But I don't suppose I can dissuade you from your course of action?
Very well. Then let's talk about how I can help ensure you make it back alive once more.

{{GM}}Doctor Castle pauses in her work, setting down her tablet and appearing to grow introspective.{{/GM}}

I suppose you might expect that I had a fall from grace. Certainly that is the case with many of my... peers. If not that, you might think that I was compelled to do this because a friend or family member became a shadowrunner, and I hurried to their assistance one fateful night.

But the reality is that I grew up knowing this is what I would do with my life. My parents were both shadowrunners, and very successful ones at that. So successful that they retired in a very large house and raised a litter of kids who grew up hearing the impossible tales of their parents' younger years.
Of course, we were expressly forbidden to follow in their footsteps. We were all expected to attend university and take full advantage of the opportunities they had fought so hard to give us.

So Mom and Dad were proud to see me pursuing a medical degree, and they had only minor reservations about me applying that expertise to the treatment of shadowrunners. After all, they never would have survived long enough to meet and fall in love if not for the ministrations of several street docs along the way.
So I suppose you could say I was born into this life, after a fashion.

{{GM}}She picks up her tablet and resumes her work.{{/GM}}

Is there anything else I can help you with?
I suppose it's nights like these that ensure I have plenty of work come morning.
I take it there's more life and limb to be risked, then?  I suggest you ere on the side of limbs, since I can charge you four separate times.
Back so soon?  And uninjured, no less. The job must have been easier than I expected.
What can I do for you?

{{GM}}Doc Castle looks to be better rested and in better spirits than the last time you saw her. She's also clearly had a shower and a change of clothes, maybe even some sleep. Her eyes are sharp when they turn up from her work to greet you.{{/GM}}

Welcome back, $(l.name).

Still all in one piece, I see. More's the pity for me, but I'll still take your money. Perhaps a full physical is in order?  Or we can call it a medical consultation. That's where I roughly determine the odds of your survival based on your professional... proclivities?  It comes with a lollipop.
So what will it be?

{{GM}}Doctor Castle sets down her work and fixes you with a hard look. Her expression normally falls on an axis of varying degrees of indifference, depending on her level of tiredness. But now, it has shifted over to something decidedly darker.{{/GM}}

Is this morbid curiosity?
Then I suppose I should be equally professional and answer without judging you for asking. And I should not let it bias me the next time you fall under my knife.

To give you a hard number is difficult. There are many who I would have considered patients that never made it home. And so I suppose one could say they died while under my care, though not as a result of it.

But the number I think you want is the number of patients who died on my table.

And that number is six.

Six who I was unable to save, spread across a thirteen year career. And I remember each and every one, down to the smallest detail, including the moment where I realized I could do nothing more for them.

I would argue that four of those six were beyond helping from the moment they passed through my door. The fifth died as a result of complications while installing a stolen piece of experimental cyberware, which I had cautioned against using.
Entirely my fault. I made a mistake. I can't claim I was overtired, or inexperienced, or distracted. I simply made the wrong decision, and a young man died as a result.

{{GM}}She picks up her tablet and resumes her work.{{/GM}}

Yes, well, will there be anything else?
I suppose that would be prudent.
It's fine. And I have no reservations about answering the question.

Have you reconsidered that physical?  If you do well enough, I might be able to pass along a discount for a Doc Wagon contract.

What will it be?
How can I help you?

{{GM}}For once, Dr. Castle is not utterly absorbed in her work. In fact, she seems to have set aside whatever she's doing to give you her full attention. Perhaps word has spread about what you've already faced and what is still to come.{{/GM}}

I'm glad to see you're all right, $(l.name). I was afraid they'd be carting you back to me in pieces.

I don't suppose it would do me any good to suggest an early retirement?  Or, at the very least, that you might delay whatever is coming long enough for you to get a decent night's sleep and a full meal?
You shouldn't have to die at all. That's something you still have control over.

Oh, but who am I kidding?  I know your type all too well. Words of caution have little worth, and words of prohibition only make you more eager to throw yourself into the fire.

You're like children that way.
So, fine. Do what you will. Just tell me what I can do to keep you alive that much longer.

Well, let's review. In the past few days, I've lost one patient, and nearly lost a second. I've also learned that a man claiming to be a fellow doctor was responsible for the first of those deaths. And now I hear that another supposed force for good has been committing heinous acts on innocent men and women.

Quite frankly, it's almost enough to make a woman give up on the whole human race.

But then I remember why I don't. I remember how many innocent lives Mrs. Kubota has saved by giving them place and purpose here at the Union. I remember seemingly useless wastes of flesh like Paco risking their lives to save the ones they love. I remember that some people are still willing to stand up against those who would seek to do harm to others.
And that's the real reason you won't hear me argue against your chosen course of action. Because I agree that it must be done. I only hope it can be done without any more innocents coming to harm.
I know. But do what you can and bring us one step closer.
I'd like to wake up to a world short one less evil.
Now, how can I help make that happen?
Then do the next best thing. Put an end to this.
I appreciate the lie.
And I imagine those were the last words spoken by many brave idiots.
Who said I do. A good cash cow like you only comes around every so often, and you're no good to me dead.

Forget something?  Or just taking one last browse through my stock?  Best to be prepared. Better safe than sorry. Measure twice, cut once. And a hundred other trite sayings that seem woefully inadequate under the circumstances.

But what shall it be?
What can I do for you?

{{GM}}The doctor herself is an unassuming sort, perhaps the type to go unnoticed entirely, if not for the spritely spirit perched on her shoulder like her own personal gargoyle. The spirit's burning eyes follow you constantly, even as the doctor's own eyes are buried in her charts. However, she does look up long enough to acknowledge your approach.{{/GM}}

I'm Dr. Castle. I understand you were instrumental in bringing Coyote back to us. Thank you for that.
Thank you. It's a shame she wouldn't let me repair her face, though.
Surprisingly routine, as it so happens.
High praise for a simple arm swap, especially since she wouldn’t let me repair her face.

{{GM}}She notes you eyeballing the facilities.{{/GM}}

I can tell you're surprised to find a full-service medbay under a dive bar in a slum. Don't be. This is a shadowrunner bar, after all.

For a purveyor of cyberware and trauma kits, there is no better place to set up a practice. I patch runners up, install and maintain their cyberware, and provide medical supplies for their runs. I may not be as mobile as Doc Wagon, but I'm the next best thing.

Can I help you with anything?
Trauma kits are fully-automated stabilization units that include a defibrillator, spray-on synthetic skin, and medical nanomachines. They can save a runner's life, if you move fast enough.
Exactly, but you have to be quick. This is Doc Wagon's own field tech, but even their stuff has its limits.

While modern medical technology makes surgery less disruptive than it used to be, it's still an ordeal for both the body and the spirit, requiring extensive recuperation to properly heal.

I am trained in the ways of the spirit world as well as the scientific world. I do my best to heal the whole patient.

This little guy supports the healing rituals I perform on my patients after surgery, dramatically reducing their recovery time. Not standard procedure, of course, but the results speak for themselves.

{{GM}}Doc Castle gives you a final once-over, noting your gear.{{/GM}}

I'm sure we'll see each other again.
You don’t look like you're bleeding, so I assume you want to see my current inventory of cyberware and medkits.
Can I help you?

The magic dealer's area is abuzz with chaotic activity as you watch the contents of a dozen boxes fly through the air. The articles land on Aljernon's table in some elaborate sorting scheme while the man himself casually sits reading a magazine from the previous century.
Welcome, $(l.name). Is there anything I might provide to make your life easier, or perhaps make another’s more difficult?

{{GM}}The elf's face curls into a grimace.{{/GM}}

I'm sorry. I find the very thought repugnant.

You must understand, a shaman does not follow the literal incarnation of his totem. One who pledges himself to Cat does not drink milk from a saucer or claw the drapes. Instead, a shaman follows the ideals of his totem.
Dog is less a canine and more a symbol of loyalty. Coyote is less a scavenger and more a trickster.

So you can understand why I find the idea of an insect totem so foreign. To what quality would a shaman aspire in such a case?

{{GM}}A chill visibly runs through him.{{/GM}}
Please, let us speak of more pleasant things. Perhaps, commerce?
Hello again. Please let me know if there's any way I can help.
What more can I do for you?

{{GM}}There is a twinkle in Aljernon’s eye that wasn’t there when you spoke at the Seamstresses Union. {{/GM}} 

It is good to see you again. There is much to do.
I do not “run” at all, $(l.name). I go where I am needed. I am needed here.
I am doing what I do, $(l.name). Providing those in need with the tools they require.
No. I do not explain. I provide a service.
Seek me out after you have spoken to James Telestrian. Perhaps I can be of service again.

Two elements are required to bring one across the Void: a shaman and a host. 

First, the spirit calls upon a shaman, often in dreams. The spirit seduces the shaman with promises of great power. The shaman then accepts the spirit as his totem. 

Next, the insect spirit requires a suitable host.

The initial bugs prepare a nest for the summoning of a Queen. Once a nest has its Queen, she literally explodes with newly-manifested insect spirits. They swarm out of the nest, feasting on all the flesh they can find and implanting more insect spirits into the fresh corpses.

Again, and again, and again.

{{GM}}Aljernon eyes you keenly.{{/GM}}

Bravo. That is precisely the idea. The insect spirit exists on both planes simultaneously.
I have been impressed with you for some time, $(l.name). It is good that you are here.
May I provide spells, spirit foci, or fetishes to help you on this critical quest?
Speak it.
I am a peddler of magical spells, spirits, and foci. Nothing more.
No.
Do you require my magic?
You require some magical assistance?

{{GM}}The elf called Aljernon appears to be in a hushed but intense conversation as you approach. However, the other party of this discourse is nowhere to be seen. Turning to greet you, Aljernon dismisses his invisible associate with a wave of his hand.{{/GM}}

Welcome, fellow child of the Sixth World. What can I help you with today?
My story is not so different from those who have come before. I am but a merchant, though one whose wares are far from mundane.

I came here from Tir Tairngire four years ago, after the governor of Seattle signed an exclusive trade agreement with the Council of Princes. And so as the flow of magical goods came to pass through this city's ports, so have I.
At times I miss the Tir, but as unlikely as it may sound, this new home of mine is far more interesting.
Is there anything else I can help you with?
Welcome back. What more can I do for you?
How can I help you?

{{GM}}Aljernon stands stock still, his eyes closed and his face composed in a mask of stern concentration. But then a small snore escapes his nose, instantly shattering the elf's carefully manicured mystique. A clearing of your throat is enough to rouse the talismonger from his "meditation".{{/GM}}
Ah, $(l.name). I was watching your approach from the astral realm. So good of you to visit again.
What might I do for you on this fine day?
Always a curious place, the astral. Though a mirror world to our own, it is ever changing.
I suppose the same can be said of our reality, but things of a transitory nature here on the physical plane can cause long-lasting changes in the astral.

The Barrens that we can see and touch records the past with blood stains on the sidewalk and bullet holes in storefront walls. But the astral goes further, recording the emotions that accompanied these acts of violence. These events... texture astral space.
There is a great deal of fear and distrust. In some places, it is strong enough to impact the working of magic.

But enough of my doom-saying. You must have better things to do.

Is there any way I can help?
Given the state of the astral these days, I might soon make that trade myself.
Sadly, not all who are Awakened to the astral plane have their eyes so opened. There are plenty of magicians who revel in the dark energies left in astral space.
Did you need something else?
How can I help?

{{GM}}Aljernon looks the same as always. So much so that one may wonder if he ever takes time to eat or sleep. Perhaps his presence on this plane of existence is insubstantial enough that he isn't subject to such base needs. Or perhaps he just does these things when no one is looking.{{/GM}}
Dark clouds surround you, my friend. But perhaps there is something I could provide that would help with whatever weighs so heavily upon you?
The spirits are stirring. Something has upset the natural order. There is something new... different.

{{GM}}Aljernon takes a hard look at you for what may be the first time. But this is the hard look of a magician, whose eyes pierce the veil of reality and burrow through a man's soul.{{/GM}}

I can see you have met with disturbing visions.
But there is something else out there. Something not born of life, as other spirits are.

{{GM}}Shaking his head, Aljernon opens his clasped hands to you, as if begging forgiveness.{{/GM}}

Too often this world leaves us confounded. I pray I have not left you in such a state with my idle musings.
Please, can I be of service to you today?

{{GM}}Aljernon smiles at you, much as a parent smiles at an overconfident child.{{/GM}}

On the contrary, spirits are the very essence of the natural world. They are born of land, sea, and sky. They are born of Man and beast and plant.
Would that I knew. The spirits speak in riddles. They leave me with more questions than answers.
Is there more I can help you with?
What can I do for you?

{{GM}}Past the bar, the edges of the safe house become somewhat indistinct due to the magical haze surrounding a particular elf. 

The man seems only half of this realm, his mind wandering the far horizons of astral space while his body peddles his otherworldly wares.{{/GM}}

Good evening, young $(l.Race) and welcome to this humble home that we call the Union. I am Aljernon Half-Dream.

To ease your way through the Sixth World, I offer you the best in magical foci, spells, and fetishes for the conjuring of spirits.

I knew you would be back - the stars foretold it. 

Actually, it was more like the laws of supply and demand. What magical needs can I satisfy?
What can I do for you?
I understand that you have saved the day by moving a troubled soul from this plane to another. I congratulate you. Is there magic I could provide to help you with your next task?

{{GM}}Father Willy's greasy black hair and disturbing tattoos are quite the contrast to the flawless features and exquisite clothes of Lynne Telestrian. He doesn't look the part of a spokesman. You see the adulation in the eyes of those who linger around him just to bask in his presence a moment more. Apparently, he is a charismatic speaker.{{/GM}}

Welcome, my friend from the shadows. I rejoice to be with you.

Please. I lived in the shadows for many years until I came into the light and fellowship of the Universal Brotherhood. I see on you the chains of guilt and sorrow, built link upon link by the crimes you commit in name of nuyen.
Yes, that is what I told myself for years, until the weight of my chains brought me to the pit of despair. But the Universal Brotherhood saved me! It can save you too, my friend.
Ask and I will provide what wisdom I can.

Justice? I'm afraid I don't know what you are talking about. But I do know that Justice is a point of view and that you sound as if you are looking at it through a cracked lens.

If you need to talk to someone I may be able to help. Perhaps a counselor?
A hopeful soul is filled with strength. What I can do to add to this strength?
That realization has now led you here, how may help you along your path?
We are more than friends here at the Universal Brotherhood. We are family. That is an unbreakable bond. Join us and find the power of Belonging.

I once thought so too. I thought that belonging to something meant giving away my freedom. My individuality. But now I see the Truth. How together, we are more than just the sum of our parts. 

You can learn this, too. Is there any other wisdom I can impart upon you?

Nor did I, once. I walked in shadow, as you do now, seeing others as tools, pawns, prey. 

I thought that belonging to something meant giving away my freedom. My individuality. But now I see the Truth. How together, we are more than just the sum of our parts. 

You can learn this, too. Is there any other wisdom I can impart upon you?

That's the whole point, friend. I crawled out the shadows and found a family here. Now, I Belong to something. Something important. Something that will change the world.

Our counselors on the other side of the public area well help you and lend a kind ear to hear all your troubles. Is there any other wisdom I can impart upon you?
No, it doesn't! Perhaps you have already taken the first steps, my friend. How may I assist you?
Hello again, my friend. How may I help you along the path to Truth?
May I ask what business you have with Grandmother Jessica? She is very busy.

{{GM}}He looks surprised.{{/GM}}

What would Grandmother Jessica have to do with such grisly business?
Are you accusing her of being involved with those murders?

My friend, you are delusional. Grandmother Jessica is a bringer of new life and truth.

She is spending time focusing herself in the Inner Sanctum, where she is protected from the likes of you.

If the police feel that Grandmother Jessica can contribute anything to their investigation, I am sure she will be happy to cooperate. However, I will not disturb her mediations in the Inner Sanctum for a ruffian looking to exploit her or ruin her good name.

You should seek enlightenment with one of our counselors.

If the police feel that Grandmother Jessica can contribute anything to their investigation, I am sure she will be happy to cooperate. However, I will not disturb her mediations in the Inner Sanctum for a ruffian looking to exploit her or ruin her good name.

You should seek enlightenment with one of our counselors.
I understand. I’m afraid that I cannot help you. Grandmother Jessica has entered the Inner Sanctum to meditate and cannot be disturbed. If you wish, I can tell her that you asked for her.
Very well. I will let her know you were seeking her guidance.
If that is your wish. Is there anything else?

Grandmother Jessica has entered the Inner Sanctum and cannot be disturbed. I must tell you that the news of her brother's murder will not change anything for her. 

When we join the Universal Brotherhood, we put our birth families behind us and devote ourselves to Belonging to something larger.

I know that this seems heartless and it may be hard to understand, but if you speak with one of our counselors it will all start to make sense. Is that all?
Many are attracted to the bright light that is Lynne Telestrian, but she cannot see everyone. What business do you have with her?
Mother Lynne left about 15 minutes ago. Regardless, I don't believe that a Telestrian would have any information relevant to a serial killer.

You are delusional and need help. Our counselors could help you find the stability that you clearly need. Surely there some other gift of wisdom I may give to help you find your way out of the darkness.
I know enough. I know that you are in pain and that the Universal Brotherhood can help you. Belonging is healing. I urge you - speak to one of our counselors. Learn the Truth.

Mother Lynne is a beacon that helps many come into the light of the Universal Brotherhood. Unfortunately, she left the building about 15 minutes ago.

One of our counselors could help you to join our family you should seek their council. Will that be all my child?
Yes? What is it?
Ah, very well. I am one of the Elders here. I can help with that. Where is the problem?
What is the issue? I don't see anything.
What was here a moment ago?
{{GM}}Father Willy has been knocked unconscious by the chloroform.{{/GM}}
{{GM}}You quickly frisk him take his ID card.{{/GM}}
{{GM}}The Father's uniform doesn't fit you{{/GM}}
{{GM}}You change into the Universal Brotherhood uniform{{/GM}}
How big a bug?

{{GM}}He looks around anxiously and then calms down.{{/GM}}

Very funny. Now get back to work or I will report you to the head of maintenance for wasting my time.
$+(l.honorific), I am reporting you to the head of maintenance for wasting my time.
Zzzz ZZZzzZz zzzzZzzz
This is my home! Get out!

Why do you care?

... it... it's my son. That bastard took my son!
One of Stevie J's goons, Ryker. He runs that filthy BTL squat across the hall.
My son, Zipper... he's not a strong boy. And Ryker knows it. Lured him in with those damn chips. "Better Than Life"? HA! What life?!?
And now they're TORTURING him in there!
Those sickos have a simsense recording studio over there! Overheard them say they have a "special guest" up in the penthouse. They plan to torture her with a recording of MY SON'S DYING BREATH!
What about MY SON?!
Thank-you, thank-you.
You bastards!
This is different! Those sickos have a simsense recording studio over there! Overheard them say they have a 'special guest' up in the penthouse. They plan to torture her with MY SON'S DYING BREATH!

They don't have BTL where you're from? It's an illegal type of simsense recording. You know, a recording of... uh... brain patterns or something. They slot it into your head and it's like you're there, living whatever it was they recorded. But these guys remove all the safety limiters from the simsense, and get people addicted.

Ryker over there has his own recording studio, so he can make his own product. They're using my son as a guinea pig to record a torture BTL. So that his boss Stevie J, upstairs, can torture anyone he likes just by slotting the chip. Doesn't even have to lay a hand on 'em. Overheard them say they have a "special guest" up in the penthouse right now.
Have you saved my son yet?
Thank you so much.
I don't know how to thank you...
Leave me alone, you brute.
Oh thank-you, thank-you. I take back all the horrible things I was thinking about you.
Oh thank-you, thank-you. I take back all the horrible things I was thinking about you.
This is my home! Get out!
I don't know how to thank you...
I don't know how to thank you...

Dear Sister Julie,

It is my pleasure to invite you to join us in the Inner Sanctum as a Mother of the Universal Brotherhood. 

I have left your upgraded keycard in your locker. 

We rejoice to be with you!
Father Willy
$(l.name), if we go up there, I'm not leaving until we find Coyote.
$(l.name), if we go up there, I'm not leaving until we find Coyote.
Hey, $(l.name), you're not leaving me down here!

{{GM}}Echoing from down the hallway{{/GM}}

Hey, $(l.name), you're not leaving me down here!
I think so, yeah.
Japanese mafia. Oh man.
Come on $(l.guy), let's go find Coyote.
Jeez, $(l.guy). Do you really think this is the time?
Coyote...
$(l.name)! Can’t you see she’s in pain?
Woah!
What do you need, babe?
Coyote, be careful.
I’m sorry, Carla. I’m so, so sorry.
What’s up, baby?

Sure. Of course. They’re innocent.

I also brought a concussion grenade like you asked. Better than fragging the tweakers if they get caught in the middle.
Right behind you babe. Let’s do this.
I’m sorry, Carla. I’m so, so sorry.

Sure. Of course. They’re innocent.

I also brought a concussion grenade like you asked. Better than fragging the tweakers if they get caught in the middle.
I have a concussion grenade. Better than fragging the tweakers if they get caught in the middle.

The kid in front of you sports the trademark yellow of the Cutters gang. Young, clean-shaven, he stands like he owns the street and everyone on it. He seems distracted, though, glancing around with increasing agitation. He looks over as you approach.
Watch yourself. What you want?
Watch yourself, little $(l.man). What you want?
Watch yourself, dandelion eater. What you want?
Watch yourself, trog. What you want?
That’s none of your fragging business. Who the hell are you?
Yeah, might be. Who the hell are you?
What the - who the hell are you?
And I need YOU to tell me why you think that's my problem. I'm not her boss. Find her yourself.
The tough-guy swagger seems to drain out of Paco. The Cutter is gone, and before you stands a kid in a yellow jacket that doesn't quite fit.

Coyote's missing? Oh man. That would explain...

She was supposed to meet me here over an hour ago. Look, sorry for getting in your face like that. What else do you know? If she's missing, I need to find her.
Yeah, she's a piece of work. Pulls the gear off'a dead runners, sells it right back to the live ones. Coyote must've been in a rush for some new heat, to buy a gun from her...
I know of him, sure. Blake doesn’t allow any Cutters to take side gigs though, so I got no reason to deal with him. Coyote hasn't said anything about taking new work...
Wait a minute... drek! I know where she went. Damn, why couldn't she wait. Damn it!

The Royale Apartments. The landlord, Stevie J, runs a drug ring outta that hellhole. Coyote grew up there... doesn't like to talk about it much. She's been looking for a way to settle the score with that guy for years.

Few days back, I heard Mr. Delilah was looking for runners to steal some sorta item out from under Stevie J's nose. She must've taken the job - I'm sure of it. And if his thugs caught her...

{{GM}}There's a grim determination in Paco's eyes.{{/GM}} I'm going over there. You comin'?
Damn right we will.
Your only lead? Hey... what the hell do you want with her, anyway?
Well... when we get her back, I'm sure she'll help you out. Now let's get moving - the Royale is just a few blocks from here.

{{GM}}Paco looks at you warily.{{/GM}} Fine. But that better be it. 

Now let's get moving - the Royale is just a few blocks from here.

Of course I know my way around a fight. Stevie J better be ready for a world of hurt. {{GM}}Paco curls his hand into a fist.{{/GM}}

Now let's get moving - the Royale is just a few blocks from here.
Zebra meat... what? Look, that shop's just down the block if you want to check it out. But I'm going to the Royale, with or without your help. What's it gonna be?
What, you been rummaging through her stuff? Yeah, that shop's just down the block. What the hell she would need zebra meat for, though... beats me.
Relax, man. My friend just has a question.
Oh.. drek! THAT's why Coyote wanted zebra meat. Everyone talks about the pet Hellhounds Stevie J keeps locked up somewhere in the Royale. And if she never picked it up...
What the hell is that supposed to mean?
Look, that's not my problem. I'm at the bottom of the Cutter ranks anyway - I couldn't do drek about that even if I wanted to.
I don't have time for this. We need to find Coyote.

{{GM}}As your eyes adjust to the flashing lights, you spot the body of a woman, dead on the pavement behind the police line. Panic spreads across Paco's face.{{/GM}} 

Oh... oh no. Is that Coyote?? This isn't happening. God damn it!

{{GM}}Paco steps forward and breathes a huge sigh of relief.{{/GM}}

No... no. It's not her. Thank God.

Look, let's not hang out here too long, alright? Too many Lone Star pigs around. {{GM}}Paco looks over at the victim again.{{/GM}}

It's too bad, whatever happened here... I'm not going to let anything like this happen to Coyote.

Looks like you impressed him. 

I know a fence for those gems. Van Gaas. Follow me.

Looks like you impressed him. 

I know a fence for those gems. Van Graas. Follow me.

Looks like you impressed him. 

I know a fence for those gems. Van Graas. Follow me.
The Royale Apartments. What a hole. Can't imagine what it was like for Coyote, growing up here... {{GM}}Paco trails off.{{/GM}}
If Coyote's here, we have to hurry. She's good but... well... these BTL guys pay to stay well informed. They may have known she was coming.
That must be Coyote. They've got her up there!
Yeah, yeah, you're right. I'll be okay.
Was hoping to get some peace and quiet tonight. You got something for me?
Wouldn't happen to be an Ares flight recorder, would it? Some very powerful buyers are looking for that right now. I can get rid of it for you, for say, 20%? That leaves 2,000 for you.
Pleasure doing business.
Shrewd. Alright, I'll do you a favor.
I'll be here.
{{GM}}Bruno ignores you. He appears to be listening to something in his earpiece.{{/GM}}
You change your mind? My offer of 2,000 stands.
I'm very busy. You show me something worth my time, or you walk away.
I've got 800 nuyen for something like that.
It's done.
As long as you keep bringing in stuff like this, sure.
Don't think too long. Info like that has an expiration date.
That's dangerous info. I'd rather not handle it, so how about 600?
It's done.
I do mind. I'll get someone to do it for me. Deal.
Suit yourself. I'm not the most patient person.
You change your mind?
Hello, stranger. I'm busy right now, so I hope you've got something that's worth my time.
I could certainly find someone interested in that information. Will take some legwork to verify the veracity of such a list... how about 2,000?
Good. I've got other business to attend to. Here's your money.
Good senses, chummer. Alright, I'll give you 3,000.
Uh huh. You know where to find me.
You make up your mind? My offer of 2,000 stands.
I'm enjoying my drink, here. What is it?

Depends on the year. Let me take a look.

{{GM}}He angles his cyber-optics towards the ring, and ponders a moment.{{/GM}}
The inset gems alone are worth 800 nuyen or so, but a collector would probably pay more for that year's ring. You want me to get rid of it? 1200 nuyen.
It's done. I'll transfer the money.
Great. Have fun.
I said I'd pay 1200. Final offer.

{{GM}}He still hasn’t looked at you. He’s going a mile a minute.{{/GM}}

Nothing for you tonight. Sorry.

I'm doing a thing right now. Important thing.

Talk to you later.
I need to finish this call perhaps another time?

{{GM}}$(s.name) is fiddling with his cyber-eye while noodling on his PDA.{{/GM}}

You’re back. Still busy. What’s your story?
I have a fondness for pretty things. Lemme see.

{{GM}}You hear the servos in $(s.name)’s cyber-eye whirr as a magnifying lens slips into position. He bends his head over the stones for two seconds. Maybe less.{{/GM}}

Hmph. Where'd you get this drek? A gumball machine? I’ll give you a thousand for them.
Done. Gimme your credstick.
Suit yourself.
I am.
Fifteen hundred and it’s done.
Done. Gimme your credstick.

{{GM}}He’s puzzled. He looks. You look back - the “I-can-make-you-feel-a special-kind-of-pain” look. You see it register.{{/GM}}

Uh, okay, I’m lookin’.
Eighteen hundred and it’s done.
Gimme your credstick.

{{GM}}He looks amused.{{/GM}}

Could be.
It’ll take time for you to get top nuyen for it. Let’s call it two thousand.
Done. Gimme your credstick.
Stones? I dunno. I might be. Lemme see 'em.

{{GM}}He nods his head.{{/GM}}
 
That’s right and...?

{{GM}}You have his attention.{{/GM}}

Rare stones, huh? Let’s see these rare stones.

{{GM}}$(s.name) nods. His cyber-eye whirrs.{{/GM}}

Ready to unload those stones?
Okay. Gimme your credstick.
Hello again. Can I help you?
Well, if it'll help you, that means it wont be helping McKlusky. {{GM}}Officer Aguirre reaches down, picks up the necklace, and hands it to you.{{/GM}}
Yeah, sure thing. Good luck out there.
Your commlink chips and the screen shows the smiling face of Officer Aguirre. If he’s smiling, it must be about money.

Yeah, we have our own clubhouse and everything.

As per our discussion, I’m informing you of another Ripper murder. The victim worked at the NTSB investigation facility down on the docks. You owe me for this.
Better get here quick - before McKlusky arrives.
There’s been another Ripper murder, this one at the NTSB investigation facility down on the docks. You owe me for this.

Yeah, yeah. We’re buddies. Let’s go dancing soon.

Listen, the Ripper got another one. The victim worked at the NTSB investigation facility down on the docks. You owe me for this.
You again. How'd you find out about this murder? The press hasn't even picked it up yet.

You two dating or something?

Let me save you some time. This one is open and shut. I've already gone through the researcher's computer.
The victim worked here at the NTSB warehouse as a black box researcher.

The National Transportation Safety Board. This warehouse is used to go over the wreckage from plane crashes to try to determine what happened. The vic was in charge of their highest priority investigation at the moment.
We've gone through the victim's computer and his case notes indicate he was selling secrets. Idiot kept records.

Tell me about it. Some runner like you probably offed him to cover up his Corp master's involvement and tried to make it look like a Ripper killing.

Here's the office key. Check it out for yourself if you want.

It did. Some runner like you probably offed him to cover up his Corp master's involvement and tried to make it look like a Ripper killing.

Here's the office key. Check it out for yourself if you want.

Nope. Some runner like you probably offed him to cover up his Corp master's involvement and tried to make it look like a Ripper killing.

Here's the office key. Check it out for yourself if you want.
Check the attitude at the door, punk. Lone Star still has jurisdiction on this crime scene. We've gone through the researcher's computer, and I have suspicions that this is a copy-cat crime.

Whatever. I have better things to do. Like find the Emerald City Ripper and make something of myself. 

We've gone through the researcher's computer, and I have suspicions that this is a copy-cat crime.
Took your sweet time, didn't you?

We had time to go over the evidence while you were en route. Turns out this might not be a Ripper murder after all. 

I left the computer on in the office over there if you want to see for yourself.

Just a corpse. 

And one that's not gonna do anything for my career, I can tell you that. I left the computer on in the office over there if you want to see for yourself.

If by "work together on this" you mean, "I take your money and supply you with information" yeah. Let's just do this.

After we spoke, I did some more digging. Trying to get ahead of McKlusky on this one. Turns out this might not be a Ripper murder after all. I left the computer on in the office over there if you want to see for yourself.
What?
Good catch. That information's probably worth a fortune if you found a buyer.

{{GM}}The officer flashes his best drek-eating grin.{{/GM}}

Just trying to help, Detective McKlusky.
She's the victim's next of kin, sir. His sister. She's been praying over him for the last hour.

Yes sir. 

Oh, did you get my message? I pulled enough evidence from the NTSB office computers to rule out the Emerald City Ripper. Looks like this is a copycat killing.

{{GM}}Officer Aguirre trails after the detective, only meeting your eyes for a moment.{{/GM}}

She's the victim's sister, sir. She--

{{GM}}The plain-clothes Lone Star officer before you sports a tacky hat and a crooked grin to match.{{/GM}}

So you're the one who's working for the dead man, eh? McKlusky warned us you might be sniffing around after the Ripper. Lucky for you, I got here before McKlusky. I'm Officer Aguirre, pleased to meet'ya. Now, seeing as this crime scene is going nowhere fast... what can I do for you?

Not much, really. We know it was about three hours ago, and we know that her eyes have been surgically removed. Didn't need Dresden to figure that much out. He's been looking at the body though, so he might have more. Me, I've been scanning the rest of the scene and looking for witnesses, but no luck so far. Damn Ripper might as well be a ghost.
Let's just say McKlusky and I have... conflicting interests.
Ha! Plenty, if you ask McKlusky. But the truth is, we're as clueless as you probably are.
Hey, hold on a minute there. You haven't put in a donation for the Lonely Orphans Fund!
Yeah, see... you make a contribution to the fund, I put you on a list and let you know the next time we find any... orphans... that you might be interested in.
Excellent! Shall we say, 300 nuyen?
{{GM}}He makes a face.{{/GM}} Yeah, well, some orphans have more expensive tastes than others. 200, you can take it or leave it.
{{GM}}Aguirre shrugs.{{/GM}} Your loss, then.

{{GM}}His face splits into a wide grin.{{/GM}} Excellent! I'll start an account for you. We get any useful new leads on the Ripper, I'll give you a call.

Now... I'd better get back to work before McKlusky shows up. See you around.
{{GM}}He sighs.{{/GM}} Yes. Subtle. 300 nuyen and I'll slip you any new information we get on the Ripper case.
Look, if I'm going to stick my neck out, the carrot's gotta be juicy enough. 200.
{{GM}}He sighs.{{/GM}} Look, you pay me off, I slip you any new information we get on the Ripper case, get it? 1,000 nuyen.
{{GM}}The officer's friendly demeanor falls away.{{/GM}} Hell, I shouldn't even be letting you set foot on this crime scene, let alone answering your damn questions. Frag off, then.
