Well... I can understand that. Alright, tell you what. If you're interested, I can put you down for a donation to the Lonely Orphans Fund.

Got some cut-out chips in last week. Tried them out on the patients with violent flashbacks, hoping it would at least mellow them out some. 

It was like night and day - once the chip was installed, all of their psychotic-break triggers were blocked. Ms. Yuskin has gone four days without attacking the staff or herself.

In today's interview Ms. Yuskin told me she hated her legs. Said they were stumpy, unattractive. I haven't done a transplant in years, but I offered to give her new legs. Her face lit up like it was Christmas. 

At least her cut-out can block out the surgery and painful recovery process. I've put in an order to the Organ Grinders to send me anything leggy. Maybe I'll get some elf legs in and see how she likes them.

I was walking to the Organ Grinders downtown, and there was some kind of event happening at MegaMedia. They had a puppet there from Maria Mercurial's label with a personafix chip installed, making her an exact doppelganger. They were just using her to hawk some simsense re-release of a Mercurial Live show, but it gave me an idea...

The cut-outs, the body modifications, and my healthy supply of "patients"... I'm perfectly set up to be a bunraku fixer. If I can find a supplier for personafix chips, I can sell full service bunraku, even program the behavior trees.

Found a buyer for the first bunraku. A man in the Barrens offered me ¥20,000 for the female troll I've been modifying. He likes them big, he says. All that's left is to arrange delivery. He says he can put me in touch with some more buyers if I'll accommodate special orders. 

These morons lives are already over anyway- the least they could do is line my pockets.

{{GM}}The elf standing before you may quite possibly be the ugliest elf you've ever seen. His meticulously clean lab coat, format jacket, and old-fashioned bowtie give him the look of an undertaker from centuries past. As you approach the window, he locks eyes with you, smiling a thin, unnerving smile.{{/GM}}

Good day to you. How can I help you?
I'm afraid you're confused. Silas Forsberg was a patient here, who died tragically in a fire.

{{GM}}Holmes forces a sigh through the plaster grin on his face.{{/GM}}

We see so many delusions in this place. And still I can be surprised.
I suppose I shouldn't be. After all, this is a place for broken things. It's only fitting that a broken mind should seek us out.
Guards!
My word!  Such a violent paranoia. It's a pity...
But what should I expect?  This is a place for broken things, after all. And you are so very broken.
The only trail I see is the one that you have left in your own wake.
In fact, I believe the Ripper may be standing right in front of me.

I am Dr. Holmes, yes.

{{GM}}The man stresses the word "doctor", a flash of anger in his eyes.{{/GM}}

I'm sorry. Who's dead?

{{GM}}He acts genuinely confused, but he can't seem to drop the smile from his lips.{{/GM}}

{{GM}}The elf behind the window is immediately familiar. There is no mistaking the world's only unattractive elf, particularly in the macabre attire of gentleman undertaker. There is a momentary flash of recognition in his features as well, but it is gone in an instant, replaced by a chilling smile.{{/GM}}

Good day to you. I'm Dr. Holmes. How can I help you?
Beauty lies in the eyes of the beholder, $(l.sir).
But you have me at a disadvantage. Do I know you?

Ah, yes. How tragic.

{{GM}}He closes his eyes and shakes his head, but the smile never slips.{{/GM}}

My kill?

{{GM}}He places a hand to his heart in a thin approximation of shock.{{/GM}}
Oh, that?  I was merely a concerned citizen. Curious, I suppose, like all the rest.

And which secret would that be?  

{{GM}}His grin widens.{{/GM}}

Yes, but which one?  

{{GM}}His eyes flash with delight in his own wit.{{/GM}}

{{GM}}Another intercom crackles at your elbow. One solid blow would offer a few moments of blessed silence, but this is also a rare chance to get a word in on the good doctor, who greets you with more of his chittering laughter.{{/GM}}

You are a persistent one. A fine specimen indeed.
Patience, patience. We're not done here yet.
I have one last examination to conduct.
Pitezel!  Subdue them!
Hide?  Nothing of the sort.
I'm simply in observation. And what I have next in store should prove most illuminating.
Few friends for a face such as mine.
Though I have one associate I would be happy to introduce you to.

{{GM}}The nearby intercom speaker crackles to life. The voice of Dr. Holmes begins to blare from it.{{/GM}}

Attention all personnel. We have a dangerous patient loose inside the facility. Subject is a $(l.sex) $(l.race), but may be coordinating with other violent offenders. Use all necessary force.

{{GM}}The intercom begins speaking as soon as you approach. Holmes must be watching, from somewhere.{{/GM}}

You don’t understand, do you?  This is a place for broken things. But only by further breaking them, can they be remade. And so we must break you.

{{GM}}From another intercom, Dr. Holmes continues.{{/GM}}

Someone once told me that I was a broken thing. But he also said I could remake myself. He wanted to break me down, so I could put myself back together again. And I did, but only after I broke him.
I could remake you as well. What wonderfully twisted thoughts must churn in a mind such as yours. But I’m more inclined to use you for parts.
Holmes drops to the ground, the light in his eyes fading fast. But something keeps the shriveled husk of his soul stuck to this mortal coil for a few moments more.
This is a place of broken things... I remake them...
She... she asked me to remake her...

{{GM}}He manages one more laugh, his glazed eyes rolling toward a workbench across the way.{{/GM}}

... she was playing... both of us...
Then, with a final bloody whimper, the Emerald City Ripper breathes his last.

That man is an ass.

{{GM}}She stares daggers at McKlusky from across the warehouse.{{/GM}}
But there's no helping it now. I should just be grateful you were able to collect these items before those clods carted everything off.
Not from here, I'm afraid. Spirits such as these have domains, to which they are anchored. We need to get back in there.
I can't stand the waiting, but I don't see any other choice.
You sure you're up for this?
Whatever your reasons, I thank you.
Now, since we have the time, we should probably enlist some help. They may post security overnight.
And I'm going to go out on a limb and say you know where to find the kinds of people we need.

Good. I'll put up the money, if you find the bodies.

{{GM}}She produces a fistful of crumpled nuyen from inside a leather pouch, starts to count it out, then shoves the whole wad at you.{{/GM}}
Meet me back here at midnight. The spirits will be strong then.
Don't worry. I always follow through.
Thank you.
Agreed. I expect it will be clear by nightfall.
A little breaking and entering, perhaps. Under cover of darkness.

{{GM}}As you approach the exit, you realize that Shannon lingers quite a few steps behind. Looking back, you see that she is half-turned, looking pensively toward the horror show of Holmes's lab. Finally feeling your eyes on her, she faces you and raises her head in a proud, almost defiant manner.{{/GM}}

We have done much good here tonight.
We have removed two vile creatures from this world, and so ended a growing shadow they cast upon the city.
Our paths crossed and joined, and we did this thing together. But now here our paths must divide.
No, it won't be over for some time to come.
There is still more work to be done, for both of us. But what must be done differs, for each of us.

I came here to find justice for my brother, and that has been done. His spirit can now find rest. But there are other victims of the Ripper, both alive and dead, who still struggle to be at peace. Many of them are here in this place, filling the halls with their torment. I cannot leave them behind.
But the spirits have something else in store for you. A different path. You must finish what you've begun. You must confront the first evil that fostered the one we have just ended.
Thank you. Thank you for everything.
Because of you, my brother's killer has met swift justice. And justice of the only sort such a man as Holmes deserved. Death.

I will now set to the task of healing those he has left behind. For every madman we faced here tonight, there are a dozen innocent souls, crying out in need.

The spirits of the departed will also need help in passing, or else I fear they may become like those we met in the hangar. They all deserve my help.
Good luck to you, $(l.name). I hope you can find the same justice for your friend that I found for my brother.
I do not do it for their gratitude, but because it is a mercy I can offer.
I'm afraid not. But thank you.
This was never a job. The work we did, we did because it was right.
And I'm sure I could use your help with what I must do next.
You should not see this.
Come on. If we stay much longer, I'm liable to do something I'd regret. Or worse, something I'd enjoy.

{{GM}}With the spirits gone, the young shaman releases her hold on the magical tether connecting her to the other realm. She reels from the backlash, or perhaps from the emotional toll of knowing her brother's last moments.{{/GM}}

They saw him. They were with him when he died...

{{GM}}She takes a series of controlled breaths, only shuddering with the first few.{{/GM}}

No, but I will be.
I... I don't want that for him. Not what those poor souls have endured. My brother deserves to be free.
Yes. The elf and the troll.
We have to find that piece of the elf the spirits spoke of. It's our best hope of stopping this.
Do you truly believe that, after what we just witnessed?

{{GM}}She regards you with an expression falling somewhere between confusion, exasperation, and pity. Then she shakes her head.{{/GM}}

Oh, but what does it matter?
Thank you. And you're right. This isn't over yet.

Blood... This is what the spirits wanted us to find. The piece of my brother's killer. It's not much, but it's enough.

{{GM}}She scowls at it, looking every bit like she intends to reach through the small sample and dismember its owner from afar. And perhaps she can.{{/GM}}
All I need is a drop. And adequate time.
But I'm still feeling quite drained. I'll need to rest before I can try anything so involved.
When I'm able, I'll commune with Bear, consult the spirits, and do what must be done.
But in the meantime, you might take some portion of the sample to pursue a more... conventional avenue of attack.
Good. Let me know if you learn anything about this elf and troll pair.

And should you find them, I want to be there when they are brought low. I want to see the light leave their eyes.

For my brother.
I can. Given enough time.
Not very often. For my brother, however, they will come.
About time you showed up. Looks like we weren't the only ones who wanted to take advantage of the situation.
A bunch of mercs have locked down the area. I don't think they're here for us, though. My guess is there's something worth a bit of coin left in that warehouse.
Well, it was for Lone Star - they're all dead. Hope you're a better shot than they are.
Me too. Figured we'd just have to sneak past a few Lone Star sentries... but I saw at least four mercs out there. With bigger guns than Lone Star carries.
Probably not. They've been doing regular patrols around the perimeter, and the front door looks like it's locked.
If you're looking for runners again, I'm your guy. Discreet service only.

What? You need runners again? 

Good. I got some new contacts. Some primo mercs.
You again? Need a crew?

{{GM}}Mr. Delilah looks small and tough, with an untraceable shotty on his back and a heavy vest under his trenchcoat. He's got the air of someone who gets things done and occasionally does them himself. He might be an ex-runner, one of the rare ones smart enough to move over to management when he felt his reflexes slowing down.{{/GM}}

What do you want?
What business? I got no business with you.
Yeah, what’s it to you asshole? Can’tcha see I’m busy here?
At the mention of Coyote, he finally gives you his full attention.

Why didn’t you say so? 

Coyote is late and my client is getting anxious. Where is she?

No kidding? 

Hrm. Well, whatever. She's tough. She'll pull through.

So, who're you two?
Great. We're all introduced. Now, give me my stones.

{{GM}}In an instant, there's a jeweler’s scope on his eye. Moving quickly through what appear to be the most valuable stones, he stops when he finds a what appears to be an ordinary pebble inscribed with Hebrew characters.{{/GM}}

{{GM}}In an instant, there's a jeweler’s scope on his eye. Moving quickly through what appear to be the most valuable stones, he stops when he finds a what appears to be an ordinary pebble inscribed with Hebrew characters.{{/GM}}

That's the one. {{GM}}He pockets it.{{/GM}}

Okay, you did the job. But you're late. And Coyote knows that in this case, late equals no payment. But, I’m feeling magnanimous tonight so you guys can can keep the gem you already pocketed.

{{GM}}In an instant, there's a jeweler’s scope on his eye. Moving quickly through what appear to be the most valuable stones, he stops when he can't find what he's looking for. He glares at you, furious, and throws the rest of the gems in your face.{{/GM}}

It's not here.

I’m smart enough not to ask my clients why they want what they want. I just arrange for them to get it. 

Now hand it over.
Double what I currently owe you? Done. Now hand it over.
In your line of work, a short memory can be an asset. This was not one of those times.

Coyote may not have told you, but the penalty for being late with the delivery was no payment. 

But I’m feeling magnanimous tonight, so you guys can can keep the the gems I threw in your damn face.
It don't make no difference to me. Where’s the stones?
What is this, a romance trid? I don’t give a crap about boyfriends. Where’s the stones?

{{GM}}Mr. Delilah looks small and tough, with an untraceable shotty on his back and a heavy vest under his trenchcoat. He's got the air of someone who gets things done and occasionally does them himself. He might be an ex-runner, one of the rare ones smart enough to move over to management when he felt his reflexes slowing down.{{/GM}}

What do you want?
A smart-ass huh? I love a smart-ass.
No.
Now tell me what the hell you want so I can get on with my night.

{{GM}}He glances at you sideways like you're speaking a foreign language.{{/GM}}

She sent you. 

To talk to me. 

What, did she slag the run? Is that it?
Why don't she tell me herself? That's a show of disrespect.
Holy hell. How'd she make it back?
You do that.
What if I am?
We're done for now. Buzz.
Interested in some real firepower?

{{GM}}The ork stands at attention and looks every bit like he's waiting for a commanding officer who he can crisply salute. He only appears "at ease" when he's cleaning the barrel of a rifle or counting out the bullets in a magazine. The rest of the time he appears as he is now, ramrod straight and primed for anything.{{/GM}}

And how are you, my friend?  Fine day to fire a weapon. What say you?

After a fashion, yes, $(l.sir). Sergeant in the 31st California Militia. It's no longer a recognized unit, and there are those who would say we never were. But someone had to stand up to the Tir, and it fell to us.

Yes, $(l.sir). I was in CalFree when the elves came marching down from the north in '36. After the UCAS pulled their support, we had little in the ways of an organized defense. But what we did have was a stockpile of weapons and ammo down in Oakland.
The locals in NoCal held out long enough for us to bring the heat. And I tell you, all the magic hoo-ha in the world ain't enough to stop a V11 Hotrock from cracking your dome.

We drove them elves all the way back to Yreka. Would have pushed them all the way back to Portland, too, but some ninny down south reached out to the Japanese for aid and got our own version of Normandy for the trouble.

Things went to hell in a handbasket from there. Don't much like to talk about those later days...

So let's talk guns instead. Can I get you anything?

Back for more, eh?  You know, if you're looking to test out some gear, I've got a little firing range set up a few klicks from here. Space enough to level a building without hurting a fly. You just let me know what you need.
What can I get you?

{{GM}}On his workbench, Buster currently has a MilSpec shotgun fully disassembled and its parts neatly arranged. He lovingly massages each piece with a microfiber cloth coated in some kind of gun oil or polish. Whatever the stuff is, it leaves the pieces shining brilliantly and pungent as hell.{{/GM}}

Got some catalogs just come in, if you might be interested in an order. I can get gear shipped overnight from the manufacturer, though I won't lie to you, you'll be paying for the convenience.

Otherwise, I got plenty of the usual bang-bangs waiting for a good home. What do you say?

Well, there are two schools of thought on the matter. There are those who swear by their smartguns and such because they make the weapon more accurate, and thus more deadly. Then there are those who subscribe to the bigger boom theory, putting all their money on the biggest and baddest rounds.

But you ask me, it's all situational. That's where the real advances have come. You can pack a Panther assault cannon, but it won't do ya no favors if you're fighting in a basement hallway. By the same token, a roomsweeper might carve up your average pack of gangers up close 'n' personal, but its range is a big limitation.
Military life never taught me that. It was dealing to runners like yourself that afforded me this epiphany. You've got to be ready for anything.
On that note, might I interest you in some custom mods?  Or perhaps some specialty grenades?

I hope you're planning for the worst, and arming yourself as appropriate.

Is there something I can show you?
What can I do for you?

Theodore "Buster" Gruberman is a well-groomed ork, dressed with a precision that suggests the straight lines of a military officer's uniform. His hair is cropped short, high and tight, as they say, and the neatness this presents is only compromised by the uneven tusks protruding from his mouth. The only other defect in this picture of perfection is the man's cybernetic right arm, which is obvious enough to be noticeable but not so obvious as to ruin the line of his suit.

Things that go bang in all shapes and sizes, plus other odds and ends, on occasion. Consider me your own personal armory. All weapons are guaranteed to meet strict UCAS military spec or your money back. In addition, I can handle routine maintenance, repairs, and upgrades, if you so desire.
And if that wasn't enough, I also teach a safety and instructional course every weekend. This week we're covering bayonets. Mark my words, they're making a comeback.
So what can I get you?

Kiss Kiss, Bang Bang... love that one. You, my friend, were right to check back with me so soon. Circumstances can change just that fast, and you know that you need more things that go boom in your life. Check out what I have in stock.
What can I do you for?

{{GM}}Despite the hour, Buster is fastidiously put together in another wrinkle-free suit. Well, let's see here. I left CalFree on July 8th, 2038, at 6:13 in the morning. It was a Thursday.

From there, I wandered a bit. Plied my skills as a merc in a half dozen different wars, official or otherwise. Never did take to running the shadows like some of the boys I knew. Too subtle for my tastes, I suppose.
Surely there was no shortage of full-scale engagements to be had, so I didn't want for work. Might still be doing it today if I hadn't lost the arm.
What?  This here?  No, this is a more recent upgrade, thanks to Doc Castle over there. For a good while I was sporting something a good deal clunkier. I keep the old one in back, as a reminder.
{{GM}}Buster lets loose a raucous laugh that seems at odds with his prim exterior.{{/GM}}
It was all so stupid. Some Azzies were lobbing smokers behind our lines, more to mess with us than anything. I decided to toss one back. And didn't it just figure that the one I grab ain't a smoker.
I got it off, but only just barely. Shredded my old arm but good. Got plenty of other scars to remember the moment by as well, but the arm was the big one.
So take a word of advice from an old soldier. Make sure you're never holding a grenade in one hand unless you just pulled the pin with the other.
Now if I haven't given you reason to doubt my professional competence, how about we talk turkey?  What can I get you?

Typical. I hate all you pigs!

{{GM}}The streets have not been kind to this man... but they've also hardened him. This man is clearly a survivor, though one wrestling with the onset of age and arthritis.{{/GM}}

You... I saw you over there with that rat bastard cop. Whad'ya want?
My stuff! Mighty decent of ya... don't see that kinda thing too often out here.
I'm homeless, not helpless! Thank'ye... but no thanks. I remember the old days, the taste of REAL coffee, and let me tell ya - that drek? that's NOT real coffee.

{{GM}}He squints at you, suspicious.{{/GM}}

You a copper? Or... working for some corp?
Good. I never tell those cops 'n salarymen nuthin'. What ya want to know?

Hey... what makes you think I'm SINless? 

Har! Just messin' with ya. 'Course I'm SINless. System I-dee number, my ass. What kinda questions ya got?
Har! And I'm a unicorn. That's a good one. How can I help ya?

Nope - and I can't say I'm sorry I missed it.

I was hauling crates for Mrs. James up at th' market. Can’t carry as many as I used to... so it took awhile. Got back here in time to see a coupl'a tourists puking all over my home turf. By then, that jerkface in the uniform had already set up shop in my alley.
Sure... for the last coupl'a months I been sleeping there. But I spend my days out doin' odd jobs for the street merchants, or panhandling tourists over near the Seamstresses Union.

Hmm. Well... you know, earlier in the night I saw a big 'n ugly troll in green hospital scrubs snoopin' around the block. He bought some donuts and two cups of soykaf off'a Dan over there. Seemed nervous, and he did everythin' with his left hand because his right was all screwed up with some cyberware.

Well... it was big, and I think it must've had some hospital attachments because I saw some needles. It was a lot like one I saw back in '44, when I got captured by elves. They did all sorts of experiments on me, let me tell you. Never trusted one of them cyber-people.
My stuff! Mighty decent of ya... don't see that kinda thing too often out here.
See ya around.
Hey there, young'n.
Brackhaus speaks slowly with a deep, melodious German accent. He takes his time, accentuating each word - relishing each vowel and each consonant - tasting them as if they were a delicacy.

My lord Lofwyr has witnessed the insect spirits' physical manifestation before - roughly 9,000 years ago. As you are aware, magic ebbs und flows from the Earth, cycling from peak to peak over the course of 5,200 years. As the level of magic grows...
Har'lea'quinn iss correct. The insect spirit will then slowly consume its host  while transforming it into the spirit’s own insectoid body, thus manifesting itself fully on this plane.

My lord Lofwyr knew this day would come but he did not know precisely when nor where. Your rescue of Mr. Telestrian’s daughter has exposed the existence of an insect spirit for the first time in this cycle of the world.

You haff engaged the enemy - you know why. 

The insect spirit is only a resident in the transformed host’s body. Conventional weapons can hurt the body und expose the spirit but the spirit itself exists on two planes. It cannot be destroyed by mundane means. Hence, Project Aegis.

We are not early. We merely have experience on our side.

The insect spirit is only a resident in the transformed host’s body. Conventional weapons can hurt the body und expose the spirit but the spirit itself cannot be destroyed by mundane means. Hence, Project Aegis.

Herr Telestrian’s biotechnology and agricultural divisions worked with my lord Lofwyr’s thaumaturgical engineers and designed Project Aegis to destroy an insect spirit once it is released from its host. The formula, a fluorescing astral bacteria strain, exists in the physical and astral plane at once and can thus affect the insect spirit.

{{GM}}Brackhaus raises his hand and Harlequin's clapping instantly stops.{{/GM}}

There iss one final note. A warning, if you will. You haff seen the danger the insects represent but you have not witnessed the shaman's power. The shaman must tap into a powerful source of magic in order to summon a Queen. We do not know what abilities that power source will grant. Beware of the insects but do not underestimate the shaman.
We did not allow many opportunities during our briefing for you to ask questions, shadowrunner. You may ask dem now.

When the membrane between planes thins, the insect spirits reach into the mind of a shaman and begin their manipulation, playing on weaknesses and offering unlimited power if the rituals needed to bring the spirits here are performed. 

But once a shaman takes on an insect spirit as a totem, they begin an inevitable decline into insanity, slowly losing their humanity.
Eventually, the shaman completely succumbs, choosing the contentment and sense of clear purpose that being part of a hive provides. Perform your role. Serve your queen. That is all.

Based upon the previous cycles of magic, the first insects are not due to appear for another 700 years. My lord Lofwyr believed he was well ahead of schedule.

Something is different this time. It iss... concerning.

Perhaps it is due to the population of humans and meta-humans on Earth being so much higher than in previous ages. As a result, the volume of magic created by sentient beings is correspondingly higher. Or perhaps it is the density of the population coupled with the advances of society and technology that has altered things.

Magic has never returned to a world like this one before. The density of sentient creatures, coupled with the density of information, coupled with the a new concept - the technological persistence of memory - heightens a society’s existential angst. Thus, more people realize how truly horrible existence is - simultaneously. 

That in itself may be a form of magic. Lofwyr is studying the question now.

{{GM}}The German man’s eyes narrow.{{/GM}}

Do not misconstrue my relationship with Lord Lofwyr. I do not serve.

As the level of magic in the Sixth World grows the - for lack of a better word - the *distance* between the various planes of reality decreases. When the membrane between the planes is thin enough, ritual magic may be used to draw beings from one to another.
Yes. Good luck.

lines.cn: c21-s1 Approach team with Guard Officer
lines.t: The facility is on lockdown. The remote scanners cleared your badge, but who are these people?

lines.cn: c21-s1 Approach team with Guard Officer
lines.t:
{{GM}}He seems satisfied.{{/GM}}

I see. Well, until you deliver them to Grandmother Jessica you’re responsible for them, so don’t let them go wandering off.

lines.cn: c21-s1 Approach team with Guard Officer
lines.t: Is that so? Who have they been working undercover for?

lines.cn: c21-s1 Approach team with Guard Officer
lines.t: Then Father Willy needs to escort them into the Inner Sanctum.

lines.cn: c21-s1 Approach team with Guard Officer
lines.t:
{{GM}}He considers.{{/GM}}

Ahhh. Okay... well until you deliver them to Grandmother Jessica, you’re responsible for them, so don’t let them go wandering.

lines.cn: c21-s1 Approach team with Guard Officer
lines.t: That doesn't sound right to me. Now stay out of the restricted area or we will be forced to open fire.

lines.cn: c21-s1 Approach team with Guard Officer
lines.t: Oh, I see. Well, until you deliver them to Grandmother Jessica you’re responsible for them, so don’t let them go wandering off.

lines.cn: c21-s1 Approach team with Guard Officer
lines.t: And who are you?

lines.cn: c21-s1 Approach team with Guard Officer
lines.t:
{{GM}}You hear the clicking of a keyboard.{{/GM}}

Okay, newly minted Mother Julie, welcome to the Inner Sanctum.

lines.cn: c21-s1 Approach team with Guard Officer
c21-s1 Approach team with Guard Officer
lines.t:
{{GM}}You hear the clicking of a keyboard.{{/GM}}

Welcome to the Inner Sanctum $(scene.FatherMother) $(l.name).

lines.cn: c21-s1 Approach team with Guard Officer
lines.t:
{{GM}}You hear the clicking of a keyboard.{{/GM}}

It says here you should be Mother Julie.

lines.cn: c21-s1 Approach team with Guard Officer
lines.t:
{{GM}}You hear the clicking of a keyboard.{{/GM}}

It says here you should be Father Willy.

lines.cn: c21-s1 Approach team with Guard Officer
lines.t: You think this is my first day? I know Father Willy and you're not him.

lines.cn: c21-s1 Approach team with Guard Officer

lines.t:
Scanning...please stand by... Lockdown Active.

No Inner Sanctum security card detected! Intruder Alert!

Scanning... Please Standby...

Approved uniform not recognized. Open fire!

{{GM}}At the end of bar sits an old guy with a gray beard and deep circles under his eyes whom no one else seems to see or even notice that he exists. This is likely because he doesn’t, he’s been dead for almost 30 years. Besides you, the only person that seems to know he’s there is the bartender who keeps his frosty mug full of Hefeweizen beer.{{/GM}}

Hey I know you. You're $(l.name). You backed me when I was alive. How can I repay the favor?
Damned if I know! You live your life making stuff up, then you die, and end up on a stool in a bar in a story you made up. So meta.
Mostly, I just tell stories. But I’m occasionally good for some free gear, so come by to say hi anytime you're at the Union.

Hmmm did you hear the one about the night Shadowrun formed in my head? 

You see, I wanted to do a cyberpunk game, but R.Talsorian beat me to it (damn Mike Pondsmith to hell) and I didn’t want to do the *second* cyberpunk game. So I started looking for a way to organically mix magic and fantasy into cyberpunk because I saw them as juxtaposing themes. I thought it would be interesting to integrate them. Every culture on Earth features fantastic creatures and magic in their mythology so it all had to come from somewhere.

My dad is a big mesoamerican archeology fan so I kind of grew up with the Olmecs, Toltecs, Mayans, and Aztecs - and of course the calendar, which all of a sudden struck me as wonderful way of explaining where the magic comes from. The Mayan calendar has a 5,200 year long count, with each 5,200 years viewed as a new world - that timing almost lines up with the Judeo-Christian world-creation date of about 5,800 years ago (which is a little spooky).

The sixth world was due to start in 2012, I thought, "what if magic ebbed and flowed from the world every 5,200 and the world changed when it did. Like a moth becoming a butterfly when the environment is right?"

Alright that’s enough for one beer. Come back later for more useless trivia.
Why yes I do, here's a handy frag grenade. Be careful not to throw it near friendlies.
Hey $(l.race) welcome back, you're either up really early or really late.
I was wondering when you would ask. Actually, at one point in the early twenty-teens I knew the whole thing by heart. Sadly, those grey cells died a long time ago - maybe when I did.

Hmm... it’s early, so here's a short one. After coming up with premise for Shadowrun, I worked with Tom Dowd, Bob Charrette, and Paul Hume, who took the idea and ran with it. They focused on the game system while I focused on the fictional setting and look and feel of the universe.

To promote the game before publication, we created a 16 page booklet which featured a timeline, short pithy quote, a very short story, and lots of the wonderful art that the FASA art team had been creating. It was that little booklet that forced me to figure out how to reduce all these big complex ideas into something that could be quickly understood.

This is the problems with presents. The first couple are a surprise and appreciated, but then they become expected, and eventually demanded - I know from raising kids and dogs. So today I have nothing for you but kind words and my best wishes.
Hey, I know you. You’re... uh... $(l.Name)! Thanks again for your help back in 2012. What can I do for you today?

Damned if I know. While I was alive, I wished I’d written Harry Potter because I’d be incredibly rich and have brought joy to endless millions. Now, in death, I wish I'd written Harry Potter because I would be haunting Hogwarts instead of this dive.

Hmm... well, picking up on the last story - 

According to Scott Berfleld, what happened next was me calling him on the phone to tell him all the Mayan calendar stuff and about the Sixth World, going on and on with tons of details I’d come up with. 

What I didn’t realize was that it was about 2am when I called and past 3am when he finally begged me to let him go back to sleep.

Indeed I do! Doc Wagon’s armed medical response teams are how runners live to fight another day. They're kind of like Blue Cross/Blue Shield meets Black Hawk Down. 

You can buy their remote trauma kits and give them to your hired runners to revive them. A Doc Wagon trauma kit brings back one hired runner from Death’s Door - kind of like a one-time-use resurrection pill. 

Is that cheating? Nah, it’s a single player game - think of it more like easy mode! Anyway here's a trauma kit for you. You may need it.
Have fun.

The air grows cold, and the spirits of dead children coalesce from the vapor of your breath. Their cherubic faces are burned, and their lips quiver as if they are about to cry. But their eyes are round and vacant, and they glare at you now, unblinking.

We are the innocents who have perished in the flames... choking on smoke... as we fell from the sky, crying for our mothers. 

You bring anchors to your world... which was once home to us... and we will use them to testify.

We no longer see the world of flesh, Seeker. Only the essence and auras of living things. 

Words, though... words may echo through the veil, and sometimes... sometimes we may hear them.

Last night, this place was filled with a scream that went on and on, drawing us to it. It was a man, crying out for a witness as he died...

And so we came to bear witness... but fled in terror before the malevolent spirit... that profaned the man's remains.

This spirit was Other. It was not of this place. It had twisted its way through the veil and through the dark to come here.

When the Other had gone and we returned to our vigil, we found two creatures of flesh.

One you would call an elf, unsullied by technology and able to channel the energies of the cosmos... yet his spirit was corrupted from within. He was dark and twisted... yet not like the Other, so we did not flee.

The second we knew to be a troll. Ribbons of his essence had been flayed from him, leaving cold machinery behind. 

His aura was the aura of one simple and confused.

Between elf and troll lay the remains of the man whose sister now chants to us for justice.
The elf... his essence remains in this place. Where the man died. Something has been left behind. A small part of him, perhaps.

Through us, you shall bear witness and hear the words of the elf and troll. 

Prepare yourself.
The spirits begin to fade. All but the one. Its eyes harden and it takes full measure of you, as if to commit everything about you to memory.
No. We must not stay.
Our presence grows weary.

One of the spirits stares at the object for a long, long time. It lets out a sigh of relief as if reunited with a loved one after a long absence. It closes its eyes. Its face, once a mask of pain and despair, appears hopeful.
May I depart, Seeker? I wish the rest I have long waited for.
The spirits begin to fade. All but the one. Its eyes harden and it takes full measure of you, as if to commit everything about you to memory.
And I may require something further of you. Some day.
The spirits begin to fade. All but the one. Its eyes harden and it takes full measure of you, as if to commit everything about you to memory.
No. We must not stay.
I don't know how it happened.
I know it's "duty above all" for you security types but I am the victim here, I swear.

{{GM}}The wage-slave is on the edge. He's staring vacantly at his terminal with his hands to his face and his fingers twitching nervously.{{/GM}}

Wha-wha do you want? Can't you see I'm busy??
Buzz. Unless you can clean up a shattered career, leave me alone.

{{GM}}He shakes his head.{{/GM}}

I doubt it. You know the guy who said, "Life isn't fair"? He was talking about me.
You don't have to tell me. In fact, I'd prefer if you didn't tell me anything. Just buzz.

{{GM}}Any fear the wage-slave had vanishes as he discovers a way to assert his role in the pecking order.{{/GM}}

I'm a Level 3 Account Analyst and you are about to find out just how much more valuable my time is! I'm going to get your supervisor.
It's just so unfair! They have no right to take me down for something that security should have caught!
I mean I didn't want this to happen! I mean, hell! Who would?
Most of the time, I don't know if the cred I earn is worth it. But, its better than being one of those SINless scum -  like the ones who did this!

{{GM}}He looks up at you sharply and suddenly all his worry is gone, replaced by his natural desire to assert his role in the corporate pecking order.{{/GM}}

So I'm a screw up, is that it? Well enjoy your untarnished record for its final few minutes! I'm going to get your supervisor!

{{GM}}He looks up at you sharply and suddenly his worry is gone, replaced by his natural desire to deflect blame from himself.{{/GM}}

Are you saying I'm responsible? I don't have to stand here and listen to this! I'm going to get your supervisor!
None of this will matter after I'm fired, out on the street, and eating dog food. Just leave me alone.
Thanks so much, I won't forget this when your review comes up.
You bet just remember to keep this just between us.
{{GM}}The officer sighs.{{/GM}} And I've been trying to explain to you that this is an official Lone Star investigation and I can’t let anyone in here.
Look, you SINless garbage. I've got a job to do. Find a new blanket, or I'll find a reason to use my stun baton.

{{GM}}He looks in your direction.{{/GM}} 

Now what?
Doesn't mean jack to me. Unless someone radios me with an update, my orders are that nobody gets in.
I ain’t ever heard of that - what is it?
You think I'm stupid? I go by the book, or I don't get paid. There's nothin' in the book about that bull-drek.
You think I'm stupid? I go by the book, or I don't get paid. You're not getting in.
Sorry, not without going through the proper channels. You go back to the coroner and tell him to clear this through Lone Star before you get back in my face again.
Your client is the chalk line? That’s messed up $(l.man), but I respect it, ok, but don’t mess anything up. Not that anyone would likely notice because the forensics aren’t coming back.
Sure dig in. I certainly wouldn’t mind those guys getting taken down a peg or two. Man do they put on airs if you know what I mean.
Got it, chief.
Yeah? Where's your uniform?
Yeah... we've been spread pretty thin lately. Anyway, it's about fraggin' time someone showed up. Thanks for the relief.
You again? What?

{{GM}}Full of life with a quick smile, the man tilts his head and watches you approach.{{/GM}}

Hey $(l.guy), what’s shakin’?
I hear that, omae. I might have something for you, if you got the nuyen.
You ever hear of the Nephilim Network? Of course you have. An in-the-know $(l.guy) like you has to have heard of *the* premier merc team out there.
We’re a tight unit. We cost a bit more than the common rabble but we’re worth it.
Then you know you can hire us to run with you when you need expert help - not just any choob calling himself a runner. We cost more but we’re worth it.
It’s everything to me, omae. We could do business.
I got something that might rock your world. If you got time.
Omae! You looking to hire the best or for some common rabble?
You got it! Look up the Nephilim Network next time you're hiring - you won't be sorry.
It’s your funeral.
Wiz. What’s on your mind?

It’s your usual classic story for the ages. Hometown boy does good. Joins an elite squad then BOOM! Boom is a technical term for an explosion. Got blown up, got hurt real bad, and now I have these shiny legs. I’d do it all again. Obviously, I’d try to miss the bomb. Now, I’m here, running the best damn elite merc unit out there.
Need to know, chummer. And you don’t need to know.
Oh, you know how it is. There was a girl, and a gun, and a back against the wall. You might say, me and this place... we were made for each other. I ain’t gonna tell you more than that, omae.
Good to see you around here, chummer.

>>Local access connected
>>Network access connected

>>Project established on 5/12/2053. 
>>Project terminated 5 days ago. 
>>Project resources, status, and cost centers are restricted to CEO access

>>Primary hits(s) on Watts, Sam: Record not found
>>Secondary hits(s) on Watts, Sam: Watts, Melinda: Payment record(s) 1952681, 2911012
>>Billing system access not available from this terminal

>>Primary hits(s) on Watts, Jessica: Record not found
>>Secondary hits(s) on Watts, Jessica: Watts, Melinda: Payment record(s) 1952681, 2911012
>>Billing system access not available from this terminal
>>Logging Off

>>Project Terminated 5 days ago. Final invoices submitted to Saeder-Krupp Schwerindustriegesellschaft (Saeder-Krupp Heavy Industries Company).
>>Project resources, status, and cost centers are restricted to CEO access.
>>1,000,000 nuyen payment to Melinda Watts for contract 12401-Watts on 8/12/2032
>>10,000 nuyen monthly payment to Mitch McKlusky. Initiated on 5/20/2052 - payments ongoing.
>>Logging Off
{{GM}}This terminal is active and displays a login screen that reads "Accounting Division", "Level: Human Resources I".{{/GM}}

>>Local access granted
>>Network access granted
HR Communications
>>Employee status: Terminated
>>Brotherhood, Universal: Record not found
>>Logging Off
Attention. $(l.name), please report to the north hall to meet with the administrator.
Welcome, $(l.sir)! What business brings you to Mercy Mental Hospital?
Ah, a new customer. Please wait in the common room up ahead. I'll notify the good doctor.
Which patient would that be?
We have no patient by that name. Are you sure you've come to the right place?
We have no patient by that name. Are you sure you've come to the right place?
$+(l.sir), this is a medical facility. If you're just here to make jokes at our expense, I'll have to ask you to leave.
Oh? We take security here very seriously. We haven't had a patient escape in over 60 years.
I'm sorry, do you have a badge? Who sent you?
I see. Well, I'm afraid our policy prevents us from discussing such things without a Corporation backing the investigation. Surely you understand.
I'm sure that can be arranged. Please wait in the common room up ahead, and I'll notify him.
All I can do is arrange a meeting with the administrator. He might be able to help you. Please wait in the common room up ahead, and I'll notify him.
It seems that the good doctor Holmes has something special planned for you. I'm afraid I can't let you leave.
Good luck in the basement... Holmes and Pitezel are waiting.
Hey there, need anything from me?

{{GM}}Just one glance is all you need to tell that the good doctor is exhausted. Her crumpled scrubs are stained with a mix of blood and other fluids too colorful to be naturally occurring in the human body. Her eyes are those of a person who has built up a substantial sleep debt and has no idea when or how to begin paying it off.{{/GM}}
I'm not one to talk, but you look like drek. Afraid there isn’t much I can do for that.

{{GM}}Doc Castle snaps her fingers and calls forth the spirit normally seen perched on her shoulder. It appears from out of the ether, and the doctor immediately seems less fatigued than she was a moment before.{{/GM}}

Now, what can I do for you?
Here at the Union?  Yes, fine. I'm just getting back myself.

Days here tend to be slow, so I volunteer at a medical center on the other side of Touristville.

And I had a rough shift.
What always happens. Some run or some deal or some meet went south. One thing led to another, etcetera, etcetera.

I didn't hear all the details. All I really know is what came in on the lips of the victims healthy enough to still speak. Apparently, one of the Barrens gangs incurred the wrath of the local Seoulpa Rings, leading to a rolling shootout through the city streets. Several bystanders were injured.

But the real mess didn't begin until the party crashed into the yard of an old industrial plant out on Union Hill Road. They woke some sort of toxic spirit, which then began running amok, causing easily as much damage as the initial battle.

And we were left to pick up the pieces, of course.

But enough about my troubles, let's hear about yours. Tell me what ails you.
By any measure, yes, they are. But today was especially difficult.

Hadn't you heard?  Doctors don't like to talk about their problems. It makes us appear less godlike.

But I'll humor you.

You're testing me, aren't you?  Trying to see just how long I can go without rest. Well, keep trying.

How can I help you?
What can I do for you?
I don't want any trouble. I just cook here. Leave me out of whatever this is!

Don't come in here looking for any food. 

I fed you janitors for cleaning my oven once an' you guys did a terrible job.
Yes $(scene.BroSis)? What can I do for you?
You don't belong here.
I don't need this drek, I'm going for a smoke break.
I didn't think you $(l.guy)s were allowed to have friends outside of the Universal Brotherhood?
Not my worry.
Yeah - all for the greater good of the Universal Brotherhood.
What does she want now?
The most important thing is DON'T TOUCH ANYTHING! Especially the damn alley door or the whole facility goes into lockdown mode.
It opens to the alley outside - DO NOT open that door or the whole damn facility goes into lockdown mode.
One goes to the cafeteria and the other goes to the Home area and the heavily guarded door to the Inner Sanctum.
I've got a break until dinner so I'm going out for a beer. See ya around.

{{GM}}The scientist looks absolutely bewildered.{{/GM}}

Who are you? W-why are you doing this?
What if I say no?
Then yes. YES! I’m $(s.name).
A career change? W-what do you mean?
Wait, you’re from Aztechnology, aren’t you? I told them I wasn’t interested in working for them!

So that’s it? You’re just going to hand me over to them? Jesus! These people! Listen to me, whoever-you-are! I *know* I’m a wage-slave - I get it. But I shouldn’t be a real slave, should I? All you’re doing is transplanting me from one corp to another so I can be an “employee for life”. Shouldn’t I have something to say about it?
What if I refuse?
I hope Aztechnology has nice uniforms.
How much are they paying you?
I can't beat that... Renraku froze all my personal funds while I'm here, for "security." All I have on me is ¥5,000. Let me go, and it's yours.
Let me go. Just walk away.
Run. Somewhere. I don’t know. But I shouldn’t tell you anyway. Safer that way.
H-how do you know my name?

{{GM}}You’ve been running with New Larry for about six months now. He’s a combat mage with a bad tattoo and a bad attitude. He knew Dowd almost as well as you did.{{/GM}}

Dowd. Never saw anybody die like that before. Idiot.

{{GM}}He shakes his head.{{/GM}} 

I hate this fragging city, $(l.name)! It's wet and the rain feels like acid and I want out of here.
So ka, sorry... I’m just really ready to go home. Portland’s way more my speed.
Yeah, sure, of course. I’ll wait all night if I have to!
Sure, $(l.name), sure. Whatever you say.
Cut the drek, Sam. We both know why Dowd went down and it wasn’t the Fixer or some other paranoid chip-dream of yours.
You were sloppy.

{{GM}}New Larry checks his watch. Licks his lips. Looks over your shoulder at the darkness. He’s looking for someone and it’s not the Fixer.{{/GM}}

Okay, I can see where this is going. You chummers are damaged. I’m out.

{{GM}}He stops and a smile slowly appears on his face.{{/GM}} 

Looks that way. Sorry, $(l.name). I kinda like you. I just like money better.

{{GM}}He stops and a smile slowly appears on his face.{{/GM}} 

What? You gonna take my gun away, $(l.name)? You know I don’t need one.

{{GM}}He stops and a smile slowly appears on his face.{{/GM}} 

Honestly? It didn’t take much. Just enough to get me back to Portland and set up with a cush corp job. I’m out of this racket.

{{GM}}New Larry relaxes and throws you a drek-eating grin.{{/GM}}

Looks like my new Renraku friends are finally here. They’re gonna take that hard drive off your corpse... buddy.
{{GM}}As soon as he notices Paco, the proprietor's eyes become hard and angry.{{/GM}} What do you want? You know we can’t afford more!

Yeah... everything's swell. {{GM}}Manny continues to stare daggers in Paco's direction.{{/GM}}

The name's Manny. Now what do you want?
Do I look like a fat old man to you? I'm Manny. Maury's my dad. Now what do you want?

Some people eat it... but I wouldn't recommend that. Tough as nails. We mostly sell it to corp security teams who use it to reward their Hellhounds. The flamers go crazy for the stuff for some reason.
Whatever. Anything else?
Nope. I don't go in much for that Shaman stuff.

I’ll look it up...

Yeah, got it right here. Two days past the pickup time, didn't think anyone was going to come for it. Here - it's all yours now.
Why don't you ask him.
It means that your gang likes to stroll through here and relieve us merchants of our nuyen. My dad stood up to them... and he’s still in the hospital.
Tell that to my dad.
Whatever. Make it quick.
Yeah, what now?

The suits sure love their mission statements and all that drek.

But today isn't the day for Telestrian Corp's, "Duty Above All" crap. Several of us did the ultimate duty last night.

I guess we should just be happy Mr. Telestrian isn't in today.
{{GM}}Something trips in the guard's mind. He looks at you very intently.{{/GM}}
How did you know that they made it to the executive level?

{{GM}}He shakes his head.{{/GM}}

Yeah, they chewed through those guys pretty good. Must've been pros.
Not really. There's just as much nothing in there as the last time I checked.
If any one asks, you saw me in there, okay?

The Barrens? Hell, if you have to ask, you probably shouldn't be out here. This stretch we're on is called Touristville though, and I suppose it's the closest thing around here to respectable. Fewer muggings, and you might even survive drinkin' the water if you're lucky. Wouldn't recommend it, though. 

{{GM}}He scratches his armpit.{{/GM}}

Now behind me... that's the Seamstresses Union. You want drinks, drugs, favors or information 'round here... the Union's your spot. Hell, that place is the reason I’m in business. Folks get the munchies when they party. Mrs. Kubota owns the joint, and she's quality - a hard woman, but fair.
Enjoy.

Maybe. What's it to you? I sell a lot of donuts, to a lot of people. I know how Lone Star profiles metahumans. I tell 'em about a troll that bought donuts from me right before a murder, they'll haul him in just to see if anyone will pay the bail. Not today, my friend.
I have to say at this point it's not looking good.
Nonetheless, our records show this terminal was left unsecured in direct contravention of Telestrian corporate policy. You failed in your duty.

Perhaps when Mr. Silverstar gets his next promotion that will become a value for you data pushers as well. Right now it seems your value is "clock out early and ignore my responsibilities". Don't go anywhere, I need to make my report to Erik.
What can I do for you?
The top floors may only be accessed with the expressed authorization of Erik Silverstar, our VP of Security. You can head to the lower floors if your supervisor wants.
Mr. Silverstar wants to see you in his office immediately.
There you are! It seems that some of my best are letting me down and its good to see that you're displaying one of the most important of my three key values.
More than that! I must say you displayed the value of vigilance in spades and from an janitor no less.

Of course you do, now please start filling out the report on the data pad and I'm afraid I must ask you to not sit in my chair given your attire. I need to go head downstairs and investigate your findings, I'll be right back.

Well rewards and punishments must given where required, now please start filling out the report on the data pad and I'm afraid I must ask you to not sit in my chair given your attire. I need to go head downstairs and investigate your findings, I'll be right back.

{{GM}}[He laughs]{{/GM}}

Well yes that is the first value and I'm glad more than those outside of security follow it and from a janitor no less! No, I am speaking of my third and final value of vigilance wish seems to be sorely lacking around here.
Stop right there my friend, I don't know what your story is but I assure you, I will find out. Hold $(l.him).
Ashes to ashes, dust to dust. Lord receive the souls of Sam and Melinda Watts, mother and son, to live forever by your side. Amen.
I’m sorry for the recent loss of your brother but am glad that reinterring your mother’s body here has brought you comfort. She loved this parish so.
Miss Watts, family members, and friends. Thank you for attending the service. I suggest we don't linger long. The cemetery isn’t safe after dark.

I understand. Good night, and God bless.

{{GM}}The Priest clasps his Bible to his chest and then moves at a brisk pace toward the neighboring church.{{/GM}}

Of course. I'll be just inside if you need me.

{{GM}}The Priest gives you an appraising look before moving off toward the nearby church.{{/GM}}

Thank you, child. Feel free to call on me, as you need.

{{GM}}The Priest clutches his Bible to his chest and then turns toward the neighboring church.{{/GM}}

DNA MATCH LOCATED

100% MATCH

\\ARREST RECORDS DATABASE\

Subject: Silas Forsberg
Status: Deceased
Profession: Chop Shop Surgical Assistant 
Priors: Breaking and Entering (2 Counts)
Public Indecency (1 Count)
Brought in for questioning on accusations of unlicensed plastic surgery. No charges were filed.
AUTOPSY RECORDS

SUBJECT: Silas Forsberg
Notes: Subject was found overdosed on half a dozen different sedatives. Several anti-anxiety medications were also found in his system. Face was mutilated, possibly self-inflicted. Identity could not be confirmed immediately due to the disfigurement - had to check dental records to confirm. No next of kin.

Large puncture wounds were found in several places on the body, possibly large bore surgical needles. Body had been decomposing for several weeks before the landlord noticed the smell and called the police when no one answered the door.
Forget him. I'll tell you anything you want. I'll do anything you want. I'll be your little playmate, BUYER NAME.
{{GM}}She's assembled in a standard config: face of a schoolgirl, body of a stripper. You'd need some thick beer-goggles to miss the "work" she's had done.{{/GM}}
Oh, don't mind him. Am I more to your liking? Let me take care of you, BUYER NAME.
Of course we're okay. We're ready for a little party. You you want to have a little play party with me, BUYER NAME?
{{GM}}She's assembled in a standard config: face of a schoolgirl, body of a stripper. You'd need some thick beer-goggles to miss the "work" she's had done.{{/GM}}

Change your clothes, change your life, right? Not only will you look better - not that you look bad now or anything - but each one will help to keep you on the right side of the ground. 

Take a look.
Hey, you want a new look? Got just the thing for you - should give you an edge out there, too.
Hey $(l.guy). I got some extra outfits I'm trying to unload, you want first dibs?
Need a fresh set of clothes?
Hey $(l.guy), I got some armored clothing here if you're interested.
That’s... that’s great! Hang on, let me slot Sam’s video for you.
Uh... sure. Of course you do! Hang on, let me slot Sam’s video for you.
It's a little complicated. I’ll... just slot Sam's video for you. Hang on.
Yeah... of course. Well... technically I’m not a lawyer anymore but... uh... I’ll just slot Sam’s video for you. Hang on.
Y-you there! This block is off limits! There's uh... we've got a gas leak on the docks here. No loitering.
Hey! Are you even listening? Get outta here!
Look $(l.guy), I didn't want to have to do this.
Frag, I knew she was lying. Told me they jammed all the Lone Star channels. Dammit. I'm out.
Whoa, hey, I got nothin' to do with that! It was those guys in the warehouse, they did it! Frag, I'm gettin' out of here!

{{GM}}The guard looks bored. He toys with his gun absentmindedly, like he might shoot something on a whim just to watch it die.{{/GM}}

Hey, you're about to trespass on Corporate property. You looking to get dusted, street scum?

Sure you are. And I'm Lofwyr, the King of Dragons. 

Beat it.
Oh, uh, of course. I'll open the gate, Aguirre is just inside the warehouse. Definitely no holes in our security, here. Tip-top shape.
What, you like looking at dead bodies? Sicko. Get out of here before I call for backup.
Oh, uh, of course. I'll open the gate, the response team is just inside the warehouse. Sorry for my rudeness.
Cooked up a special treat for you in our BTL lab downstairs, babydoll.
{{GM}}When she sees you standing behind the ganger, Coyote's face breaks into an evil looking grin.{{/GM}}
Don't know why you're grinning, crazy girl. Trust me, you are NOT going to like it.

{{GM}}The ganger points to the chip brought from downstairs.{{/GM}}

Okay, junkie, hand me that BTL and go back where you came from. And tell the Tickler that we appreciate his work.
It's a trick!
Thank you for your help - they'll be back soon, though. At least you've bought us some time to scrape together the rest of the money.
I cannot thank you enough. I hate to see such bloodshed... but those men would not take no for an answer. We simply want to make our way in the world.

{{GM}}The shopkeeper holds out a few nuyen.{{/GM}}
Please... it's the least we can do.
Well, damn... I wish there were more people like you around here. I've never seen anyone stand up to those thugs without getting beaten bloody.
Thank you for your help - they'll be back, though. At least you've bought us time to prepare for next month.
{{GM}}Bzzzzz.{{/GM}} Yeah? What do you want?
Screw you. Go back to your BTLs, junkie.
You're gonna pay for that, punk.
About time. Gimme a second here to send the elevator.
{{GM}}Bzzzzz.{{/GM}} Wha?  ...Wh-what do you want?

Cherry, you have to listen to me. If you stick around here you could end up dead or worse. The Ripper is out there and he’s real. That last killing happened just down the block! 

And now Coyote’s missing. They’ll probably find her tomorrow in a dumpster without her head. Come on, Cherry Bomb, think!
I want what you want. A better life. A better world for everyone. The Universal Brotherhood can give you that...
This isn't some trick to get us back together. Things are different now. I'm different now. The Brotherhood...

This isn’t about money. It’s about binding the world together in brotherhood. Come with me. Attend a Discovery Meeting. Get to the core of who you are. 

I heard Lynne Telestrian give a talk last night called, “The New Family of the Sixth World”...
Sorry, that's as far as you go. Don't worry... all the Brotherhood's mysteries will be revealed in time. Now please step away.
Take another step and it will be your last. Head back upstairs immediately.

Hey - I have not seen you in briefing sheets. Wait... I've never seen any of you before!

{{GM}}He reaches for his weapon.{{/GM}}
{{GM}}He reaches for his weapon.{{/GM}}
Where's Stevie holed up?
Hey old man, you know how to get upstairs?
Hey old man, you know how to get upstairs?
Once we go upstairs, I'm not leaving until I find Coyote or I'm dead. You get it?

{{GM}}The elf standing before you may quite possibly be the ugliest elf you've ever seen. His meticulously clean lab coat, format jacket, and old-fashioned bowtie give him the look of an undertaker from centuries past. He notices you approach and locks eyes with you, smiling a thin, unnerving smile.{{/GM}}

Hello there, stranger. Might I inquire... do you know which Organ Grinders facility this body will be removed to?

{{GM}}The elf giggles - a strange, high-pitched warble you would not expect to emerge from his misshapen face.{{/GM}} Oh, I'm no one of consequence. Never mind that though. A good evening to you and your friend the coroner.

{{GM}}The elf giggles - a strange, high-pitched warble you would not expect to emerge from his misshapen face.{{/GM}} Oh, just a hobby. Never mind that though. A good evening to you and your friend the coroner.
{{GM}}The elf's smile doesn't flicker.{{/GM}} I'm sure there are a great many things you don't know, $(l.race). But never mind that. A good evening to you and your friend the coroner.
Hey, $(l.guy), you have any extra nuyen? Just need some SucroZoom from the shack over there...
Thanks, chummer.
If that's how you want it...
Thats rough... Well, see you around.

Dear Charles,

We have been trying to reach you for months. We have come to the Universal Brotherhood several times and every time have been told that we could not see you. Since you are not allowed to go online in any way the only way we can try to contact you is by letters, which we have done time and again with no response. 

How can you forget the people that raised you, and loved you?

Mom and Dad

From the desk of Monica Sachnoff 

Dear Brian,

I want you to know how happy the Universal Brotherhood has made me, but I am sad that I never get any responses from my letters to you. I would email or message you, but as you know from my letters we are not allowed to go online. 

You know that I have loved you since the day you were born. Mom and Dad always gave me crap for never being able to pronounce your name when you were a baby, but it’s been an endearing name for me to call you ever since. 

Please reply to this letter!

Love Sister Monica

Hi Mom and Dad,

I hope you guys are doing great and you aren't having any more problems with the neighbor's dog. Sorry it's been so long since I wrote but once you reach my level you need to reduce your exposure to the corrupting influences of our world and that means all Matrix and commlink access. I don't know when this will get to you, they say that we will no longer need our data pads anymore when we get to the next level.

I  thought I would jot it down here and ask one of the higher level brothers or sisters to pass this along. Be sure to send them a thank you note when you get this! I don't know when I can visit you next. My time here is important - not just to me but for the coming age of peace and prosperity the Brotherhood will bring for us all.

Yours,
Jenny
Hey, you lookin' for a good time?

{{GM}}She looks at you knowingly.{{/GM}}

Alright, 20 buys you a five minute distraction. Then I've gotta get back to work and earn some real nuyen.

{{GM}}She looks at you knowingly.{{/GM}}

Well, business is slow right now anyway. I guess this might at least be entertaining.
Yeah, the bums around here have been stealing old crap from that place for years. Not worth my time, though.
Who raised ya, a pack of wolves? That there ladder is rightfully mine!
Five, eh? Sure. Maybe that noodle machine is still workin'....
Uh, you can have it! I didn't mean nothin'!
Oh, you breakin' into the NTSB warehouse too? If you see any o' them airplane blankets or pillows, snag me one.
Want to buy something?

>>Legal Records Search Results, contract 12401-Watts - Contract not found. 
>>Related files, William Ettelson, Telestrian General Council notes: 

James, your proposed agreement with Melinda Watts will likely be effective in protecting your children’s inheritance, but it is not without risk. As you know the generation skipping trust established by your grandfather calls for the oldest living issue to be the trust manager and beneficiary.

While Ms. Watts has agreed to modify her children's birth certificate from unknown to Telestrian employee Nathan Miles, if the children ever discover that they share a father with you they could make a claim on the Trust based upon DNA evidence.
Please *cough* please help me!
I don't know who you are... but... thanks.
Oof. I don't think I can move... can you at least tell my dad?

I meet a lot of people. No problem. You need some brainbenders tonight? I got some of that merc stuff. Freakers, taxi drivers, you know.
Nobody's more trustworthy than ol' Bobby. Kubota wouldn't let me in the place if I wasn't legit.

Thank you, Sister Sally.

Julie,
We are all so proud of you for advancing to the Inner Sanctum. Remember us lowly Brothers and Sisters and come visit us often!
