{{GM}}Covered in glowing magical talismans and fetishes, the troll does not seem fully of this world. He mumbles to himself constantly, apparently participating in several conversations at once - but with entities you can neither see nor hear. {{/GM}}

... *mutter*... I told you. It's not like that at all... *mutter*... Bring me proof and you shall have it... *mutter*... I am honored, your majesty...

*mutter*... Alas, that was his fate...*mutter* ...But, he is not the master yet! *mutter* {{GM}}He looks you in the eye, his other conversations on hold.{{/GM}}

... May I show you my magical wares?

*mutter*... THAT was why I said to use mustard instead of catsup!... *mutter*... Forgive me, Jean. I was a fool... *mutter*{{GM}}He looks you in the eye, his other conversations on hold.{{/GM}} 

You may peruse my magical wares and see their glory.
You idiot! There's no need to anesthetize him - he’s already dead!

{{GM}}He lets out a little giggle.{{/GM}}

Yes... they all volunteer.
Just like I volunteered for this bit of butchery.

{{GM}}The woman is a classic elven beauty. Confident. Poised. Expensively dressed.{{/GM}}

Of course. I am glad this ancient ritual brings you some measure of solace. I hope you'll be able to put all of this behind you now.

{{GM}}The beautiful elf breaks in, the essence of calm.{{/GM}}

Jessica, that’s enough. We have business to attend to.

JESSICA! We need to go. Now!

{{GM}}She presses a button on her commlink.{{/GM}}
Please, Ryker, no. He's my son!
You pig! Give Zipper back now or...

{{GM}}He covers his face with his hands.{{/GM}}

Oh God, no. Please no.

//NewsNet// (1) return on subject: Silas Forsberg 
The body of Silas Forsberg, a Chop Shop surgical assistant, was found in an apartment in Snohomish earlier this week. The body had been there for as long as a month when Forsberg's landlord noticed a rancid smell and contacted authorities. Lone Star representatives have issued a statement saying Forsberg's death has been ruled a suicide. According to reports, his body a mass of puncture wounds and the cause of death was determined to be an overdose of anti-stress medications and sedatives.

The man's employer claims he was a dedicated employee, though he suffered from bouts of depression. Forsberg's next of kin could not be located, but the attorney appointed to his estate has located a will written weeks prior, leaving all of his belongings to his psychiatrist. The identity of the psychiatrist has not been released due to privacy concerns. Yet another sad end to a life, as is far too common here in the Sprawl.

You morons just shot up a Renraku research lab!

Ever HEAR of Renraku?!

I have a fragging HERD of BASILISKS, idiot.

You're going to need a lot more guys.

>>Local files search results for Project Aegis: 
>>Title: Security Threat Assessment, Lynne Telestrian. 
>>Text excerpt:
 ... at this point we cannot confidently assign a motive to Ms. Telestrian’s hiring of shadowrunners to destroy the company’s Columbia River facility but there are two leading contenders. The most likely is that she is working for a rival megacorp seeking a competitive advantage, but an alternate explanation is that it has something to do with her deep involvement with the Universal Brotherhood.

While Telestrian Industries of course would never interfere with an employee’s religious choices, our 2053 investigation raised concerns that Ms. Telestrian’s devotion to the cult-like Universal Brotherhood could represent a security risk to the company, and it is possible that this has become true.

{{GM}}Jessica's eyes roll back in her head. Whatever magic she is channeling is fast approaching a crescendo. Lynne speaks in her place.{{/GM}}

You may have taken Marie-Louise, our chosen, but another Telestrian will take her place. I give myself willingly!  I will be host to our Queen, and the mother of a new world!
No!  I will become a perfect being. Through me and through my sacrifice, a new age will begin!

Our weapons specialists have rapidly prototyped a delivery device for the fluorescing astral bacteria strain. They've created some prototype launchers which fire Aegis-filled shells. When fired, the shells will discharge a high-velocity stream of the bacteria.

In order to destroy one of the bugs, it must first be damaged using conventional weapons or magic until the spirit is released from the host body. 

Then the insect spirit must be shot with the Project Aegis prototype launcher to destroy it.
Hey asswipes, take another step forward and we are going to plant you right there! This is Halloweener property now!
Wait a minute... you're rolling with Jake? Bad idea, chummer. The Halloweeners are collecting that bounty. And killing you? Well, that'll be a nice bonus.
You really think you can come in here and shoot up *my* place? Do you know who I am?

But... no... I can't...

{{GM}}And with a bloody gurgle, Stevie J is no more.{{/GM}}
{{GM}}The man is beyond healing. As you look down at him, you notice the quality of his suit and shoes. This isn’t a runner and he’s not from the street.{{/GM}}

You... get... nothing.

{{GM}}He convulses and dies.{{/GM}}

>>Email and IM search results for Project Aegis. 
>>Title: Sample security. 
>>From E.Silverstar@telestrian.ucas 
>>Message: 
Mr. Telestrian In light of the recent attacks I have relocated the only remaining sample of Project Aegis to the safe in my office. The safe is hidden and the safe’s lock is mechanical and thus not at risk from Matrix decker attacks. Sincerely, Erik Silverstar, Director of Security, Telestrian Industries Seattle Office.
{{GM}}The creature makes a mangled sound of chittering and human agony.{{/GM}}
Whoa, uncle, uncle! I was just playin' with you. I'll call off the rest of the Halloweeners, Jake! Call it even, yeah?

>>Phone VM search results for Project Aegis
>>Call from Thomas Telestrian 
>>Recorded 5 days ago.

Dad, its all gone. 

The runners attacked last night and managed to blow the whole damn Columbia River facility to bits. I have had teams sorting through the rubble all morning and they haven’t found any viable Project Aegis material. The sample I sent you is all that’s left. This is a mess, lots of people dead and the building a total loss. Call me to discuss what you want me to do next. Tom.
Need some new armor?

{{GM}}The shotgun-wielding thug squeaks in panic.{{/GM}}

It's the crew that took out Stevie J's place. I told you it was only a matter of time!
This guy doesn't look like one of the hired mercenaries.

Sorry, Doctor. I usually put them out before you go to work on them at the hospital.

Did this one volunteer too?

Looks like we woke up the neighbors. We've got ghouls incoming! 

Hey $(l.name), maybe we can use this to our advantage.

{{GM}}The woman scratches herself like a cat at a couch leg.{{/GM}}

Please... can you spare some nuyen?

I got nothing left to give, $(l.guy). It all goes to BTLs.

Everything.

Please! Just a few nuyen.

Yes... no! Hell, what do you want me to say? 

I'll tell you anything. Everything... 

Pleeaaase?
{{GM}}She drops to her knees, pleading.{{/GM}}

But... but that doesn't make sense. The BTLs are here! I just... need... something...

{{GM}}She collapses, whimpering, and seems lost to this world.{{/GM}}

Hey, we can't all be... like you. Please, I just need one BTL to get through the day. Two, tops. 

What do you say?

Yes, yes! I just need another BTL to get through the day. Something pretty. 

Something... soft...

{{GM}}Monotone.{{/GM}}

 Great. Have fun.
{{GM}}His one good eye wavers.{{/GM}} Nope. I’m good. See you on the way out.

{{GM}}His good eye twitches as he thinks.{{/GM}}

Yeah, sure that math works for me. See you on the way out.

You eject the BTL Gino’s been riding. He gasps suddenly and his eyes flutter open. He looks confused. Worse than the other chipheads you’ve seen. His lips are white and chapped, his eyes sunk into his head. There’s vomit on his shirt and sweat on his forehead. When he speaks, his words are slurred. He’s burnt.
Wha? - what...the hell. Who slotted me out? Who fragging slotted me out?

Gino wrenches his wrist from Coyote’s grasp and he staggers back, reaches under his shirt and pulls out a gun. Coyote watches him, horrified, with tears in her eyes. Gino waves it around, blinking wildly.
Back off! Just back the hell off!

{{GM}}He presses his hands to the sides of his head, his finger still on the trigger. Squints.{{/GM}}

What the hell is wrong with you people?

{{GM}}He looks at Coyote. Looks at you.{{/GM}}

We’ll...we’ll... go slow.

{{GM}}He presses his hands to the sides of his head, his finger still on the trigger. Squints.{{/GM}}

I... no. No, I gotta slot back in. Get away from this drek.

It’s real to me! I can smell it! Touch it! I can fragging taste it! I was a god in there... king. I was a KING! Can you imagine it? 

{{GM}}He looks around the room. Sees the squalor. The bodies. Then he glimpses his own pitiful reflection in the machinery.{{/GM}}

No. 

No, no, no, no no.

No. What? Wasting? I...

{{GM}}He looks around at the squalor of the room, then glimpses his own reflection in the machinery.{{/GM}}

No. 

No, no, no, no no.

No. 

No, no, no, no no.

{{GM}}Jamal has the look of a long time chip-head. Emaciated. Hollow-eyed. But hungry and desperate.{{/GM}}

Watchoo lookin’ at?

{{GM}}Jamal has the look of a long time chip-head. Emaciated. Hollow-eyed. But hungry and desperate.{{/GM}}

Watchoo lookin’ at, half-$(l.man)?

{{GM}}Jamal has the look of a long time chip-head. Emaciated. Hollow-eyed. But hungry and desperate.{{/GM}}

Watchoo lookin’ at, big-$(l.man)?

{{GM}}Jamal has the look of a long time chip-head. Emaciated. Hollow-eyed. But hungry and desperate.{{/GM}}

Watchoo lookin’ at, spike-ear?
You hear right. I got a few. I sell ‘em at a discount. Charlie charges 50 nuyen for ‘em but I can get you three for 100¥.
And I was thinking slot off.

{{GM}}His lip curls into a feral snarl.{{/GM}}

Dis is the Barrens, omae. You want it, you take it.

{{GM}}His lip curls into a feral snarl.{{/GM}}

Dis is the Barrens, omae. You want it, you take it.

{{GM}}He looks suspicious.{{/GM}}

How d’you know I have a pass card?

{{GM}}He grunts.{{/GM}}

Well, you heard right.

{{GM}}He grunts.{{/GM}}

Well, she told you right.
I sell ‘em at a discount. Charlie charges 50 nuyen for ‘em but I can get you three for 100¥.
And I was thinkin’ slot off.

{{GM}}His lip curls into a feral snarl.{{/GM}}

Dis is the Barrens, omae. You want it, you take it.

{{GM}}A new voice, with the heavy digitization of someone with a lot of cyber, calmly interjects.{{/GM}}

We don't need more guards. I've got it covered. With a push of a button, I can make these chip-heads into my own personal killer puppets.

characters.name: Eccher
characters.bio: Troll construction contractor drinking at the Seamstresses Union bar

lines.t: Hoi chummer. What can I do for you?
Not much to tell about me. What you see it what you get. Eccher’s the name.
I am. I am. Just landed a big contract here in Seattle. Fuchi wants to build another building. It’s a good thing. Worth drinking to.
Nah. I don’t ask. Just take the nuyen and run. And by “run,” I mean “sign the contracts and make sure everything gets done on time, to spec.”

characters.name: Switchblade
characters.bio: Street samurai teammate in the 3-years-ago dockside flashback; knew Dowd

lines.t:
{{GM}}Switchblade has the air of an old pro. Looks to be in his mid-twenties - old for a Street Samurai. He knew Dowd almost as well as you did. Knows enough to be on edge about this meeting with the Fixer, too.{{/GM}}

I don’t have a good feeling about this. The run was not exactly clockwork, then the Fixer sent us this bizarre meet location, and now he hasn’t shown up.
Neg - not yet. Let’s give the Fixer another five minutes.
Your call.
Sarcasm doesn’t suit you. Another five.

characters.name: Sampson
characters.bio: Troll bouncer at the Seamstresses Union stairs; goblinized, taken in by Mrs. Kubota

lines.t: Hoi. Only paying customers are allowed upstairs. Is there something I can do for you, $(l.sir)?
It’s not the size they charge more for. It's the blood under the fingernails.
I think that Van Graas is in the bar near the stage. He's a dwarf with a cyber-eye. You shouldn't be able to miss him.

I crawled in here after I gobli... I Awoke. 

Mrs. Kubota took me in and gave me a job. I've been here ever since.
$+(l.sir), you haven't paid to go upstairs. Is there something I can do for you?

characters.name: Valerie Telestrian
characters.bio: James Telestrian's institutionalized first wife at Mercy Mental; frail old woman, broken mind

lines.t:
*** sputter *** 

... spike baby... spike baby...

          *** sputter ***

DON'T LOOK AT ME! 

Pleeeeeaasssse, nooooo.

UNCLEAN! CURSED!

Ahhhhh.

{{GM}}She sighs and suddenly her mind seems to clear for a moment. You see a window of sanity.{{GM}}
I can be good, Doctor. Just tell me what you want to know.

I'm here because I'm unclean. Unclean. Unclean.

Tainted.

I mated with a saint but I birthed a demon.

Jesus, God. Forgive me. It was MY genes, MY BLOOD!

James. Sainted James. 

We both KNEW  it was my fault and yet James, bless his heart, didn't divorce me. I TOLD him that he should find another wife, that the Bible says a man can TAKE another wife to bear him him children, if his first wife CAN'T.

He did you know - take another wife. 

MELINDA WATTS. 

They had beautiful twins. Beautiful.

And because I fear going before God to be judged for my sin. 

For bringing the FREAK into the world.

{{GM}}She snaps to attention instantly, every fiber of her withered husk focused on you.{{/GM}}

Oh, yes! The pretty pills. James used to give me those.

{{GM}}Her wrinkled mouth pops open like a baby bird waiting for a worm but her wizened eyes are feral and intent.{{GM}}

Gimme the pills! I'll answer! Gimme the yum-yum!

characters.name: Fixer
characters.bio: Junkyard fixer who brokers the meeting with the Mr. Johnson in c18

lines.t: So, $+(l.name) are you looking for work?
No, but my Boss knows you. If you are interested in work, he's interested in hiring you.
My Boss knows a lot of names...some working for him some working against. Which side do you want to be on?
I'm just a humble fixer looking to match you with a certain Mr. Johnson.
My Boss has all the details are you interested?
Just through here. In the back of the junkyard.

characters.name: Security Speaker
characters.bio: Voice on a hidden loudspeaker at the c18 junkyard property

lines.t: You are trespassing on private property. Your movements are being monitored. State your business.
Look chummer. Hit the street and crawl back into the chip you were using before you stumbled in here.
ID Card? Prove it. Use it on that scanner there.
What exactly did you find?
You are trespassing on private property. Your actions are being monitored.
The noise of a scratchy PA system blasts through the room.
{{GM}}The wall is crumbling here. Through a hole you can see a stairwell leading up.
{{GM}}With a little work, it looks like your drone could fit through here...{{/GM}}
{{GM}}The wall is crumbling here. Through a hole you can see a stairwell leading up.
{{GM}}The stove in the apartment next door has a Fire Elemental hearth spirit. The people who live there might not appreciate meeting it.{{/GM}}
{{GM}}The wall is crumbling here. Through a hole you can see a stairwell leading up.
{{GM}}With a little maneuvering, it looks like your drone could fit through here...{{/GM}}
{{GM}}The wall is crumbling here. Through a hole you can see a stairwell leading up.
{{GM}}A severed arm lies next to the zipped body bag.{{/GM}}
{{GM}}The arm looks human, and appears neatly severed at the elbow. There's a large gold ring on the index finger. Its owner is probably the occupant of the bag.{{/GM}}
You slip the heavy ring off the cold finger and feel it's weight in your hand. Inside is an engraving. It reads, "Screamers 2048".
You step away from the scene.
The patient information for Josiah C. Dawson is open on the cyberterminal.
Looks like this elevator only goes to the Penthouse.

Sam's body is half covered by a sterile surgical sheet, his face an ashen white - for the first time without a smirk on it. Below the chest there's a small, pencil thin incision covered in dried blood. Beyond that, the corpse is immaculate. It would seem that the killer knew exactly what they wanted from him, and took it.
Next to Sam are several plastic envelopes containing the evidence found on his body. You can examine evidence through the bag without spoiling it.
Moving things around, you can make out that it's a card from a place called "The Seamstresses Union". There is something handwritten on the back, but blood has made it illegible.
It's a standard cheap unsecured credstick. No way of knowing what’s on it without slotting it.
You slot the credstick into your PDA. 300¥ is added to your account.
It's Sam's shirt. Several of the buttons are missing, and blood has thoroughly soaked it. The bag sloshes a bit in your hand.
The trivid player holds a collection of personal diaries. Some of the video files are missing.
The puppet stares blankly at you.
Use the chip eraser on the bunraku?
This looks like the coat and blanket that the old man was trying to get back.

As you shuffle the bundle of cloth to the side, a printed receipt falls out from beneath the folds of the blanket. It's a bar tab receipt from the Seamstresses Union, dated two days ago at 3:02 AM - right around the coroner's reported time of death. The customer: Sam Watts.

The server's name is listed as Coyote.

As you pick up the bundle of cloth, a printed receipt falls out from beneath the folds of the blanket. It's a bar tab receipt from the Seamstresses Union, dated two days ago at 3:02 AM - right around the coroner's reported time of death. The customer: Sam Watts.

The server's name is listed as Coyote.

{{GM}}The bright yellow police tape cuts through the darkness, directing your eyes to the white chalk outline and dark red stain marking the slab of pavement where Sam Watts died. Standing at the entrance to the alley is a Lone Star officer. 

The cop looks cold, hungry, and irritated at the homeless man who's currently pestering him.{{/GM}}
The wall is crumbling here. Through a hole you can see a rickety stairwell leading up, but it seems it can only be accessed through the locked apartment next door.
Your drone fits through the hole, snakes to the right and back around to open the apartment door from the inside.
Looks like there's a hole in the wall behind this old dresser.
The wall is crumbling here. Through a hole you can see a rickety stairwell leading up.
Your drone fits through the wall, snakes to the right and back around to open the apartment door from the inside.
There's an open vent on this wall. It's too small for you to fit through... but you might be able to send a drone through to open the door from the other side.
Your drone fits through the hole, buzzes to the left and activates the locked door from the inside.
You can feel a wild spirit within this object. You will only be able to control it for a moment, but it's enough time to open the locked door from the other side.

You scan the ground near Lucy's body and spot a small wooden object, mostly hidden beneath the dead woman's hair. This must be the necklace Frank was talking about. A thin broken cord trails off to one side - it must have snapped during the struggle.

You pick up the necklace. It's an intricately carved piece of wood in the shape of a dragonfly, with small turquoise stones set within its eyes. The wood feels very study for its weight. With genuine wood such a rarity in the Sixth World, this piece might be worth quite a bit to the right buyer.

The room falls silent as they all consider the scenario, faces grim. 

Telestrian breaks the silence.
The bunk is a mess and reeks of booze. Searching through the sheets, blankets, and pillow, you find an old photograph that has seen a lot of wear.

The picture is of a blond boy and girl, both about age 14, sitting on a dock on the edge of a lake. They appear to be twins.

The boy has his arm tight around the girl's shoulders and is mugging for the camera. The girl is planting a kiss on his cheek while making rabbit ears behind his head with her fingers.

Written in a woman's hand are the words:

Sam & Jessica
Lake Sammamish State Park
Summer, 2040
There is a mid-grade security panel attached to the nearby door. It's set to require a password for entry.
Your notebook - calendar, contacts, that kind of thing.

The list is sad, dried up. 

Carter Detroit - Fixer - no response to messages
Dowd - Runner - dead
Felton Nash - Fixer - missing since Feb. 
New Larry - Runner - dead
Sam Watts - Runner  - ??? probably in a gutter somewhere
Sangoma  - Runner - dead
Half Jack - Dealer - retired or dead
The list goes on. All either dead ends or just plain dead.
It’s empty. Nothing to do.
You stare at Sam’s face on your commlink. Shake off the memory. Focus.
The panel has a message which reads, "DO NOT OPEN exterior door without express permission of a Brotherhood Mother or Father."
The image on your PDA dissolves as the call ends.
Sanctuary Door Control Panel

Holmes's workbench falls somewhere between coroner's slab and medieval torture device. It is decorated in the many colors of death, and littered with the instruments of that trade. To one side, there is a leather-bound journal stuffed with uneven pages. To the other is a pocsec, its small screen still glowing. Beneath the bench is a rolled sheaf of papers held closed with a tied length of surgical tubing.

The bench has clearly played host to numerous bodies over its lifetime. It includes limb restraints, as well as skeletal traction mechanisms. At this table, Holmes likely dismembered bodies, or quite possibly put them back together. The tackiness of the blood suggests it has been used relatively recently.

Leafing through the pages, you find few intelligible entries. Holmes may not have been a real doctor, but his handwriting certainly fits the stereotype.

Stuffed in the last few pages is a copy of a disinterment order from a local cemetery, with the grave's occupant marked as "Melinda Watts".

Holmes is still logged in, granting you access to his currently loaded files. Prominent among them is a hospital report from a donor program. It lists the organs beside the names and vital statistics of the recipients. Your eye catches Sam Watts' name beside the entry for "liver". Also on the list are the Ripper's other known victims, along with several others who may have shared in the same fate.
There is also a large sum of nuyen which can easily be transferred to your account.
Unfurling the large sheet of paper, you discover a diagram of the human female form, rendered to an impressive level of detail. It appears to be the blueprint for making Holmes's very own monster.
The broken vending machine seems like it could be moved.
{{GM}}The machine is grinding on the ground. It needs lubricant in order to move.{{/GM}}
The vending machine grinds across the cement to reveal a secret door.
With some slight creaking the vending machine slides away to reveal a secret door.
The pallet has several boxes of blank Universal Brotherhood ID cards.
Without an ID programmed on to it, the card doesn't work. You put one in your pocket as a souvenir.
The warming stove could be used to warm up a plate of food, melting something, or maybe starting a fire.
You melt it such that it obscures the lack of ID information but is still recognizable as a Universal Brotherhood keycard.
You place the kerosene can on the stove and turn up the heat.
The table is covered in candies of every shape. All covered in elaborate colorful packaging.
Looks like the maintenance staff has left their equipment out.
There is a hammer, a number of screwdrivers, and seemingly random collection of screws, bolts, and nuts.
There is a can labeled "kerosene" and a can labeled "paint solvent."
You take the kerosene can.
The plastic ID card melts in the solvent enough to be recognizable as a UB card but hide that it did not have anything printed on it.
The shipping pallet has six boxes with chloroform bottles in them. Chloroform could be used to knock someone out, if you get them alone.
The bottle slips easily into your pocket.

Next to the desk is a cardboard box filled with blank Universal Brotherhood ID cards. 

If you could get one and damage it, maybe you could use it to get into the office and program a new card.
You slip a blank keycard into your pocket.
You already have one already and there is a large stack in the box if need another one.
The holographic map desk displays the the Universal Brotherhood logo
The holodesk animates to display a high level blueprint of the facility with labels to touch to zoom into each area.
The holodesk zooms in to show the entrance hall, auditorium, and cafeteria of the Universal Brotherhood’s Seattle Chapter.
The holodesk zooms in to display the storeroom, staff locker room, and kitchen with a notation that the kitchen is actually part of the Home Area from a security point of view.

The holodesk zooms in to show the four dormitories, the office, and the kitchen area that make up the Home area. You notice that the kitchen has an external door and that there is heavy security around the door to The Sanctuary Area.

The cardboard box is labeled "UB Blank Keycards".

Universal Brotherhood Terminal
Login: Sister Dawne Wehrs
Seattle Chapter Brothers & Sisters Database

Administrator Functions:
Enter Universal Brotherhood User Name & Password
Enter password or use password recovery
Select correct answer to personal question "My favorite frisbee catcher"
Incorrect Answer
Incorrect Answer
Incorrect Answer
Incorrect Answer
Admin functions unlocked.

Father Willy has authorized the following new keycards to be printed. 
Click name to see UB ID alphanumeric.
No logs viewable at this time.
No logs viewable at this time.
The wastebasket is filled shredded paper, which appears to have been letters. There are two letters which escaped the shredder.

Universal Brotherhood 
Seattle Chapter
Keycard Printer

Please enter the following information when requested:
1) Name
2) Housing location
3) Universal Brotherhood ID alphanumeric
4) Private Password
The list below is the names that are currently approved to have new keycards printed for. If you need a new card and your name is not on this list please see Father Willy.
Select housing location...
Select UB ID alphanumeric...
Select correct password retrieval prompt...

Keycard printing for $(scene.BroSis) $(l.name) ... 

Keycard complete. WARNING: This keycard provides access to a high-security area. Approved uniforms are required in all high-security areas.

Keycard printing for $(scene.FatherMother) $(l.name) ... 

Keycard complete. WARNING: This keycard provides access to a high-security area. Approved uniforms are required in all high-security areas.
Processing ...

Keycard printing ...

Keycard complete. WARNING: This keycard provides access to a high-security area. Approved uniforms are required in all high-security areas.

Information incorrect.
Please reenter valid information to print a new keycard.
The display reads, "Enter blank keycard to activate machine."

On the lower bunk is a draft of a letter:

"Dear Mom and Dad,

I write you every day but I never hear back from you. Maybe what they're saying here is true. Maybe you never loved me at all. I think that this will be my last letter. 

Your ex-son, Charles."

Box routing slip:
Transfer for sale in thrift shops.

Reason for transfer:
Shane could not accept the Truth and will no longer be needing these things. He has been moved downstairs.
The boxes contain clothes and personal items including a photo of Cherry Bomb. Digging deeper, you confirm that they are the belongings of Brother Shane.
The boxes contain clothes and personal items including a photo of Cherry Bomb. Digging deeper, you confirm that they are the belongings of Brother Shane.
You find a fresh Universal Brotherhood Sister's uniform on the bed.
The locker has a name-tag on it, reading "Sister Julie".
The locker makes a lot of noise when you bust it open. Inside you find an Inner Sanctum keycard.
You quietly pick the lock. Opening the locker, you find an Inner Sanctum keycard.
The locker door is ajar.
The locker has a well worn uniform that would fit a Dwarf. In the uniform's pocket is a keycard.
The keycard has a big Universal Brotherhood logo and the name "Edward Sharpel, Janitor".
You put the Janitor's coveralls over you clothes.
The uniform is too small for you.
The door to the locker has been ripped off.
There are two uniforms crammed into the locker.
You put the janitor's coverall over your clothes.
The uniform is too big for you.
You put the janitor's coverall over your clothes.
The uniform does not fit you.
The locker door has tape over the latch to prevent it from locking.
The locker contains neatly clipped articles and a uniform that will fit a human or an elf.

The articles are about a variety of missing persons cases in which the missing person had become a member of the Universal Brotherhood. 

In the articles, a spokesperson for the Brotherhood says that these people are not missing, they have cut ties to the families who abused them as children.
You put the janitor's coveralls over your clothes.
The janitor's uniform doesn't fit you.
The locker contains a Brother's uniform that will fit you.
The locker contains a Brother's uniform - it does not fit you.
The locker contains a Universal Brotherhood Sister's uniform that would fit you.
You put the Universal Brotherhood uniform over your clothes.
The locker contains a Sister's uniform that would not fit you.
The locker contains a Brother's Universal Brotherhood uniform.
The locker contains a Brother's uniform. It will not fit you.
Where to?
The large tub is labeled, "Cooking Lard". The lid is extremely greasy.
The lard doesn't fit in a pocket, so you're going to have to carry it around.

He drops the gun and Paco quickly scoops it up. Coyote grabs him fiercely. He slumps into her arms and she rocks with him for a long time, her head buried in his shoulder. When she looks up, she locks eyes with you, the deepest gratitude reflecting on her face.
Coyote shouts something but it’s too late. Gino jams the gun into his datajack and pulls the trigger. There’s a pop - barely a gunshot - and he’s gone.
Coyote shouts something but it’s too late. Gino jams the gun into his datajack and pulls the trigger. There’s a pop - barely a gunshot - and he’s gone.
Coyote shouts something but it’s too late. Gino jams the gun into his datajack and pulls the trigger. There’s a pop - barely a gunshot - and he’s gone.
Where to?
The phone before you has a simple numerical keypad. You can use it to call Sam's law firm and cash in on the Dead Man's Switch.

You tap in the phone number Sam’s lawyer gave you days ago. The phone rings seven times before someone finally answers. The image of the lawyer on the other end of the vidphone is noticeably different from the one you saw a few days ago. The noise in the background sounds like it’s coming from a bar.
He fumbles for a moment and the camera jerks crazily. Then it goes to static and is replaced by Sam. Good old Sam.
He reaches forward and shuts off the camera. The vidscreen goes to static and the call ends. There’s a moment of silence and then you feel a tug on your jacket. It’s Dresden.
You hear the sounds of soft music playing within and what many soft slippers gliding across a smooth floor.
The door is locked.
Your decking skill isn't high enough to hack the door, but you learn that it needs a special keycard from a Brotherhood Mother or Father.
You must let your team in from the alley before attempting to open the secret door.
There's a gap between the fuselage and the building just large enough for someone of your stature to crawl through. It looks like you can sneak through here to bypass the security checkpoint.
As you start to leave the warehouse, your commlink chirps and the face of Coyote pops on the screen.
The three are silent as the news sinks in.
Looks like you could get the access panel off easily, but the Telestrian technician could turn around at any moment.
The access panel comes off with a quiet pop and you place a chip inside.
It would only take a few moments to install the chip, but perhaps it's not the wisest course of action while in view of a loyal wage slave.
The access panel comes off with a quiet pop and you quickly install the commlink chip.
This is clearly one of the panels, but to tamper with in one in full view of Telestrian security would be ill-advised.
The access panel comes off with a quiet pop and you place a chip inside.
McKlusky looks down at his shoes as you drive your knee into the bridge of his nose. He goes down with a thump.
He goes down with a thump.
{{GM}}A distant cry of pain echoes down from somewhere above, and the young shaman pauses to listen to the tortured sound.{{/GM}}
{{GM}}Better-Than-Life Database Management System{{/GM}}
You pick up a worn and mangled notebook. It smells of sweat and alcohol.
The notebook contains lists of illegal simsense rides and the names of clients who have purchased them.

The Better-Than-Life rides run the gamut from fantasies like “I CAN FLY” to real life recordings such as “BUM KILLER”.

The list of rides doesn’t seem to have much value.

Upon further examination, the client list is more than a simple list of names. It also contains private information about some of the more well-to-do or connected clientele. It may be that the syndicate behind this lab is directing certain clients here to secretly extract information from them while they are under the influence.
The notebook is likely worth something if you can sell it.
A locked safe that has both a card reader and data jack.

{{GM}}The entire floor of this tenement has been given over to a chaos of technology and squalor. Across the room, a chromed out decker argues loudly with an armed thug, abruptly falling silent as they turn towards you.{{/GM}}

Coyote's computer is ancient - probably fished it out of a junkyard. It doesn't even have a datajack, and the cracked display is covered with fingerprints. Tapping the keyboard causes the dust-caked fans to spin up, only to display on-screen: Password?

Without the password, the only other button on screen is a "Password Recovery" option.
The crack easily unhashes the password, "trustno1" and you are transferred to the desktop.
The computer has a basic list of applications.
Coyote’s bed has a diary with several papers sticking out of it.
There's a receipt stuck between the pages, and a diary entry.

I came back from my shift to find four of Paco’s goons sleeping on our apartment floor. It’s getting fragging ridiculous. I want to be with him, with the real Paco, but this Cutter drek keeps messing everything up. I love him. But he’s totally different with the gang. 
“It’s how I make cash, baby,” he always says.
I try to tell him he doesn’t need the cash—I can support us both with what I make at the Seamstresses’ Union—but he still goes on these runs. With these bozos all over my floor, I feel like he’s just seeing how far he can push me before I kick him out. I try to be patient, but why does it have to be all one way?

As soon as the last Cutter was out the door I lost it. I told him if he ever pulled drek like that again that he would be sleeping in the alley. Of course he begged and pleaded with me, telling me it wouldn't happen again. 
I don’t want to deal with this anymore, but I don’t want him to leave. He’s the reason I got through all that stuff last year, got my bartending license, got this apartment, and this life. I know he cares about me and loves me. More than his involvement with the Cutters. I just wish I could slice out that gang from our life together, slice out the fear that comes along with it.
A receipt for a Browning Max Power pistol from Jin Park downstairs with a note saying how big guns on hot women turn her on.
The paper has a hand-written poem on it, and a diary entry.

Sometimes it seems like Paco reads my mind, or my diary. Maybe he does the latter. I wouldn’t be surprised. HI PACO!

Ever since last week, he hasn’t mentioned the Cutters once. He leaves the apartment with a “See you in a few hours, babe,” and returns later without comment. I don’t know if it’s really going to help for us to avoid the subject in conversation completely. But I have felt better without our constant arguing about it.

The last two nights, I’ve come home from work to Paco waiting up for me, slouching on the old dumpster couch with a novel four inches from his face. I imagine that as I turn the key in the door he perks up and makes himself look especially studious for when I get the door open. He seems superficially surprised to see me, but I love this little act.
Let’s just say that "Paco" should stick to guns and motorcycles and leave the poetry to others.
There is a receipt, and an old photograph stuck between the pages.
The picture shows a young girl with caramel skin and dark brown hair. She has a snake wrapped around her arm, yet she is smiling. The back of the photograph has "Shadow" scrawled on it.
A C.O.D. receipt for a special order: 5 pounds of zebra meat from Maury’s Meat Emporium, located near Pike Place Market.
A receipt for a wall safe, installed near the bathroom door. Set to a combination of 342436.
{{GM}}Hidden in the garbage strewn alley is a high-tech safe with a MagCard reader on the front.{{/GM}}

{{GM}}With a smooth hiss, the safe opens to reveal a collection of gear that is a perfect match for your skills. 

You take the gear.{{/GM}}

{{GM}}As you approach, the man sizes you up. You can see the age-old fight-or-flight equation running behind his drug-clouded eyes. Beneath the track marks on his arms, you spot a set of tattoos that indicate he is, or at least once was, a shaman.{{/GM}}

{{GM}}There is a large pile of old shoes on the ground next to the airplane seats. They could be discarded luggage, or possibly the shoes worn by the occupants of the plane at the time of the crash.{{/GM}}
{{GM}}Sorting through the shoes, you realize that there are three shoes with no matching pair. A left blue sneaker, a right red high-heel pump, and a left black dress shoe.{{/GM}}
{{GM}}The dress shoe is a black lace-up with a rubber sole.{{/GM}}
{{GM}}The pump is a department store knock-off of an Italian brand. It is otherwise unremarkable.{{/GM}}

{{GM}}The sneaker is a child's and has musty, sweaty smell. Oddly, you get a sort of shock, as if from static electricity, when you touch it. This must be one of the items the shaman was looking for. 

You take the shoe.{{/GM}}
{{GM}}You find nothing interesting about the shoes in the pile.{{/GM}}
{{GM}}This appears to be the victim's locker. The door is slightly ajar. Inside, you see several of the dead man's personal items.{{/GM}}
The blue toothbrush is still wet from being used. It smells of mint and cigarettes.
The comb is black plastic, and has some wiry grey hairs stuck in it.

{{GM}}As you reach for the comb, the hair on the back of your hand stands on end. And when you take it off its little shelf, your heart thumps in your chest. Hard. This must be one of the items the shaman was looking for. 

You pocket the comb.{{/GM}}
Head downstairs?
The researcher's work cyberterminal has been left on, presumably by the Lone Star investigators.
The computer is locked with a two-factor encryption key.

The auto-crack in your deck's program easily pieces together a real token from the data within the returning authentication key.

The cyberterminal unlocks.

The smell of fried food, powdered sugar, and slightly-burned soykaf is almost enough to overpower the dusty sewage stench of Redmond's streets. The troll working the stand is covered in food stains older than he is.
{{GM}}The dwarf merchant is packing up her stand for the night.{{/GM}}

{{GM}}The room beyond the bars is a stinking jumble of burned flesh and dog flop. An enormous hellhound, its fur streaked with whip marks, growls low. The eyes of a second hellhound burn menacingly in the corner.{{/GM}}
The hellhounds devour the zebra meat in a few massive bites, and they let out a contented growl.
The room beyond the bars is a stinking jumble of burned flesh and dog flop. An enormous hellhound, its fur streaked with whip marks, growls low.
The hellhound devours the zebra meat in a few massive bites, then lets out a contented growl.
The room beyond the bars is a stinking jumble of burned flesh and dog flop.

Mrs. Kubota is tending bar herself when Paco walk-carries the mangled and bleeding Coyote into the Union. As soon as the boss lady lays eyes on her missing bartender, the place gets quiet - fast.

By the time she rounds the bar to meet you, Coyote is the color of wet spackle and there’s something new in her eye - fear. This woman has faced down hellhounds but the sight of Mrs. Kubota has her staring at the floor and mumbling.

The small meat stand presents an enormous diversity of dead animals - from cow and canine to the exotic and paranormal. The pictures on the back of the stand feature a much older version of the man in front of you.
Call Jessica?
The line rings for a long time and is then answered by a way too chipper Jessica Watts.
Mr. Kluwe is working the velvet rope. No one gets upstairs unless they go through him. The huge troll is dapper in his enormous, custom-tailored suit.
{{GM}}This terminal is active, logged in, and reads "Accounting Division:, "Level: Project Management II".{{/GM}}

{{GM}}Quickly, you move to give the ganger a taste of her own medicine...
But you're not quite quick enough and a struggle ensues.{{/GM}}

{{GM}}With lightning speed, you slot the BTL into the surprised ganger neck. 
Soon the ganger is convulsing in an interior reality of pain and terror.{{/GM}}

{{GM}}The ork before you wears the standard Stuffer Shack employee getup. The uniform is well-kept and well-fitted, but the tears streaming down his large, crooked face do little to improve his appearance. He does not seem to notice your approach.{{/GM}}
The piano looks like it’s been here since the Union was built, but doesn’t look like anyone has played it in earnest for almost as long.

The piano seems normal until you notice that it is very rigid. Upon further investigation it's because the piano is bolted to the wall. A close look at the floor reveals skid marks, indicating that the piano slides to the left.
You’re a natural, you should give up Shadowrunning and become a touring pianist. Johnny does not look impressed.

As you slowly peck the notes out on the keyboard, they spark a faint memory of wonder - immediately forgotten as the entire piano slides to the left revealing a hidden staircase. You descend the stairs into the Union safehouse.
{{GM}}It's hard to say whether this is a heap of items and refuse recovered from a plane crash... or simply trash that hasn't been taken out yet.{{/GM}}

{{GM}}A sticky substance of some sort seems to have seeped through the trash pile, and your hands are quickly covered with the stuff. After searching for a minute, you unearth a handful of interesting items.

{{GM}}The earring is made out of burnished steel, formed into the shape of a small salmon. An intense, primal fear washes over you as you touch it's surface. This must be one of the items the shaman was looking for. 

You take the earring.{{/GM}}
A dented tin lunchbox that's seen better days. You can barely make out the face of a grinning troll on the front.
Surprisingly, the credstick is still functional. You slot it into your PDA and 200¥ is added to your account.

In shadowrunner circles, the term "doctor" is often used quite liberally to describe any sawbones with a needle and thread. But in the case of the Union's resident medical expert, nothing could be further from the truth. The safe house boasts a fully-equipped medical suite, complete with shamanic fetishes. This is Sixth World medicine of the highest caliber.

Every inch of the tech alcove is covered in a chaotic patchwork quilt of circuit boards, chips, wires, displays, and a million other things that you can’t identify. In the eye of this techno-bits storm stands a dwarf, immaculately dressed and supremely calm.
Head to Mercy Mental hospital to confront Dr. Holmes?

{{GM}}Your stomach lurches as the spirit passes into you, a heavy presence settling somewhere deep within your soul. You simultaneously feel like driver and passenger, as time folds in on itself and you experience the moment after the murder that took place here the night before. You don't so much as see the elf and the troll as *feel* them.{{/GM}}
The broken mirror was hiding a small safe.
The safe beeps cheerfully in response and the door comes open.
The supervisor has the look of a Stuffer Shack manager with delusions that he's on a "career track" rather than what he is - a disposable, dime-a-dozen resource.
The open panel is a rat's nest of cables and half disassembled hardware. Some of it is loose.
The two men are staring into the vent and shaking their heads.
A raspy, manic voice booms over the penthouse PA system.
The guard thinks for a moment and activates his commlink. He turns his back on you and has a brief conversation. Then he turns back.
The cold storage drawer is labeled “John Doe” but the internal thermostat is set to 21 degrees celsius.

The cold storage drawer opens to reveal the fully-clothed body of a man, arms folded across his chest. In addition to sporting some of the brightest orange hair you’ve ever seen, the body seems to be in very good condition...

{{GM}}In one quick move, he jumps down from the drawer and stands before you. For someone who just woke up in a morgue locker, he seems unfazed and pretty well put together. You spot a datajack drilled into his temples and some shamanistic tattoos peeking above his collar - an interesting combination.{{/GM}}
Doctor Castle is drying her hands from a previous surgery when you approach.

Aljernon's eyes are far away, but he tilts his head when you approach, and indicates that he senses your presence. In a moment, his eyes focus and he returns. When he lowers his gaze upon you, they warm.
The painting is of a small lighthouse in intense storm at night perched on a rocky outcropping. Its light stabs out into the darkness. The plaque reads "Vigilance".
Depicted is a clock tower at a train station in downtown Portland in the Tir. The plaque reads "Efficiency".
This painting shows a wild mountain landscape with clouds ringing lofty peaks. The plaque reads "Independence".
This shows a profile of three elves in Telestrian security uniforms looking upwards at a flag flying the Telestrian logo. The plaque reads "Duty".
This is a photo-realistic painting of five massive harvesters in Telestrian colors in a staggered formation cutting a swath across a rippling field of wheat. The plaque reads "Unity".
{{GM}}Dowd. That’s a name you haven’t heard in a long time...{{/GM}}
There is an open bottle of expensive Scotch and a single glass that appears to be half empty, and a half smoked cigar.
There is a safe that has a large DNA scanner on the front most likely keyed to Mr. Silverstar.
With a click and faint hiss of escaping air the safe opens.
As you approach the Janitor he looks up, seeming surprised that anyone noticed he was here.
{{GM}}You access the computer...{{/GM}}
The database has a vast array of BTLs, covering all topics, from disturbing and sad to romantic and adventurous.
Settings for the recording studio software.
{{GM}}The buzzing and humming of nearby electronics comes to a swift halt.{{/GM}}
{{GM}}Nothing noticeable happens.{{/GM}}
{{GM}}A simple password poses no obstacle.{{/GM}}
{{GM}}You cut through the feeble security protocols with ease...{{/GM}}
Elevator Operations Menu
You quickly scribble down the sequence of numbers that appears on the screen.
The panel appears to be connected to the elevator nearby. It sports a numeric key-pad and an intercom button.
The panel beeps disapprovingly.
The panel appears to be connected to the elevator nearby. It sports a numeric key-pad and an intercom button.
The silence on the other end of the intercom speaks volumes.
The panel beeps disapprovingly.
The mourners stand graveside, listening to the Catholic Priest’s words of prayer and solace. Jessica’s face is filled with relief as the last of her family is laid to rest.
This terminal has been left unlocked with root access.
There are some log files that were recently accessed, and an encrypted research database.

Father O'Malley leaves, granting you an opportunity to speak with Jessica. But it's hardly a private conversation, as the elf, Lynne, has chosen to stay and support her friend. As it so happens, Jake and Coyote have also lingered, lending you support, too.
{{GM}}You listen as the sounds of gunfire and spell bursts fade away and the silence of the dead returns to the cemetery.{{/GM}}

{{GM}}A voice from a hidden speaker fills the room.{{/GM}}

She rocks back and forth, her milky eyes clouded by cataracts and shame. White hair tumbles down her bony back and her fragile skin is so pale, the blue veins under it run like subway lines on a map. 

You glance down at her ID bracelet:  Valerie Telestrian. 

Telestrian. That name's coming up a lot.
She stares into space, her head moving back and forth, uncomprehending.
You must let your team in from the alley before attempting to open the Inner Sanctum door.
As the sugar slides down your throat you get a warm feeling about being in a world that is comforting and familiar.
As the sugar slides down your throat you become disoriented as the world seems totally different and filled with multi-legged monsters - at least until the sugar high recedes.
Streamers and greeting cards decorate the lower bunk.
There are two greeting cards are taped to the bunk. One is a giant, home-made card. The other is professionally printed and features the logo of the Universal Brotherhood.
It's the right size for you.
The uniform doesn't fit you.
The troll has the relaxed look of contentment as he drinks. He gives you a nod as you stop by him.

You know that nothing in this world can save him anymore, right?
